segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: DECEMBER 1, 2022. COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO, YOU WILL DO THE INTRODUCTION FOR THE INVOCATION.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH, MR. CHAIRMAN. MY PLEASURE TODAY TO INVITE AND WELCOME PASTOR CHRISTOPHER HARRIS, A NATIVE OF POLATKA, FLORIDA AND PREVIOUSLY IN EDUCATION ADMINISTRATION. KNOWN AS A WISDOM TEACHER, SAVVY ADVOCATE AND ADMINISTRATOR. LEADER WITH THE SHEPHERD'S HEART THAT LEADS ORGANIZATIONS TO HEALTH AND SUSTAINABILITY. ENERGETIC CREATIVE SPEAKER, AUTHOR, PODCASTER AND CONTENT CREATOR. LEADING PEOPLE, INSPIRING FAMILIES AND LEADING ORGANIZATIONS WITH MULTIMILLION DOLLAR BUDGETS. AND MANAGES COMMUNITY, LASTING CHANGE AND TRANSFORMATION. HE CURRENTLY SERVES AS CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER OF CROSSOVER CHURCH OF TAMPA, FLORIDA. HE RESIDES IN TAMPA, FLORIDA, WITH HIS WIFE OF OVER 20 YEARS AND FOUR CHILDREN. DIRECTOR OF ADMINISTRATION AND VISION FOR FULL GOSPEL BAPTIST CHURCH FELLOWSHIP INTERNATIONAL AND AS FOUNDER. THE PODCAST TO 29 NATIONS IN THE WORLD AND AUTHOR AND CO-AUTHOR OF THREE BOOKS. ALSO INVOLVING HIM BEING THE FOUNDER AND CHIEF CONTENT CREATOR FOR INSPIRED GROUP LCC, THE TIMOTHY PROJECT AND THE HEALTHY URBAN CHURCH INITIATIVE. AN ALUM OF FLORIDA STATE UNIVERSITY WITH A MASTER'S DEGREE AT THE UNIVERSITY OF SOUTH FLORIDA. SEMINARY TRAINING AT PRINCETON THEOLOGICAL. AND SERVING AT DUKE DIVINITY SCHOOL AND DOCTORATE AT NOVA SOUTHEASTERN UNIVERSITIES. HIS COMMUNITY WORK WITH 400 HONORS, AWARDS AND COMMENDATIONS INCLUDING BEING RECOGNIZED BY THE CITY OF TAMPA AND A BOARD MEMBER OF CLOTHES TO KIDS, URBAN LEAGUE OF HILLSBOROUGH AND MEMBER OF LEADERSHIP TAMPA. IT IS MY PLEASURE TO WELCOME THIS GENTLEMAN HERE WHOSE LIFE MISSION IS TO INSPIRE PEOPLE TO LIVE WISE LIVES. WE PLEASE STAND AND PASTOR, TAKE IT AWAY. THIS IS MAYBE MY TENTH TIME, AND I APPRECIATE THE HONOR AND OPPORTUNITY. LET'S PRAY. GOD OF PEACE, GIVER OF HOPE, SUSTAINER OF LIFE, SOURCE OF HEALTH AND HEALING, WELL SPRING OF WISDOM AND AUTHOR AND FINISHER OF OUR FAITH WE PAUSE IN THIS MOMENT TO ACKNOWLEDGE OUR NEED FOR YOU. WE DESPERATELY CLAIM OUR DEPENDENCE ON YOUR HEALTH TO LIVE WELL, LOVE WELL AND LEGISLATURE AS WELL. RELIANCE OF RIGHTEOUSNESS OF RIGHT AND WRONG AND NOT OUR EGOS, POLITICAL PREFERENCES OR DISTORTED PERCEPTIONS. WE THANK YOU, DEAR GOD FOR THE PEACE AND AUTHORITY WE ENJOY TODAY IN THE PEACE NOT ONLY IN OUR CITY BUT OUR NATION. WE OFTEN TAKE FOR GRANTED. HOLY SPIRIT FILL OUR CITY LEADERS WITH UNDERSTANDING, DISCERNMENT AND KNOWLEDGE. GRANT THEM FAVOR, INCREASE THEIR FAITH, PREPARE THEM TO PURSUE YOUR WILL FOR THE CITY. CAUSE THEM TO KNOW AND FULFILL PURPOSES FOR THEIR LIVES INDIVIDUALLY AND FOR OUR LIVES COLLECTIVELY. FATHER, I ASK THAT YOU WILL PLACE PEOPLE IN THEIR PATHS THAT WILL PROVIDE SOUND, GODLY ADVICE AND COUNSEL. FOR WITHOUT YOUR COUNSEL, OUR PLANS WILL ALL GO AWRY. DEAL WITH OUR LEADERS OF KEEPING THEIR HEARTS AND MOTIVES PURE. WE ASK THAT YOU WILL MAKE THEIR HEARTS PLIABLE AND WILLING AND OBEDIENT TO YOUR WILL AND TO YOUR WAY AND CAUSE THEM TO GLORIFY YOUR NAME. GREAT GOD, PLEASE FORGIVE ALL OF US FOR OUR SINS OR MISGIVINGS. FORGIVE THOSE WHO DO NOT CONSULT YOU DAILY. FORGIVE OUR LEADERS THAT OPERATE FROM GREED, HYPOCRISY, PRIDE, ARROGANCE, SLANDER, ELITISM, IMPARTIALITY, MANIPULATION, UNGODLINESS OR ANY ACTS OF SINFULNESS. WE ASK THAT YOU RECEIVE OUR FORGIVENESS AND NOT PRAYING FOR OUR LEADERS CONSISTENTLY AND OF THE THINGS THAT WE DO FOR SELFISH MOVEMENTS AND NETWORK. I ASK THAT WE ALWAYS PRIORITIZE PEOPLE OVER OUR POLITICS AND OVER OUR REFERENCES. GOD, YOU ARE THE GIVER OF LIFE AND HEALTH AND WE PRAY COMFORT OF THOSE CHALLENGED WITH SERIOUS OR CHRONIC ILLNESSES, MENTAL OR EMOTIONAL, AND I GIVE THE POWER OF YOUR HEALING TO THOSE WHO MINISTER IN THEIR NEEDS AND STRENGTHEN IN THEIR WEAKNESS AND HAVE CONFIDENCE IN THEIR LOVING CARE. YOUR WISDOM PRODUCE IN THEM CREATIVE IDEAS, SOLUTIONS TO PROBLEMS, NEW TECHNOLOGY AND NEW PRODUCTS. GIVE US SOUND JUDGMENT TO MAKE GOOD DECISIONS AND GIVE US GREATER KNOWLEDGE, UNDERSTANDING AND POWER TO LIVE SKILLFULLY AND GIVE BACK IN THIS COMMUNITY. BY NAME I CALL OUT EACH ONE OF OUR IF IT JOSEPH CITRO, ORLANDO GUDES, CHARLIE MIRANDA, LYNN HURTAK, BILL CARLSON, GUIDO MANISCALCO AND LUIS VIERA. I PRAY THAT YOU BLESS THEM AND KEEP THEM AND SHINE YOUR FACE ON THEM AND GRACIOUS TO THEM. TURN YOUR FACE TOWARD THEM AND GIVE THEM PEACE. IN JESUS'S NAME, AMEN. [PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE]

segments.text: YOU HAVE OF A PHYSICAL QUORUM.

segments.text: YES, THANK YOU, MR. CHAIRMAN, MEMBERS OF CITY COUNCIL AND MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC. MARTIN SHELBY, CITY ATTORNEY. I WILL BE BRIEF. BROADCAST ON SPECTRUM 640, FRONTIER CHANNEL 15 AND ON THE INTERNET LIVESTREAMED TAMPA.GOV/LIVESTREAM. YOU CAN APPEAR IN CHAMBERS OR VIRTUALLY AND REQUIRES PREREGISTRATION AND THE PORTAL OF THAT AND ALL THE INFORMATION IS AVAILABLE IN THE CITY COUNCIL NOTICE OF THIS HEARING AND ON THE CITY COUNCIL'S WEB PAGE, TAMPA.GOV/CITYCOUNCIL. I ASK THAT THE RULES TO BE WAIVED TO ALLOW THE CONTINUED USE OF CMT.

segments.text: SO MOVED.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: SECOND.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: ALL IN FAVOR. THANK YOU. LET'S DO OUR AGENDA REVIEW PLEASE, AND I BELIEVE OUR CHIEF OF STAFF IS NOT GOING TO BE GIVING ANY TYPE OF UPDATE TODAY. IN THAT PLACE, MISS TRAVIS. CONSENT AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 41 IS BEING ASKED TO BE PULLED AND PLACED YOU CAN UNDER STAFF REPORTS.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THAT WILL BE THE FIRST ITEM THAT WE HEAR UNDER STAFF REPORTS.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THEN YOU HAVE A WALK-ON.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: THAT'S CORRECT, SIR.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THEN AGENDA NUMBER 2, WE ARE GOING TO KEEP ON. NUMBER 3.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: YES, SIR -- MR. CHAIRMAN, CAN I BE RECOGNIZED.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: YES, SIR.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: I WOULD LIKE MR. BAIRD TO SPEAK OF THIS. I WAS SURPRISED AND WOULD LIKE THE PUBLIC TO KNOW IT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 4.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: YES, I ACTUALLY SPOKE WITH CHIEF O'CONNOR THIS MORNING AND DUE TO SOME ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, SHE IS ASKING FOR A CONTINUANCE SO WE HAVE MORE TIME TO FIGURE OUT WHAT'S GOING ON. SO SHE DID NOT SEND ME A DATE YET, SO I AM GOING TO TENTATIVELY SAY JANUARY 5.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: OKAY, JANUARY 5.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: IF IT NEEDS TO BE UPDATED I WILL DO SO AT ANOTHER TIME.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: SECOND.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: ALL IN FAVOR. MR.CARLSON, YOU ARE RECOGNIZED.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: I ENCOURAGE EVERYBODY TO READ JUSTIN GARCIA'S ARGUMENT IN "CREATIVE LOAFING" AND THE FOLLOW-UP. WE HAVE SEEN A LOT OF INFORMATION COMING OUT THAT MAY BE TECHNICALLY ACCURATE AND MAY NOT BE ACCURATE AND MAY BE MISLEADING. I WOULD ENCOURAGE IN THE REPORT COMING BACK THAT WE BE TRANSPARENT AND ACCURATE IN THE INFORMATION THAT IS GIVEN, THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU, AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 5. KEEP THAT. THANK YOU VERY MUCH. AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 6, I HAVE SPOKEN WITH VIK BHIDE, HEAD OF THE MOBILITY DEPARTMENT, AND I HAVE HAD MY QUESTIONS ANSWERED. DOES ANY OTHER MEMBER -- BOARD MEMBER -- EXCUSE ME, COUNCILMEMBER WANT TO --

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: I WILL JUST MAKE A COMMENT, MR. CHAIRMAN, THESE ARE THINGS THAT HAPPEN WITH THE PAST COUNCIL AND FUTURE COUNCILS. AND HAVE A SITUATION WHEN YOU PASS A ZONING AND YOU HAVE TO HAVE THOSE, I GUESS. WHAT HAPPENS IS IN GOOD FAITH, INDIVIDUAL WHO HAS THAT DOESN'T DO ANYTHING. HE COMPLIES, BUT SELLS IT TO SOMEONE ELSE, BUT NO TRACING MECHANISM AS TO HOW THAT GOES BACK TO THAT INDIVIDUAL WHO BOUGHT IT. UNDERSTAND THAT IS WHAT WE HAVE TO FIX.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: YES, SIR. AND I PROBABLY WILL MAKE A MOTION LATER ON TODAY THAT WE HAVE THIS ON A WORKSHOP WHERE WE CAN ACTUALLY ASK MOBILITY AND LEGAL TO COME IN AND LET'S SEE WHAT CAN BE DONE IF THE CITY CAN TAKE A ROLE IN. COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: THERE WAS NO ACTUAL WRITTEN INFORMATION OF THIS AND I WOULD SAY, YES, I DEFINITELY WANT THEM. BUT IF YOU ARE GOING TO PROPOSE THIS FOR A WORKSHOP ITEM, I THINK THAT IS A MUCH BETTER TIME SO WE DON'T SPEND TIME TODAY ON IT. SO, YES --

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: WE WILL MOVE AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 6.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: WE CAN SET IT FOR A WORKSHOP NOW.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: WHEN IS THE WORKSHOP AFTER THE FIRST OF THE YEAR. LET'S GIVE 60 DAYS FOR THIS.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: THAT WILL BE FEBRUARY.

segments.speaker: CLERK
segments.text: FEBRUARY --

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: FEBRUARY 23. HOW FULL ARE WE WITH WORKSHOPS THAT DAY?

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: FIVE, SIX, SEVEN ITEMS.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MARCH 23.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: MOTION FOR MARCH 23.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: A MOTION BY COUNCILMAN MIRANDA. SECONDED BY COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO. ALL IN FAVOR. THANK YOU. AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 7.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: I WOULD LIKE TO -- I SENT A MEMO ON THIS. I WOULD LIKE TO CONTINUE THIS UNTIL JANUARY 19. TWO REASONS. ONE, IT DOESN'T MENTION THE WORD "PURE" AND PEOPLE WERE SURPRISED ABOUT IT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MOTION MADE BY COUNCILMAN CARLSON. SECONDED BY COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO. ANY FURTHER DISCUSSION?

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: YES. I KNOW WE HAVE ACTUAL COMMUNITY MEMBERS HERE TO TALK ABOUT THIS TODAY. SO I -- THE OTHER ISSUE WITH THIS IS I READ THE MEMO, AND IT -- THIS DOES NOT SAY "PURE" BUT WE HAD AGREED TO STOP CALLING THIS "PURE." SO I DON'T DISAGREE WE NEED TO HAVE SOME TYPE OF WORDING SO THE PUBLIC KNOWS. DO YOU WANT TO KEEP THE "PURE" LABEL OR PREFER TO DO A DIFFERENT LABEL.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: SORRY, I DON'T -- MR. CHAIR.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN CARLSON.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: I DON'T REMEMBER CHANGING THE LABEL ON IT. BUT THIS ONE IS -- THIS IS THE CITY'S EXCUSE FOR DOING "PURE." SO I THINK THE PUBLIC KNOWS THE CITY IN YEARS PAST LOBBIED TO GET THIS PASSED. SO I THINK WE -- JUST FOR TRANSPARENCY REASON, I THINK WE SHOULD CALL IT "PURE" BECAUSE IT IS RELATED TO "PURE" AND I WOULD LIKE TO HEAR FROM THE PUBLIC TODAY AND WE HAVE PEOPLE WHO LAST NIGHT CONTACTED ME WHO SAID WE WOULD LIKE TO TALK ABOUT IT, BUT NOW WE HAVE ALREADY SCHEDULED OTHER THINGS. IF WE KNEW IT WAS "PURE" WE WOULD FLAG IT AND TALK ABOUT IT. WE CAN HEAR IT NOW AND ON JANUARY 19.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I AM FINE WITH THAT. IF YOU ARE ASKING FOR ANYTHING FROM THE "PURE" TAG I AM FINE WITH THAT.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: NOT ALL WATER, BUT THINGS RELATED TO CURE. MR. CHAIR.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: PLEASE, MR. CARSON AND THEN I WILL HAVE A COMMENT TO MAKE AFTER YOU ARE FINISHED.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: WE WILL ENTER INTO DEEPER DISCUSSIONS OF PURE SOME TIME SOON, AND I ENCOURAGE MY COLLEAGUES TO LOOK AT ALL THE LEGAL AND LOBBYING AVENUES WE CAN TO STOP IT. I TALKED TO PEOPLE AROUND THE STATE AND NO REASON WHY TAMPA SHOULD BE DOING IT, BUT IT SEEMS TO BE MOVING FORWARD IN A VERY SUSPICIOUS WAY. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MISS DUNCAN.

segments.speaker: JEAN DUNCAN
segments.text: YES, WE ARE PERFECTLY INTERESTED IN ACCOMMODATING THE COUNCIL'S INTEREST AND REQUEST. I WOULD JUST SAY ON THE LABELING OF "PURE." IT WAS MADE VERY CLEAR BY THE COMMUNITY AND SOME OF THE COUNCIL ANYWAY, THAT "PURE" AS IT WAS PRESENTED PREVIOUSLY IN TERMS OF A CONTRACT WITH CERTAIN FRAMEWORK AND PARAMETERS AND TIME FRAME WAS NO LONGER GOING TO BE ACTIVE IN THAT STATE. WHICH IS ACTIVE IN THAT STATE IS THE REMAINDER OF THE CONTRACT DOLLARS TO ADDRESS THE MOTION MADE BY THIS BODY. NO FORMER PURE PROJECT. NO FORMER PURE PROJECT IN THAT SAME FRAMEWORK AND IN THAT SAME CONDITION; HOWEVER, WE ARE STILL AS RESPONSIBLE EXPERTS IN THE FIELD GOING TO PURSUE AND RESEARCH AND STUDY AND COORDINATE WITH OTHER AGENCIES AND ALL THOSE OTHER DUE DILIGENCE ACTIONS OF MAKING SURE WE HAVE A SAFE, RELIABLE WATER SUPPLY, ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS FOR OUR WATER RESOURCE AND ALL THOSE RELATED ASPECTS. SO WITH THAT, WE WOULD SUGGEST THAT PURE ALREADY HAS A CERTAIN CONNOTATION AND A CERTAIN SENSE OF UNDERSTANDING WITH THE VERY LITTLE PUBLIC ENGAGEMENT WE WERE ABLE TO DO AND WE WOULD RECOMMEND SOMETHING A LITTLE MORE GENERIC AND IF THAT IS NOW KNOWN AMONG THE STAKEHOLDERS AND THE INTERESTED PARTIES THAT LABEL IS REPRESENTING THESE WATER TYPE OF CONVERSATIONS, THAT MIGHT BE A BETTER WAY TO GO. BUT WE ARE FOLLOWING DO WHATEVER THE COUNCIL WOULD REQUEST.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN GUDES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: THANK YOU, MR. CHAIRMAN. MISS DUNCAN, WHEN ARE WE GOING TO BRING THIS TO A CLOSE. THIS IS THE PROJECT. THIS IS WHAT WE BELIEVE. WE HEARD FROM THE PUBLIC. THESE ARE THE CHOICES. WHAT DOES COUNCIL WANT TO DO. WHEN IS THAT -- SO WE CAN GET THE "PURE" NAME AND MOVE ON WITH THIS WATER SITUATION. IT HAS BEEN LINGERING FOREVER AND WHEN WILL WE HAVE A DEFINITIVE PROJECT TO SAY THIS IS THE RECOMMENDATION WE BELIEVE FROM OUR EXPERTS, VERSUS WHAT THE PUBLIC SAYS AND HERE IT IS. WHAT DO YOU WANT TO DO. WHAT IS COUNCIL'S PLEASURE.

segments.speaker: JEAN DUNCAN
segments.text: THE ACTIVITY THAT IS OCCURRING NOW IS WORKING ON THE VARIOUS MOTIONS THAT CAME OUT IN SEPTEMBER. WITH THE MAY JOR ONE BEING IN FEBRUARY A WORKSHOP ON THE WORK THAT OUR CONSULTANT IS DOING IN CONCERT WITH THE CITY AND OTHER AGENCIES TO RESPOND TO THAT MOTION. WE DO NOT HAVE A CONTRACT OPPORTUNITY TO TAKE THINGS ANY FURTHER BECAUSE COUNCIL HAS NOT APPROVED ANYTHING FURTHER FOR US. SO THAT IS THE LANDING POINT FOR NOW IN TERMS OF A PUBLIC DISCUSSION. THE WORKSHOP IN SEPTEMBER.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: JUST FOR THE EASING OF MIND, IF SOMETHING IS FOR "PURE." GOT TO HAVE THE NAME OF PURE ON IT AND THIS COUNCIL HAS TO VOTE ON IT FOR ANY FUNDING, AM I CORRECT?

segments.speaker: JEAN DUNCAN
segments.text: YES, BUT NO MORE PURE PROJECT.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: BUT I AM GETTING TO THE POINT. THE POINT IS IF SOMEBODY WAS TO DO THAT, IT WOULD BE DRASTICALLY IMPOSSIBLE TO UNDERSTAND THAT THE PUBLIC UNDERSTANDS WHAT THAT INDIVIDUAL, HE OR SHE DID IN A HIGHER OFFICE TO DO SOMETHING LIKE THAT. IT WOULD BE INAPPROPRIATELY AT ANY TIME, NUMBER ONE. 23478 TWO -- NOT RELATED -- IF IT IS WATER EVERYTHING IS RELATED BUT PURE IS NOT INVOLVED IN THIS. LIKE EVERYBODY IN MY FAMILY HAVE THE LAST NAME OF MIRANDA. THEY DON'T. I HAVE A 50 SOME COUSINS AND ALL HAVE DIFFERENT LAST NAMES. IT DOESN'T MEAN THEY ARE ME. IT MEANS THEY ARE RELATED TO ME. THE SAME THING HERE. I TRY TO MAKE THINGS TO MAKE SURE I KNOW WHAT IS GOING ON. IF IT DOESN'T HAVE THE FUNDING FOR PURE, IT CAN NOT BE PURE, BECAUSE YOU CAN'T TRANSFER MONEY FROM SOMETHING THAT IS APPROVED TO SOMETHING THAT IS NOT APPROVED BY THIS COUNCIL. TO BE AFRAID OF SOMETHING, WE WILL BE STALEMATED. OTHER WATER AGENCIES THINK 15, 20, 30, 40 YEARS AWAY TO PLAN FOR GROWTH. THEY HAVE ALL THOSE PLANNING. YOU JUST GOT TO GO LISTEN TO THEM. THEY COME UP ON CHANNEL 3. I KNOW EVERYBODY DOESN'T HAVE CHANNEL 3 BECAUSE THAT IS A NONAREA THAT YOU DON'T NEED THE INTERNET OR TPG OR WHATEVER IT IS THEY GET ON. THESE THINGS ARE EXPLAINED IN THOSE MEETINGS FORCEFULLY AND THOUGHTFULLY AND THE PUBLIC SHOULD UNDERSTAND EXACTLY WHAT IT IS. I AM NOT SAYING VOTE IT UP OR DOWN. IF IT IS UP TO EACH COUNCILMEMBERS. IF IT IS PURE, IT SHOULD HAVE THE LABEL OF PURE. NOT JUST BECAUSE IT IS WATER AND A MEMORY FAMILY OF WATER.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: MR. CHAIRMAN.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN CARLSON.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: I WOULD CALL IT TOILET TO TAP. BUT MAYBE SOMEBODY ELSE WILL HAVE A DIFFERENT LABEL THAT THEY WILL SUGGEST. THE -- THE REASON WHY THIS IS TIED TO PURE, OVER THE LAST THREE AND A HALF YEARS WE DISPROVEN EVERY ARGUMENT THAT THE WATER DEPARTMENT MADE OF THIS PROJECT. THE ONLY ONE LEFT THEY ARE FORCED TO DO IT BY THE STATE. I CONTACTED THE STATE, THEY SAID THAT IS RUBBISH, THE STATE RESPONDED NOW NEGATIVELY. WHAT I BELIEVE TO BE A VERY SUSPICIOUS WAY. WE CAN TALK WITH THAT IN JANUARY. SO WHAT WE NEED TO DO IS FIGURE OUT HOW TO STOP THIS PROJECT FROM -- THE TAMPA -- AS YOU THOUGH TAMPA BAY WATER HAS A 20-YEAR PLAN AND TEN-YEAR SUPPLY AND WE ARE PART OF THAT. WE DON'T NEED 50 MGD FOR 100 YEARS AND DOESN'T MAKE THE SENSE TO SPEND THE MONEY TODAY THAT WE WON'T NEED FOR 100 YEARS. WE CAN TALK ABOUT THAT IN JANUARY, AND TIED TO PURE -- OR WHATEVER WE CALL THE PROJECT -- ONLY BECAUSE THE JUSTIFICATION THAT WE HAVE TO MOVE FORWARD BY STATE LAW. WE HAVE A LEGAL TEAM. WE HAVE A LOBBYING TEAM. THE LOBBYISTS SAY THE CITY HAS BEEN WORKING IN FAVOR OF THIS IN THE PAST, WE AS CITY COUNCIL SHOULD HAVE SOME INFLUENCE TO STOP THEM FROM DOING THAT OR ASK THEM TO GIVE US AN EXCEPTION. CRAZY TO PUT IT ON THE BACKS OF OUR RATE PAYERS, THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MISS DUNCAN.

segments.speaker: JEAN DUNCAN
segments.text: I WOULD LIKE TO SAY WE HAVE WORKED VERY HARD TO BE AS TRANSPARENT AS WE CAN CONSIDERING WE TONIGHT HAVE FUNDING FOR A PUBLIC ENGAGEMENT PLAN TO HAVE REALLY TRUE, PROPER, COMPREHENSIVE ENGAGEMENT. PUBLIC ENGAGEMENT IS VERY LIMITED TO A FEW PEOPLE THAT TAKE THEIR PRECIOUS FREE TIME AND GET INVOLVED WITH THESE TOPICS. WE APPRECIATE THAT. THAT IS NOT REPRESENTING THE ENTIRE CITY'S VIEWPOINT OF THIS I THINK SO. WE HAVEN'T HAD A CHANCE TO HAVE THAT PUBLIC ENGAGEMENT. I WOULD LIKE TO SAY -- AND I TAKE EXCEPTION THE WORD "SUSPICIOUS." WE HAVE WORKED VERY HARD TO BE TRANSPARENT TO INCLUDE ALL PARTIES OF INTEREST, TO INCLUDE THE APPROPRIATE AGENCIES. NO INFORMATION OR EVIDENCE THAT WE HAVE DONE SIGNIFICANCE THAT SHOULD BE CONSIDERED SUSPICIOUS. I WOULD APPRECIATE IF WE KEEP OUR CONVERSATIONS FACTUAL AND PROFESSIONAL AND NOT BRING IN ACCUSATIONS THAT CAN'T BE PROVEN.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK AND THEN COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I AM PERSONALLY FINE HEARING IT TODAY. I AM ALSO -- ALSO IF COUNCILMAN CARLSON REALLY WANTS IT IN JANUARY, I AM HAPPY TO DO JANUARY. THE ONLY THING I ASKED -- I SPOKE WITH MISS DUNCAN LAST NIGHT AND THE ONLY THING I ASKED IS THAT THE ACTUAL LITERATURE THAT -- THAT WE SENT TO THE -- TO THE STATE AND THAT THE STATE SENT BACK TO US BE ADDED TO SIRE. YOU SAID YOU COULD DO THAT AT SOME POINT TODAY. SO THAT THE PUBLIC HAS A CHANCE TO GO AND LOOK AT IT IF THEY ARE LISTENING RIGHT NOW OR LISTENING LATER. THEY WILL HAVE A CHANCE TO LOOK AT THOSE DOCUMENTS.

segments.speaker: JEAN DUNCAN
segments.text: THAT IS DEFINITELY PHYSICALLY POSSIBLE. THE CONCERN I HAD AFTER WE SPOKE LAST NIGHT IS THAT THIS IS REALLY A VERBAL ADMINISTRATIVE UPDATE. WASN'T ANY PROTOCOL OR PROCESS THAT THIS TYPE OF THING WILL BE LOADED IN SIRE. WE PERSONALLY E-MAILED, AND IT DIDN'T COME IN THE THOUGHT PROCESS OF DOING THAT. WE NEED TO BE THOUGHTFUL OF WHERE DO WE DRAW THAT LINE AND WHAT IS THE PROCESS FOR SOMETHING REACHING THE LEVEL OF BEING PUT INTO SIRE AND HOW DO WE ALL KNOW WHAT IT IS SO WE DON'T HAVE ONE BE INTERPRETATION AND COUNCIL HAS ANOTHER. SO I HAVEN'T PUT THE ITEMS IN SIRE JUST BECAUSE I WANTED TO HAVE THE OPPORTUNITY TO OFFER THAT THOUGHT OF COUNCIL HAVING, OF WHERE DO WE HAVE DRAW THAT LINE. I DON'T MEAN THAT IN A NEGATIVE WAY, BUT WHAT IS THAT POINT OF DECISION TO PUT IT IN OR NOT.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I WOULD SAY WITH THIS PARTICULAR ISSUE SINCE WE ARE TALKING OF THEIR RESPONSE, THAT IT WILL BE NICE TO SEE THE RESPONSE. SO I AM -- I DEFINITELY THINK THE LANGUAGE SHOULD BE INCLUDED. SO IT'S COUNCIL'S PLEASURE. I AM FINE HEARING IT TODAY. I AM ALSO -- BUT IF WE DO HEAR IT TODAY, I WOULD ALSO LIKE TO BE ABLE TO HEAR IT IN THE FUTURE BECAUSE FOLKS DIDN'T HAVE NECESSARILY TIME TO LOOK AT THE DATA OR THE INFORMATION.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: I WILL HOLD UNTIL JANUARY. LIKE COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK SAID, I AM FLEXIBLE.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMEMBERS, WE TALKED MORE ABOUT THIS ISSUE THAN WHAT WOULD PROBABLY HAVE BEEN PRESENTED. MAY I SEE IN COUNCIL CHAMBERS A SHOW OF HANDS OF PEOPLE WHO ARE HERE TO SPEAK OR LISTEN TO THIS STAFF REPORT? JUST A SHOW OF HANDS. COUNCILMEMBERS, IF I MAY, WHY DON'T WE JUST HEAR THIS, AND IF SOMEBODY WISHES TO MAKE THIS A WORKSHOP ON THE 17th, WE CAN DO THAT. WHAT IS THE PLEASURE OF COUNCIL?

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: SO MOVED, MR. CHAIRMAN.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: SECOND, HEAR IT TODAY. THE PEOPLE ARE HERE. I WOULD HATE TO SEE THEM HAVE TO COME BACK AGAIN.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: GOOD MORNING. EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR OF TAMPA BAY WATER KEEPER. WE ARE A NONPROFIT THAT FIGHTS FOR CLEAN WATER NOT ONLY IN HILLSBOROUGH COUNTY BUT PINELLAS COUNTY. WE REPRESENT THE COMMUNITY'S RIGHT TO CLEAN WATER. I'M HERE TODAY TO DISCUSS THE ITEM THAT YOU GUYS WERE JUST TALKING ABOUT THERE. TAMPA BAY WATER KEEPER APPRECIATES THE -- THE QUESTIONS REGARDING THE DISCHARGE PROJECT. WE APPRECIATE THE STRICT SCRUTINY THAT THE COMMUNITY AND ORGANIZATIONS HAVE PROVIDED YOU ALL. BUT BIG PICTURE. THE CITY OF TAMPA CURRENTLY DISCHARGES AT PINEY POINTS CRISIS WORTH OF NUTRIENTS A YEAR INTO HILLSBOROUGH BAY. WE SUPPORT THE CONVERSATION REGARDING WAYS TO LARGELY REDUCE OR ELIMINATE THE AVERAGE 50 MILLION GALLONS OF TREATED RECLAIMED WATER BEING DISCHARGED DAILY. WE SUPPORT THE CONVERSATION. AND WE APPRECIATE THE CONVERSATION. ELIMINATING OR SIGNIFICANTLY REDUCING THESE NUTRIENTS ARE GOOD FOR TAMPA BAY. BECOMING A MORE RESILIENT AND SUSTAINABLE REGION PROTECTS NOT ONLY OUR WATERSHED BUT OUR COMMUNITY, ENSURING FISHABLE, SWIMMABLE, AND DRINKABLE WATER FOR ALL. WE APPRECIATE THE CONVERSATION. WE SEE IT AS AN OPPORTUNITY. SO THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: IS THAT A SNOOK OR TARPON ON YOUR HAT. COOL HAT, RIGHT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: THANK HIM FOR COMING UP. I NEVER MET HIM. 370 TONS OF THOSE NUTRIENTS THAT YOU ARE TALKING ABOUT. THANK YOU. TAMPA BAY SIERRA CLUB AND MEMBER OF THE PURE STAKEHOLDER GROUP. TALKING ITEM 7. THANK YOU, WE AGREE. WE WANT CLEAN WATER GOING INTO THE BAY. DON'T DISAGREE WITH THAT. WHAT WE WANT TO TALK ABOUT TODAY THE STATE MANDATES THAT MANDATES TO TAKE ALL OF OUR WASTEWATER AND DO SOMETHING ELSE WITH IT INSTEAD OF PUT IT IN THE BAY. YOU HAVE TO REMEMBER THAT TAMPA IS A ESTUARY. ONE OF THE OUTSTANDING ESTUARIES IN THE UNITED STATES. A ESTUARY IS A SEMI ENCLOSED BODY OF WATER WHERE FRESH WATER FROM THE RIVER MIXES AND MEETS WITH THE SALT WATER FROM THE OCEAN. ESTUARIES ARE TRANSITION ZONE FROM LAND TO SEA AND SUPPORT A SPECTACULAR ABUNDANCE AND DIVERSITY OF WILDLIFE. CONSIDERED ONE OF THE MOST PRODUCTIVE ENVIRONMENTS IN THE WORLD AND OFTEN REFERRED AS A NURSERY ROUND FOR FISH, CRUSTACEANS AND SHELL FISH AND TO HIDE FROM PREDATORS. BAY WASTEWATER PROVIDES A FLOW TO THE ESTUARY REPLACING SOME OF THE FRESH WATER THAT TAMPA AND TAMPA BAY WATER WITHDRAW FROM THE HILLSBOROUGH RIVER AND BYPASS CANAL FOR DRINKING WATER. THE BILL 64 THAT REQUIRES THE ELIMINATION OF SURFACE WATER HAS A BIG GAP WITH UNINTENDED CONSEQUENCES. DOES NOT COVER THE ECOLOGICAL BENEFIT TO ESTUARIES EVEN THOUGH THEY RELY ON FRESHWATER FLOW. LUMPING ESTUARIES AS PART OF MARINE WATERS. ESTUARIES ARE QUITE DIFFERENT FROM OCEANS AND HAVE DIFFERENT NEEDS. THEREFORE, WE A PROPOSED LEGISLATION TO ADD LANGUAGE TO ALLOW USING RECLAIMED WATER TO PROVIDE FRESH WATER FLOWS TO ESTUARIES. WE ARE ASKING THIS TO BE ONE OF YOUR LEGISLATIVE PRIORITIES. IF TAMPA WERE FORCED TO REDUCE ALL 50 MILLION GALLONS OF DAY OF WASTEWATER A HUGE COST TO TAMPA TAXPAYERS AND BILLIONS OF DOLLARS AND IMPACT THE ESTUARY DEPRIVING OF THE CONSTANT FRESH WATER FLOW THAT WE HAVE COME TO A RELY ON. BUT ALLOWING THE CONTINUATION OF THE CURRENT DISCHARGE AS PART OF THE SOLUTION INTO THE ESTUARY IS A BENEFICIAL USE WILL GIVE TAMPA FLEXIBILITY TO PURSUE USING THE RECLAIMED WATER FOR NEEDED PURPOSES THAT ARE IN THE BEST PUBLIC INTEREST. AND TREATING THE WATER TO THE APPROPRIATE LEVELS TO MAKE IT SAFE FOR THAT PURPOSE. AND THIS WILL RESULT IN A LOWER COST OVERALL SINCE THE RECLAIMED WATER WILL BE TREATED ONLY IF NEEDED. AND CONTROLLING NITROGEN IN THE BAY IS KEY TO THAT. AS WELL. WE ASK THE MAYOR AND CITY COUNCIL TO LISTEN TO CITIZENS' CONCERNS AND MAKE IT ONE OF YOUR TOP LEGISLATIVE PRIORITIES. PLEASE DON'T ALLOW THE STATE TO FORCE TAMPA RESIDENTS TO SPEND BILLIONS OF DOLLARS TO TREAT AND REROUTE LARGE QUANTITIES OF WASTEWATER REUSE THAT WE DON'T NEED AND CAN POTENTIALLY BE HARMFUL TO THE WATER RESOURCES, HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT OF OUR REGION. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU. MY NAME IS SID FLANNERY. A LONG-TIME RESIDENT OF THE TAMPA AREA AND A RETIRED CHIEF ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST WITH THE SOUTHWEST WATER MANAGEMENT DISTRICT WHERE I WORKED FOR NEARLY 30 YEARS STUDYING AND MANAGING FRESH WATER FLOW TO ESTUARIES, INCLUDING THE ESTABLISHMENT OF MINIMUM FLOW RULES TO RIVERS THAT FLOW TO TAMPA BAY. IN RESPONSE TO THE REQUIREMENT OF SENATE BILL 64 PASSED BY THE FLORIDA LEGISLATURE IN 2021, CITY STAFF RECENTLY SUBMITTED A REQUEST FOR THE FLORIDA DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TO DETERMINE IF DISCHARGES FROM THE CITY'S HOWARD F. CURREN WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANT PROVIDED ECOLOGICAL BENEFITS TO TAMPA BAY. WITH THAT REQUEST, STAFF SUBMITTED A 20-PAGE TECHNICAL REPORT AND PREVIOUSLY SUBMITTED TO CITY STAFF FOR THEIR REVIEW. THE DEP DENIED THEIR REQUEST TO RECOGNIZE THE ECONOMIC DISCHARGE FROM THE AWT PLANT TO TAMPA BAY. IN A SHORT E-MAIL RESPONSE, DEP REPLIED AND I QUOTE, THE ECOLOGICAL BENEFIT PROVISION IN SENATE BILL 64 ADDRESSES WATER QUANTITY OF MINIMUM FLOW AND HE MINIMUM LEVEL MFL REQUIREMENTS. MARINE DOES NOT HAVE A MFL CLASSIFICATION, THE DISCHARGE FROM THE HOWARD F. CURREN WATER TREATMENT FACILITY DOES NOT QUALIFY FOR THE ECOLOGICAL BENEFIT PROVISION IN SENATE BILL 64. THIS FINDING BY DEP REFLECTS THE SHORTCOMING IN THE LANGUAGE IN SENATE BILL 64 EVEN THOUGH A MINIMUM FLOW RULE HAS NOT BEEN ESTABLISHED SPECIFICALLY FOR TAMPA BAY, FRESH WATER FLOW IS CRITICAL OF MAINTAINING THE WATER QUALITY, ECOLOGY AND BIOLOGICAL PRODUCTIVITY OF THE BAY. THE FACT THAT THE CITY'S DISCHARGE IS LOCATED ON SEVEN CHANNEL THAT DEP CATEGORIZED AS MARINE WATER DOES NOT NEGATE THE IMPORTANCE OF THIS DISCHARGE TO TAMPA BAY. IN MY OPINION, DEP'S CONCLUSION WAS NOT SUFFICIENTLY SCIENCE OR RESOURCE-BASED BUT INSTEAD WAS BASED ON A TECHNICAL, BUREAUCRATIC AMBIGUITY THAT RESULTED FROM A LACK OF CLARITY IN SENATE BILL 64. AFTER CAREFUL REVIEW, I, AND MEMBERS OF SEVERAL ORGANIZATIONS THAT ARE ON THE CITY'S PURE STAKEHOLDERS GROUP, BELIEVE THAT ADDITIONAL LANGUAGE NEED TO BE PASSED BY THE OF FLORIDA LEGISLATURE AND ADOPTED IN FLORIDA STATUTES TO REMEDY THE INACCURACY OF SENATE BILL 64. ACCORDINGLY, WE HAVE PREPARED A DRAFT DOCUMENT AND RECOMMENDED LANGUAGE THAT CAN BE ADDED TO THE STATUTE TO ALLOW FOR MORE COMPREHENSIVE ASSESSMENT OF THE BENEFICIAL EFFECTS OF SURFACE DISCHARGES TO WATER BODIES INCLUDING ESTUARYS SUCH AS TAMPA BAY. WE WILL BE HAPPY TO SHARE OUR DOCUMENT WITH YOU AND DISCUSS THE STRAIGHTFORWARD LANGUAGE WE HAVE PROPOSED THAT CAN REMEDY THIS PROBLEM. WE HOPE THAT AFTER CAREFUL CONSIDERATION, THE CITY CAN SUPPORT OUR RECOMMENDED ADDITION TO THIS STATUTE IN YOUR LEGISLATIVE AGENDA FOR 2023. I HAVE TO WRAP UP MY COMMENTS NOW. BUT I WILL BE HAPPY TO REMAIN IN THE CHAMBER WHEN THIS TOPIC COMES UP ON YOUR AGENDA LATER TODAY. IF YOU HAVE ANY QUESTIONS OF OUR RECOMMENDED ADDITIONS TO THE STATUTE. THANK YOU VERY MUCH FOR YOUR TIME.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU.

segments.text: MR. CHAIRMAN.

MAYBE THE RULE IS THERE BUT MAYBE JUST LIKE HERE, WE PASS THINGS AND HAVE TO COME BACK TO LOOK AT THINGS THAT ARE NOT TEA TOLLY -- TOTALLY CLEAR. I HOPE THAT WE GET THAT INFORMATION TO MOVE FORWARD.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: I WANT TO SAY FIRST OF ALL, THANK YOU FOR ALL THE HARD WORK YOU PUT INTO THE LETTER THAT WAS SENT. AND THANK YOU TO CHUCK WAGNER AND STAFF FOR SUBMITTING YOUR LETTER. WHEN I MENTIONED THE WORD "SUSPICIOUS," I DIDN'T MEAN TO INFER THAT SOMETHING WAS HAPPENING AT THE WATER DEPARTMENT ARE, BUT WE KNOW IN THE PAST THE CITY LOBBYING DEPARTMENT WERE IN FAVOR OF IT AND TOLD ME OUTRIGHT THEY WERE IN FAVOR NOT AGAINST IT. THEY WERE RELUCTANT TO LET THE WATER DEPARTMENT SEND OUT YOUR MEMO. SO I THINK WE SO FAR HAVE MULTIPLE VOTES OF CITY COUNCIL AGAINST THIS PROJECT. AND BY NOW, IF THE CITY WAS OPERATING AS ONE ENTITY, THE LOBBYING TEAM AND LEGAL TEAM WILL BE LOOKING FOR SOLUTIONS TO TRY TO STOP THIS. AND THEY HAVEN'T. UNFORTUNATELY NEITHER ONE REPORTS TO CITY COUNCIL. WE NEED TO DO EVERYTHING WE CAN TO STOP IT. THE SUSPICIOUS PART I TALKED ABOUT WAY YOU JUST REFERENCED IS A STATE LAW, BUT SOMEHOW THE TWO OR THREE SENTENCE EXCUSE WAS THAT IT DOESN'T HIT MFL. WHAT ABOUT CITIES AND COUNTIES THAT DON'T HAVE RIVERS? THAT IS LANGUAGE THAT LOOKS LIKE IT WAS PUT IN THERE BY TAMPA LOBBYISTS YEARS AGO. I KNOW THAT TAMPA LOBBYISTS WERE THERE BECAUSE LOBBYISTS IN TALLAHASSEE THAT TALK TO LEGISLATORS AFTER THEY TALKED TO THEM. I CAN GET MY LOBBYIST AND SEE IF ANYONE HAS REACHED OUT TO THEM IN THE LAST MONTH. THE PUBLIC DOESN'T WANT THIS. A HUGE BURDEN FOR TAXPAYERS. PEOPLE ARE PAYING TOO MUCH AND THEY BE DON'T WANT THEIR WATER BILL DOUBLED AND TRIPLED. THE ADMINISTRATION AND THE CITY COUNCIL NEEDS TO STOP THIS.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: I WILL GIVE YOU REBUTTAL. MINUTES. RECOGNIZE THE ECOLOGICAL BENEFITS TO THE DISCHARGE OF TAMPA BAY, THAT DOES NOT PRECLUDE PURSUANT OF RECLAIMED WATER PROJECTS. RECLAIMED WATER PROJECTS CAN BE REVIEWED FOR THE QUANTITY THAT YOU NEED. YOU KNOW, WHATEVER THE QUANTITY IS, BUT IT MEANS WHERE THE DISCHARGE IS OCCURRING NOW IS ACTUALLY OKAY, OKAY. AND WE CAN STILL PURSUE RECLAIMED WATER PROJECTS INDIVIDUALLY ON A CASE-BY-CASE BASIS. SIMILARLY IF THE TAMPA BAY ESTUARY PROGRAMS AS WE NEED TO DO MORE NUTRIENT PRODUCTION, THAT CAN BE PURSUED. AMENDING THE STATUTE, WHAT WE SUGGEST, WHAT IT DOES DO, GET THE CITY OUT WITH THE HAMMER WITH EVERYTHING FROM 50 MGD IN TEN YEARS. ALLOW OPERATIONAL ABILITIES TO REVIEW RECLAIMED WATER PROJECTS ON A CASE-BY-CASE BASIS.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU, COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I WILL ASK IF YOU SEND THAT INFORMATION TO ALL OF US, THAT WILL BE WONDERFUL.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: MR. CAMPBELL TALKED OF THE AMOUNT OF NUTRIENTS GOING INTO THE BAY. WHAT IS THE AMOUNT OF CONTINUAGE. AS AN EXPERT TELL ME -- I WANT CLARITY, HOW MANY TONS OF NUTRIENTS GO INTO THE BAY EVERY YEAR? I HAVE READ THE DOCUMENTS AND REPORT I GOT HAVE GRAPHICS OF NUTRIENTS -- NITROGEN LOADING INTO THE BAY. ONE THING WE SHOULD BE HAPPY ABOUT, TAMPA BAY IN LONG TERM HAS BEEN IMPROVING IN LONG PART WITH THE WATER TREATMENT AT HOWARD F. CURREN PLANT. WILL NOT PRECLUDE NUTRIENTS STUDIES. IT CAN IMPROPER, IF NECESSARY.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: IT WAS BETTER NOW THAN IT WAS IN THE '50s.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: APPRECIATE YOUR SINCERENESS. I AM PHIL COMPTON WITH FRIENDS OF THE HILLSBOROUGH RIVER, BUT TODAY I AM SHARING WITH YOU SOME COMMENTS FROM SIERRA CLUB'S VICE-CHAIR GABBY GIBBONS WHO IS THE CHAIR OF OUR POLITICAL COMMITTEE WHO IS UNABLE TO ATTEND TODAY'S CITY COUNCIL MEETING DUE TO SCHEDULING CONFLICT. GARY WANTS ME TO TELL YOU THAT I HAVE PRACTICED LAW FOR 45 YEARS. AND EACH YEAR, ATTORNEYS NEED TO REVIEW THE SESSION LAWS TO SEE WHAT CHANGES HAVE BEEN MADE TOO EXISTING LAW. MANY TIMES A TWEAK OF THE LANGUAGE IN AN EXISTING LAW THAT HAS A DEFECT FOR OMISSION OR NEEDS CLARIFICATION BECAUSE OF AN AM GIG GUTIERREZ IS CAUSING LITIGATION. SENATE BILL 64 IS A PRIME EXAMPLE OF THE TYPE OF STATUTE THAT NEEDS FURTHER CLARIFICATION. SENATE BILL 64 WAS A ONE SIZE FITS ALL AMBITIOUS WAY TO DEAL WITH A COMPLEX ISSUE TO SAFELY REPURPOSE WASTEWATER FOR BENEFICIAL USES ACROSS THE STATE TOO LARGE AND TOO DIVERSE IN TOPOGRAPHY TO ALLOW A ONE SIZE FITS ALL APPROACH. IN ONE OF OUR MANY STAKEHOLDER MEETINGS WITH CITY STAFF OVER THE PAST TWO YEARS, FORMER ATTORNEY JAN MCLEAN DESCRIBE SENATE BILL 64 AS A SOLUTION LOOKING FOR A PROBLEM. I AGREE WITH HER INTERPRETATION, IT IS NOW LAW AND THE TIME TO ASK THAT OMISSIONS BE STICKSED BY LEGISLATION THAT WILL REVISE IT. SID FLANNERY WHO DEVOTED HIS LIFE'S WORK AS A SCIENTIST ON S.W.F.W.M.D. AND MINIMUM FLOWS ON THE RIVERS AND EFFECTS ON ESTUARY WATERS LIKE TAMPA BAY HAVE SEEN SHORTCOMINGS IN SENATE BILL 64 THAT NEEDS TO BE REVISED. AND HURRIED PASSED IN 2021 AND DEALING WITH APPROACHING DEADLINES, IT IS MORE IMPORTANT THAN EVER TO FIX THE OMISSIONS. AND HE NOT ONLY FIXED BUT  HAVE IT TO FIX A GLARING OMISSION AND PROVIDED MUCH-NEEDED FLEXIBILITY FOR THE CITY OF TAMPA TO DEAL WITH THE SITE-SPECIFIC ISSUE THAT ARE COMPLETELY DIFFERENT THAN THOSE BEING DEALT WITH IN MIAMI OR THE PANHANDLE. AND STAKEHOLDERS SEEKING REVISIONS OF CITY STAFF ON MANY LOCATIONS. TODAY, WE, THE STAKEHOLDERS, ASK CITY COUNCIL TO MAKE A FORMAL REQUEST TO THE MAYOR TO HAVE THE REVISIONS OF SENATE BILL 64 TO BE THE TOP PRIORITY OF THE CITY OF TAMPA'S LOBBYISTS TO SEEK THE LANGUAGE PROPOSED BY MR. FLANNERY AND THE STAKEHOLDERS WE SEE AS A WIN-WIN FOR EVERYONE IN THE CITY OF TAMPA. LET'S TAKE A PROACTIVE STEP TO FIX THE STATUTE SO THE CITY WILL HAVE THE FLEXIBILITY THAT IT NEEDS TO EXPLORE ALL ALTERNATIVES FOR THE SAFE REUSE OF ITS TREATED WASTEWATER. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MR. COMPTON OPINION.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.text: PERFECT. MAY I ADD PLEASE, YEARS AGO I WORKED WITH JAN MCLEAN FOR FERTILIZER ORDINANCE HERE WHICH HAS BEEN EXTREMELY SUCCESSFUL. AND THAT WAS PART OF THE SIERRA CLUB'S STATEWIDE EFFORT TO GET FERTILIZER ORDINANCES PASS IN OVER 100 CITIES AND COUNTIES BUT NEVER SAID THAT THE STATE OF FLORIDA SHOULD HAVE ONE FERTILIZER TYPE OF RULE BECAUSE THE STATE IS TOO BIG AND DIVERSE AND IN SOME PLACE IT IS WOULDN'T MAKE SENSE. SAME THING IS TRUE HERE. THANK YOU.

segments.text: MR. MR. COMPTON, HAPPY BIRTHDAY. YOU CAN NOW PUT UP CHRISTMAS DECORATIONS BECAUSE IT IS AFTER MY BIRTHDAY. THANK YOU. [LAUGHTER] MY NAME IS CAROL CAMISA, AND I'M HERE AS A MEMBER OF THE STAKEHOLDERS GROUP AND REPRESENTING THE LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF HILLSBOROUGH COUNTY. MY INTEREST IS IN ENGAGING THE PUBLIC IN ISSUES, POLICY, AND SO ON, THAT IMPACT PEOPLE'S LIVES. TO DO THAT WE EDUCATE PEOPLE. WE ARE ALSO INTERESTED -- AND I AM VERY PLEASED THAT THIS BODY REFLECTS AN INTEREST IN TRANSPARENCY, ACCURACY OF INFORMATION GOING TO THE PUBLIC AND SO ON. AND I AM SORRY, I -- I -- KIND OF WATER UNDER THE BRIDGE, BAD CHOICE OF WORDS BUT I FEEL IT IS NECESSARY TO CORRECT. THE STAKEHOLDERS GROUP HAVE DONE AN AWFUL LOT IN TERMS OF PUBLIC EDUCATION AND WE HAVE REACHED OUT TO A LOT OF DIFFERENT GROUPS. I WOULD VENTURE TO SAY THAT BEFORE THE SEPTEMBER CITY COUNCIL MEETING, WE DID MORE IN A MONTH'S TIME WITH NO MONEY REACHING OUT TO COMMUNITY GROUPS THAT HAVE BEEN DONE TO DATE WITH REGARDS TO THIS PROJECT. AND I HAVE YET TO HEAR ANY ACCOUNTABILITY IN TERMS OF THE FUNDING THAT WAS GIVEN TO THE WATER DEPARTMENT FOR TWO SPECIFIC TASKS UNDER PURE. ONE OF WHICH WAS PUBLIC EDUCATION. SO I JUST FEEL LIKE IT IS IMPORTANT TO CORRECT THAT RECORD. IN ANY CASE, YOU, NO DOUBT, REMEMBER PREVIOUS DISCUSSIONS WHEN TAMPA CITIZENS SPOKE UP ABOUT THEIR CONCERNS ABOUT THE COST, ABOUT THE SAFETY OF WATER, POTABLE WATER SUPPLY IN TAMPA AND MY CONCERN NOW IS THAT THIS -- THIS SENATE BILL 64 SEEMS TO JEOPARDIZE THESE OBJECTIVES. SO WE ARE PURSUING SOME ALTERNATIVE LANGUAGE TO FIX THAT, AS YOU HEARD FROM MY COLLEAGUES. SO -- THE CURRENT LAW UNDER SENATE BILL 64 DOESN'T REALLY ADDRESS ENVIRONMENTAL ISSUES RELATED TO TREATED WASTEWATER DISCHARGES INTO THE BAY. AND THAT'S NOT SOMETHING THAT IS WELL UNDERSTOOD BY CITY RESIDENTS. THEY ARE CONCERNED OF HOW MUCH THEY ARE GOING TO PAY WHEN THEY OPEN THE TAP THAT IT IS SAFE. YOU WOULD AGREE THAT IT IS REALLY YOUR JOB TO UNDERSTAND THOSE TYPE OF THINGS AND REPRESENT TAMPA'S BEST INTEREST. SO WE TALKED ABOUT THE PREVIOUS COMMENTS THAT THIS IS A RATHER BLUNT INSTRUMENT, A ONE SIZE FITS ALL KIND OF APPROACH THAT REALLY DOESN'T MEET THE NEEDS OF TAMPA WE NEED MORE FLEXIBILITY. WE NEED TO BE ABLE TO ADAPT TO SPECIFIC LOCAL CONDITIONS AND -- THAT ARE DIFFERENT IN DIFFERENT PARTS OF THE STATE. AND I FURTHER SORT OF OBJECT THAT THIS IS KIND OF AN UNFUNDED MANDATE OUT OF TALLAHASSEE THAT TELLS YOU TO FIX SOMETHING AND THERE IS NO FUNDING INVOLVED. SO THE LEGISLATIVE PROPOSAL FOR WHICH WE ARE SEEKING YOUR SUPPORT WOULD ALLOW TAMPA THE FLEXIBILITY TO MEET THE INTENT OF SB 64 WHILE ALLOWING FOR NECESSARY FLEXIBILITY AND ADAPTABILITY. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU. TAMPA THE ADDITION TO FLORIDA STATUTES THAT WE ARE RECOMMEND ALSO ALLOW THE RECOGNITION OF THE BENEFITS OF THE DISCHARGES TO TAMPA BAY AND WILL NOT PRECLUDE THE EVALUATION OF OTHER RECLAIMED WATER PROJECTS. INSTEAD, IT WILL GIVE THE CITY MUCH GREATER FLEXIBILITY TO EVALUATE THESE PROJECTS IN QUANTITIES THAT ARE NEEDED, THAT ARE COST EFFECTIVE, THAT PROTECT THE WATER RESOURCES IN THE NATURAL ENVIRONMENT. AND THEY ARE IN THE BEST PUBLIC INTEREST. THIS IS MUCH DIFFERENT THAN REQUIRING THE CITY TO ELIMINATE AND REROUTE 50 MILLION GALLONS OF DISCHARGE A DAY IN THE NEXT TEN YEARS, WHICH THE CURRENT STATUTE DOES. OUR ADDITIONS TO THE STATUTE WILL REMEDY THIS DILEMMA. WE ARE ASKING CITY COUNCIL TO MAKE A FORMAL REQUEST TO THE MAYOR TO MAKE THIS THE TOP PRIORITY FOR THE CITY'S LOBBYISTS AND SEEK LANGUAGE BY MR. FLANNERY AND THE STAKEHOLDERS THAT WE SEE AS A WIN-WIN FOR EVERY CITIZEN IN OF TAMPA. TAKE A PROACTIVE STEP TO FIX THIS SO THE CITY HAS THE ABILITY TO EXPLORE ALL ALTERNATIVES FOR THE SAFE REUSE OF ITS TREATED WASTEWATER. I, LIKE CARROLL, TAKE THE STAKEHOLDERS AS BEING JUST A FEW PEOPLE. WE ALL REPRESENT ORGANIZATIONS THAT AMOUNT TO TENS OF THOUSANDS OF PEOPLE. WE HAVE ALL GONE THROUGH THE PROCESSES THAT THESE ORGANIZATIONS REQUIRED TO BE ABLE TO REPRESENT THEM IN FRONT OF YOU. I AM THE T.H.A.N. REPRESENTATIVE IN THE CITY'S PURE STAKEHOLDER GROUP. T.H.A.N.'S MEMBERS AND ALL TAMPA RESIDENTS ARE VERY, VERY CONCERNED OF THE SAFETY OF THEIR WATER AND HOW MUCH IT COSTS. THERE WAS A RECENT UNANIMOUS T.H.A.N. VOTE TO THAT EFFECT. YOU ALL ALSO RECEIVED MANY E-MAILS EXPRESSING THE UNANIMOUS CONCERNS OF THE CITIZENS. HOW MANY E-MAILS DID YOU RECEIVE SUPPORTING PURE? THE CITIZENS AND MEMBERS OF T.H.A.N. HAVE NOT BEEN MADE AWARE OF THIS THING THAT WE ARE ADDRESSING TODAY. THEY HAVE NOT BEEN MADE ABOUT IT YET, BUT WHEN THEY DO, THEY WILL BE WRITING TO CITY COUNCIL AND THE MAYOR ASKING YOU TO MAKE THIS A TOP LEGISLATIVE PRIORITY. WE WILL ALL BE HAPPY TO WORK WITH THE CITY TO MAKE SURE THAT THE CITIZENS' VOICES ARE HEARD. ON A PERSONAL NOTE THAT CHARLEY MENTIONED ABOUT THE BAY. WHEN I WAS A KID, OUR CAR DIDN'T HAVE AIR CONDITIONING. AND NO MATTER HOW HOT IT WAS, WHEN WE HAD TO DRIVE DOWN BAYSHORE WE DROVE WITH THE WINDOW ROLLED UP BECAUSE THE STENCH WAS ABSOLUTELY NAUSEATING. AND I KNOW CHARLIE PROBABLY REMEMBERS THAT TOO. I REMEMBER LOOKING AT THE BEAUTIFUL HOUSES -- SOME AT THAT TIME WERE ACTUALLY ABANDONED. THE BEAUTIFUL HOUSES ON BAYSHORE BOULEVARD AND WONDERING HOW DO THEY LIVE WITH THIS? HOW CAN THEY DO THIS? MAYBE THAT IS WHY SO MANY HOUSES WERE ABANDONED. WE HAVE COME A LONG WAY AND MADE ADVANCES IN OUR ECOLOGICAL BENEFITS TO THE BAY. THANK YOU. I AM A RESIDENT OF THE CITY OF TAMPA FOR OVER TEN YEARS AND I'M HERE TO SPEAK OF AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 19, WHICH IS THE PROPOSED LANGUAGE TO PROVIDE AN INDEPENDENT COUNSEL FOR THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD. I THINK I APPLAUD ALL OF YOU FOR MOVING THAT FORWARD BECAUSE I THINK AN INDEPENDENT COUNSEL FOR THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD IS CRUCIAL TO MAINTAIN ITS INDEPENDENCE AND PREVENT CONFLICTS OF INTEREST. I WOULD LIKE TO READING IS APPLICABLE FROM THE WEB SITE OF THE NATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF CIVILIAN OVERSIGHT OF LAW ENFORCEMENT WITH RESPECTS TO THE IMPORTANCE OF INDEPENDENCE. IT SAYS ONE OF THE MOST IMPORTANT AND DEFINING CONCEPTS OF CIVILIAN OVERSIGHT OF LAW ENFORCEMENT IS INDEPENDENCE. IN ITS BROADEST SENSE, REFERS TO THE ABSENCE OF REAL OR PERCEIVED INFLUENCE OF LAW ENFORCEMENT, POLITICAL ACTORS AND OTHER SPECIAL INTERESTS LOOKING TO AFFECT THE CIVILIAN OVERSIGHT AGENCY. IN ORDER TO HAVE LEGITIMACY, THE RESPECT AND INDEPENDENCE OF THE OVERSEEING LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCY. ESPECIALLY WITH THE HIGH-PROFILE INCIDENTS. WITH THAT I WOULD LIKE TO ADDRESS THE SPECIFIC LANGUAGE PRESENTED TO YOU BY THE CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE FOR THIS PROPOSED ORDINANCE CHANGE OR PROPOSED CHARTER CHANGE RATHER. CURRENT LANGUAGE BEFORE YOU FROM THE CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE WOULD PROVIDE THAT THE CITY HAS THE SOLE POWER TO SELECT, HIRE AND FIRE, ESSENTIALLY, THE ATTORNEY FOR THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD, WHICH WOULD REALLY NOT CORRECT THE PROBLEM ABOUT CONFLICT OF INTEREST THAT CURRENTLY EXISTS WITH IT BEING STAFFED BY AN ASSISTANT ATTORNEY. AND I SENT AN E-MAIL WITH THE REQUEST TO MAKE TWO MODIFICATIONS OF THAT LANGUAGE. THE FIRST MODIFICATION TO PROVIDE THAT THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD IS THE BODY THAT HAS SOLE SHORT TO HIRE AND FIRE ITS OWN ATTORNEY. AND THE SECOND MODIFICATION TO DELETE LANGUAGE FROM THE END OF THAT PROPOSED SENTENCE THAT IS UNNECESSARY AND POTENTIALLY CONFUSING AS DETAILED IN THE E-MAIL. THE LANGUAGE OFF THE TOP OF MY HEAD WAS FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT, I BELIEVE, WHICH IS REALLY AN UNNECESSARY PART OF THAT SENTENCE. I HOPE THAT YOU ALL MAKE THOSE TWO MOTIONS TO MAKE THOSE MODIFICATIONS AND MOVE THE LANGUAGE FORWARD AS I DETAILED IN THAT E-MAIL. THANK YOU VERY MUCH.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU. MY NAME IS PASTOR FRANK WILLIAMS. LOCATED AT 1112 EACH SCOTT STREET, PARADISE MISSIONARY BAPTIST CHURCH. I HAVE BEEN COMING DOWN HERE WEEK AFTER WEEK, MONTH AFTER MONTH. DON'T SEEM TO GET NOTHING DONE. BUT I THANK GOD WE ARE HERE TO SEE ANOTHER DAY. WHAT IS YOUR NAME.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: GUIDO MANISCALCO. WHEN THE CHAIRMAN STEPS OUT AS THE VICE-CHAIR, I TAKE OVER THE MEETING. BECAUSE -- I AM GOING TO READ SOME SCRIPTURE. I SAID TO YOU, HE WHO BELIEVETH IN ME, JESUS TALKING. WHO DON'T BELIEVETH ON ME AND THE WORK THAT HE DO SHALL -- SHALL HE DO ALSO. WORK THAT I SHALL DO ALSO. AND GATHER -- AND GREATER WORK THAN THEE. BECAUSE I AM MY FATHER AND WHO SOEVER ASKS IN MY NAME, THAT I WILL GIVE UNTO THEE. MY FATHER IS GLORIFIED IN THE SON. THANK YOU, JESUS, THAT HE IS. YOU KNOW, WE GOT MANY PROBLEMS HERE IN THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA. AND IT DON'T SEEM TO BE GETTING NO BETTER. IT IS GETTING WORSE AND WORSE AND WORSER. GOT A RETENTION -- THEY CALL IT A RETENTION POND OUT THERE IN BELMONT HEIGHTS. AND PRIME PROPERTY OUT THERE WHICH THEY TOOK FROM BLACK FOLKS AND DECIDED TO PUT RETENTION PONDS. I WANTED TO KNOW SOMETHING. WHY DON'T Y'ALL FILL IN THOSE RETENTION PONDS AND LET ME BUILD A NICE CONVENTION CENTER OUT THERE. YOU CAN DO IT BECAUSE IF YOU HAVE ANY POWER AT ALL, YOU CAN DO ANYTHING ELSE, YOU WILL BE ABLE TO DO THAT FOR ME IN THE NAME OF JESUS. I HAVE A LITTLE QUOTE TOMORROW. AND I WILL BRING ALL THAT STUFF UP. AND I WOULD LIKE FOR ALL OF YOU SHOW UP TOMORROW AT 9:00, WHAT THEY CALL -- JUDGE EDGECOMB COURT. YOU KNOW, I JUST WILL HAVE SO MUCH TO SAY, BUT WHEN I GET UP HERE, I FORGET ABOUT IT. BUT YOU KNOW WHAT, WE GOT TO LEARN TO RESPECT ONE ANOTHER. AND NOT DISRESPECT CERTAIN PEOPLE BECAUSE OF THE COLOR OF THEIR SKIN. RESPECT ALL PEOPLE THAT WE WILL BE ABLE TO DO WHAT WE NEED TO DO AND YOU ALL DO WHAT YOU ALL NEED TO DO FOR US. I KNOW YOU ARE NOT GOING TO CALL ME BACK UP HERE BECAUSE YOU ALL DON'T WANT TO HEAR WHAT I AM SAYING. BUT I GIVE GOD THE GLORY FOR EVERYTHING. THAT IS THE WAY I AM. AND GOD HAS BEEN MIGHTY GOOD TO ALL OF US. I JUST WANT TO PRAISE HIM EVERYWHERE I GO. WE HAVE -- YOU KNOW, JUST LIKE I SAID, I WANT TO TALK ABOUT SO MUCH. I WANT TOP TALK -- THEY ARE RINGING THE BELL ON ME. THAT MEANS Y'ALL DON'T WANT TO HEAR WHAT I GOT TO SAY. OKAY, I WILL SIT DOWN. LET ME GET MY BIBLE AND GET OUT OF HERE. THANKS FOR THE LITTLE BIT Y'ALL HEARD.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU, SIR.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: GOOD MORNING. I AM A TAMPA NATIVE. I AM ALSO AN ATTORNEY AND A WRONGFUL CONVICTION SPECIALIST. I'M HERE TO SPEAK ON AGENDA NUMBER 19, THE INDEPENDENT COUNSEL FOR THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD. THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD NEEDS A COUNSEL THAT IS NOT ASSOCIATED WITH THE CITY DUE TO CONFLICT OF INTEREST. EVEN IF WHOLEHEARTEDLY THE CITY ATTORNEY ASSIGNED TO THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD STAYS AWAY FROM THE OTHER TASKS OF ITS OFFICE, IT STILL GIVES THE PUBLIC PERCEPTION HE OF THE CITY -- OF THE ATTORNEY WORKING FOR THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD ALSO WORKING FOR THE CITY. IT'S A CONFLICT OF INTEREST TO HAVE THE SAME OFFICE ON BOTH SIDES OF AN INVESTIGATION. AND AS AN EXAMPLE, THE CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE RECENTLY SETTLED THE LAWSUIT FOR THE DEATH OF ARTHUR GREEN. SO TO HAVE AN ATTORNEY FROM THAT OFFICE WORK BOTH ON THE SETTLEMENT PROTECTING THE CITY'S INTEREST AND THE AMOUNT OF MONEY THAT THE CITY IS GOING TO PAY OUT FOR HIS DEATH, TO ALSO BE THE SAME OFFICE THAT WORKS WITH THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD, WHICH IS TASKED ON INVESTIGATING WHETHER OR NOT TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT ACTED PROPERLY WHEN ARTHUR GREEN DIED, THAT IS A COMPLETE CONFLICT OF INTEREST. THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD NEEDS ITS OWN INDEPENDENT COUNSEL THAT IS NOT ASSOCIATED WITH THE CITY. AND AS ANOTHER EXAMPLE, WE DID SEE RECENTLY, CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE REFUSING TO PROTECT THE INTEREST OF CITY COUNCILMEMBERS WHEN THE OFFICE IS TASKED WITH PROTECTING CITY COUNCIL AND PROTECTING THE CITY. AND I FEAR THAT HAVING THE CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE AS THE ATTORNEY FOR THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD WILL PUT THAT ATTORNEY IN THE SAME POSITION. THAT IS ALL THAT I HAVE. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU. MY NAME IS THEY. AND I AM SPEAKING AGAINST THE PROPOSED LANGUAGE IN AGENDA ITEM 19. AS IT STANDS RIGHT NOW, THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD'S ATTORNEY ALSO REPRESENTS THE CITY, WHICH IS A CONFLICT OF INTEREST, ESPECIALLY GIVEN OUR EX-POLICE CHIEF MAYOR AND IT WILL HINDER -- IT HINDERS THEIR ABILITY TO PROPERLY GIVE POLICE OVERSIGHT. AND THE PROPOSED LANGUAGE MAKES SO THAT THE CITY GETS TO CHOOSE THEIR ATTORNEY AS OPPOSED TO HAVING THE SAME ATTORNEY. WHICH DOESN'T SOUND INDEPENDENT TO ME AT ALL. WHAT I WOULD LIKE TO SEE IS FOR THEM TO HAVE THEIR OWN ATTORNEY TO BE ABLE TO HIRE AND FIRE WHOEVER THEY WANT AS THEIR ATTORNEY. GIVEN THE MAYOR'S OFFICE OR THE MAYOR PICKS HALF AND YOU ALL PICK HALF OF THE CRB, OF THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD, YOU WOULD THINK YOU WOULD BE ABLE TO TRUST THEM ENOUGH TO HAVE POWER OVER THAT THEMSELVES. I THINK IT IS VERY IMPORTANT FOR THEM TO BE INDEPENDENT, ESPECIALLY IF, YOU KNOW, THEY ARE GOING TO BE LOOKING OVER POLICE CASES AND GIVING THOSE RECOMMENDATIONS. WE WANT TO HAVE THEM BE ABLE TO BE TRANSPARENT AND ABLE TO CARRY OUT THEIR JOBS TO HAVE SOME SORT OF POLICE TRANSPARENCY AND ACCOUNTABILITY IN THE CITY. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU. I HAVE A VISUAL AID ON THE ELMO, IF WE COULD SWITCH TO IT. JAMES MICHAEL SHAW, JR. I AM WITH THE LOCAL ACLU CHAPTER. I WANT TO ECHO WHAT ATTORNEY COTHERN AND CLAPHOLT SAID ON AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 19. I DON'T KNOW HOW TO SWITCH TO THE ELMO. IS THERE A BUTTON. OFFICE WITH THE MODIFICATIONS WE ARE ASKING FOR. THE THEY WAY DRAFT IT, THE CITY WILL PROVIDE LEGAL COUNSEL. THE CITY WHO? THE MAYOR, YOU ALL, ATTORNEY'S OFFICE, IT DOESN'T SPECIFY. MOTION MADE AND PASSED IS TO MODEL THE LANGUAGE OFF OF THE SIMILAR LANGUAGE FROM OTHER ORDINANCES AND OTHER MUNICIPALITIES. IN MIAMI-DADE CITY CODE, 11.5-34-B, ORANGE COUNTY CODE 2-195F, KEY WEST, 3.097. ALL OF THEM SAY THE CRB IS THE BODY THAT RETAINS THE ATTORNEY. FOR IT TO BE TRULY INDEPENDENT BY THE PUBLIC YOU CAN'T HAVE AN ATTORNEY ASSIGNED TO IT BY SOMEBODY ELSE. THEY NEED TO BE ABLE TO RETAIN THEIR OWN ATTORNEY WHO CAN ACT INDEPENDENTLY AND ASK THAT THAT MODIFICATION BE MADE TO THE LANGUAGE. THE OTHER ONE -- GOING BACK TO THE ELMO. SAYS TO SERVE AS THE LEGAL ADVISOR FOR THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT. THE LANGUAGE IS UNNECESSARY. ONLY ONE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD. YOU DON'T HAVE TO SPECIFY THE ONE FOR THE POLICE DEPARTMENT. AND IT IS -- AND IT IS CONFUSING TOO, BECAUSE COULD BE READ TO SERVICE THE LEGAL ADVISOR FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT TO THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD. THAT LANGUAGE DOESN'T NEED TO BE THERE AND I ASK THAT SOMEBODY MOVE TO STRIKE IT. OTHER THAN THAT, I WAS STRUCK BY THE WORD OF THE INVOCATION THIS MORNING WHEN HE SAID THAT YOU SHOULD BE GUIDED BY WHAT IS BEST FOR THE CITIZENS. AND NOT WHAT IS BEST FOR YOUR NETWORKS. I WANT TO REMIND YOU -- AND WE SENT THIS TO YOU SEVERAL TIMES, 68% OF TAMPA VOTERS WANT TO -- WANT THERE TO BE AN INDEPENDENT ATTORNEY FOR THE CRB. 68% OF THE VOTERS. DON'T POSITION YOURSELF IN BETWEEN THE WILL OF THE WILL OF 68% OF THE VOTERS AND THE RIGHT TO VOTE ON THIS ISSUE. I NOTICE TODAY THERE IS NOT A PHALANX OF PAID POLICE OFFICERS BEHIND ME AND THEY ARE OKAY WITH THE CIVIL RIGHTS ACTIVISTS TO HAVE THIS ONE. NOT ANYTHING RADICAL AND ALREADY IN MIAMI, ORANGE COUNTY AND KEY WEST. I ASK THAT YOU ALLOW THE VOTERS TO VOTE ON THIS AND NOT POSITION YOURSELVES IN BETWEEN THE VOTERS IN SOMETHING THAT THE VOTERS WANT TO VOTE ON EVEN THOUGH 68% OF VOTERS FAVOR THIS PRO VICTIMS, 82% FAVOR VOTING ON IT. SO EVEN THOSE WHO ARE AGAINST IT FAVOR VOTING ON IT. FAVOR PUT IT TO A VOTE AND SAY I MIGHT NOT BE PERSONALLY IN FAVOR OF THIS, BUT IF EVERYBODY ELSE IS, I AM NOT GOING TO STAND IN THE WAY. I ASK YOU TO BE LIKE THE VOTERS. I WILL BE WATCHING AND THE VOTERS WILL BE WATCHING LAND NOTICE IF YOU DON'T LET US VOTE ON THIS.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU. LADIES AND GENTLEMEN, IF I SEE NO ONE ELSE STANDING TO SPEAK FOR PUBLIC COMMENT. I WOULD ASSUME THAT BISHOP PATTY IS THE LAST PERSON SPEAKING? I BELIEVE YOU ARE HANDING OUT SOMETHING FROM BISHOP PATTY?

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: BISHOP PATTY, PLEASE PROCEED. YOU AS A TAXPAYER. I COME TO ASK THIS CITY COUNCIL TO MAKE A MOTION TO WITHHOLD THE $100,000 THAT YOU HAVE ALLOTTED FOR THE NAACP. REASON BEING, FOR THE PAST THREE YEARS, THERE HAS NOT BEEN AN AUDIT OF THE NAACP. PRESIDENT YVETTE LEWIS, BE THERE, HAVE VIOLATED THE BYLAWS OF THE NAACP. THEY ARE SUPPOSED TO HAVE AN AUDIT EVERY YEAR. THAT HAS NOT BEEN DONE. NOW YOU THE COUNCIL WANTS TO GIVE $100,000 TO AN ORGANIZATION WHERE THERE IS NO TRANSPARENCY AND NO ACCOUNTABILITY. WHAT I PASSED OUT TO YOU A LETTER FROM TOBA. A TAMPA ORGANIZATION OF TAMPA BLACK BUSINESS AFFAIRS. AND THEY HAVE BEEN A PART OF POLITICAL PROCESS WITH THE NAACP FOR MANY YEARS. AS YOU SEE FROM THEIR LETTER THEY WITHDRAW THE SPONSORSHIP OF THIS ORGANIZATION BECAUSE NO TRANSPARENCY AND NO ACCOUNTABILITY OF FUNDS. THEY ARE ASKING -- AND I AM ASKING AS A TAXPAYER THAT YOU ALL DO YOUR DUE DILIGENCE -- I AM OFTEN HEARING THE WORD "TRANSPARENCY," "ACCOUNTABILITY" SO $100,000 TO AN ORGANIZATION THAT FEEL LIKE THEY ARE ABOVE THE LAW FEEL THAT THEY DO NOT HAVE TO ADHERE TO ACCOUNTABILITY. FEEL LIKE THEY DON'T HAVE TO ADHERE TO THE RULES, THEN NO MONIES SHALL BE GIVEN UNTIL YOU ALL CAN SEE WHAT HAS TRANSPIRED WITH MONIES THAT HAVE ALREADY BEEN PUT IN PLACE. THERE ARE NO SCHOLARSHIPS GIVEN TO CHILDREN AS OF LATE. THERE ARE NO BUSINESSES THAT THEY CAN POINT TO. SO ALL THIS WILL COME OUT IN AN AUDIT. AND THE AUDIT NEEDS TO BE ASKED BY IN COUNCIL. BECAUSE WE, THE VOTERS, VOTED FOR YOU TO ENSURE THAT OUR FUNDS ARE BEING LOOKED AFTER AND NOT JUST SQUANDERED AWAY. AS THE PRAYER WAS, YOU DO WHAT IS IN THE BEST INTEREST OF THE CITIZENS AND NOT WHAT YOU ARE THINKING IS GOING TO HELP PROPEL YOU INTO YOUR NEXT ARENA AS A POLITICAL FIGURE. SO I AM HOPING THAT EACH ONE YOU WILL DO YOUR DUE DILIGENCE. THAT YOU WILL DO WHAT IS RIGHT. THAT YOU DO RIGHT BY THE PEOPLE OF THE PEOPLE OF THE CITY OF TAMPA WHO IS COUNTING ON YOU NOT TO SQUANDER $100,000 TO AN ORGANIZATION THAT WILL NOT ADHERE TO TRANSPARENCY AND ACCOUNTABILITY. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU. GOOD MORNING. FOR THOSE PEOPLE WHO SAY GOOD MORNING. UHURU, THAT IS FREEDOM IN SWAHILI. WE, AS AFRICANS, NEED TO BE THINKING OF OUR FREEDOM. BAFOON, A CLOWN. THE CLASS CLOWN. A STRAIGHT BAFOON. NOTHING MORE. NOTHING LESS. WHEN IN THIS WORLD DO YOU HEAR AFRICAN PEOPLE HATING ON AFRICAN PEOPLE. AND AFRICAN PERSON HATING A CHLOE CONEY. AN AFRICAN PERSON HATING ON ORLANDO GUDES WITH ALL THE DISCREPANCIES. AN AFRICAN PERSON HATING ON TAMPA SHAUMBURGER WHEN THEY CAN'T CONTROL THE SITUATION. A BAFOON, NOTHING MORE, NOTHING LESS. NOT REPRESENTATIVE OF ANYTHING. AND TO TELL ALL THE WHITE PEOPLE IN THIS CITY, IF YOU GO ON MICHELLE PATTY'S SHOW, ON THAT SATURDAY MORNING, THAT CLOWN CENTRAL, THAT IS WHAT IT IS. WITH JOE ROBINSON, CLOWN CENTRAL, ALWAYS TALKING OF PUTTING THE FBI AND POLICE ON PEOPLE. A BLACK PERSON. ANN, A NEGRO, WHERE IN THIS WORLD DO YOU EVER HEAR NEGROES TALKING ABOUT CALLING THE POLICE ON PEOPLE. WE KNOW WHAT THE POLICE TO DO US! BUT THESE CLOWNS, IF THEY CAN'T HAVE IT THEIR WAY, THEY ARE LIKE GOSSIPERS. THEY ARE GARBAGE. NOTHING MORE, NOTHING LESS. STRAIGHT GARBAGE OPERATING IN THE HOOD. WHERE IN THE HOOD CAN YOU GO. ASK REAL AFRICANS IN HERE. WHERE IN THE HOOD CAN YOU TALK OF CALLING THE POLICE ON SOMEBODY. IT IS CALLED SNITCHING. AND BLACK PEOPLE DON'T DO IT. EVEN WHEN OUR FAMILY MEMBERS GET KILLED. BLACK PEOPLE DON'T DO IT. DON'T TELL. AM I RIGHT OR AM I WRONG? WE DON'T DO IT. A CLOWN! CLOWN CENTRAL. Y'ALL ARE ALLOWING IT TO HAPPEN. DON'T GO ON HER SHOW ANYMORE BECAUSE REPRESENTATIVE OF THE HATRED YOU HAVE FOR AFRICAN PEOPLE. NOTHING MORE. NOTHING LESS. BEING USED AS A BAFOON AND A CLOWN. THE OLDEST CIVIL RIGHTS ORGANIZATION IN THE HISTORY OF THE WORLD TRYING TO TAKE SOMETHING AWAY FROM THEM. THIS CITY OWE US TRILLIONS OF DOLLARS IN REPARATIONS AND WE AIN'T GOT THAT! AND A CLOWN DOWN HERE. A SHE-MALE CLOWN TALKING ABOUT $100,000! THE CITY OWES US THAT A HUNDRED TIMES OVER. THEY OWE US AFRICAN PEOPLE THAT A HUNDRED TIMES OVER. WE ARE 26% OF THE POPULATION. STOP COMING DOWN HERE AND TALKING GARBAGE! STOP COMING DOWN HERE TALKING OF SPEED BUMPS AND MONEY FOR PARKS AND JUNETEENTH. THAT IS GARBAGE. THAT IS BEGGING. NOW COMING DOWN WITH THAT CONFUSION. BRINGING THAT CONFUSION OFF YOUR RADIO SHOW RIGHT DOWN TO CITY COUNCIL. IT IS NOT GOING TO HAPPEN. NOT IN THIS CITY. NOT IN THIS CITY, IT IS NOT GOING TO HAPPEN. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU. DISRESPECTFUL SELF. ROBIN LOCKETT. HERE TO TALK ABOUT THE BILL OF RIGHTS. I GOTTEN SEVERAL -- TENANT BILL OF RIGHTS. I GOTTEN SEVERAL CALLS FROM COMMUNITY PEOPLE INDICATING THAT FOR ONE WHOLE APARTMENT COMPLEX -- A LOT OF PEOPLE IN AN APARTMENT COMPLEX, RATHER, THAT THIS APARTMENT COMPLEX IS NO LONGER ACCEPTING VOUCHERS, SECTION 8. GOT ANOTHER CALL FROM SEVERAL PEOPLE STATING THAT THEIR LANDLORD WILL NO LONGER ACCEPT R-3 MONEY -- R-3 PROGRAM, ASSISTANCE. THEY WANT THE TENANTS TO PAY THE MONEY OUTRIGHT. ISN'T THE TENANT PILL OF RIGHTS SUPPOSED TO PROTECT THEM FROM THAT? WHAT IS THE ENFORCEMENT ON IT. HOW DO WE ENFORCE IT. WE PUT ORDINANCES IN PLACE BUT NO ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM. THAT IS WHAT IS NEEDED. SO WE CAN PUT A LOT OF STUFF IN WRITING SAYING, HEY, AND THE BIGGEST CONVERSATION TO DATE HAS BEEN GETTING INFORMATION OUT TO THE COMMUNITY. HOW MANY OF THOSE APARTMENT COMPLEXES KNOW THAT THIS IS -- THIS IS, IN FACT, AN ORDINANCE, SOMETHING THEY SHOULD BE ABIDING BY. HOW DO YOU GET THAT INFORMATION OUT? SO IT IS A REAL PROBLEM WITH PUTTING THINGS IN WRITING WITH NO -- NO ABILITY TO ENFORCE IT. IT MAKES IT A WORTHLESS DOCUMENT.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: MR. CHAIRMAN.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN GUDES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: IS MISS TRAVIS STILL HERE. SHE IS THERE? I HATE TO PUT YOU ON THE SPOT.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: HI, MISS LOCKETT. [LAUGHTER]

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: YOU HEARD MISS LOCKETT'S QUESTIONS?

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: YEP.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: DO WE HAVE A RESPONSE FOR HER. I KNOW YOU WILL BE TRUTHFUL TO US. THAT IS WHY I CALLED YOU UP.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: THE TENANT BILL OF RIGHTS IS AN ORDINANCE AND YOUR POWERS ARE LIMITED IN WHAT YOU CAN DO. IT IS COMPLAINT-DRIVEN, NUMBER ONE. SO PEOPLE HAVE TO FILE A COMPLAINT. CODE ENFORCEMENT GOES OUT AND YOU GET A FINE. AND THE MOST WE CAN FINE THE LANDLORD IS $450. WHAT IS WRITTEN IN THE TENANT BILL OF RIGHTS IS HONESTLY THE MOST THAT YOU CAN DO AS A LOCAL MUNICIPALITY. WE DON'T HAVE THE POWER -- WE DON'T HAVE THE AUTHORITY TO DO MORE THAN THAT. BUT IT IS COMPLAINT-DRIVEN. SO IF THERE ARE COMPLAINTS, LIKE SOME OF THE ONES THAT MISS LOCKETT BROUGHT UP, THEY NEED TO CONTACT CODE ENFORCEMENT, FILE THE COMPLAINT -- WE HAVE HAD SOME COME THROUGH. IT IS COMPLAINT-DRIVEN AND WE WILL FOLLOW UP AND INVESTIGATE. BUT TO THINK THAT THE TENANT BILL OF RIGHTS IS GOING TO SOLVE IT ALL, IT'S NOT.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES

segments.text: YES. ACTUALLY OUR ATTORNEY, REBECCA JOHNS, HAS DONE TWO SEMINARS WITH THE LANDLORDS AND THE RENTAL ASSOCIATIONS. AND THEY HAVE PROVIDED INFORMATION TO THEM. SO WE WENT THERE, GIVEN THEM THE INFORMATION. SO, YES, WE HAVE PROVIDED THAT INFORMATION.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: NOT TO THE COMPLEX, HE WAS ASKING OF THE ASSOCIATIONS.

segments.text: I GUESS THERE ARE TWO PARTS OF THAT. IS IT POSSIBLE THAT MAYBE THE HOUSING DEPARTMENT OR -- CAN CREATE SOME TYPE OF PALM CARD OR CARD THAT CAN BE TAKEN WITH CODE ENFORCEMENT TO STOP BY THESE APARTMENT COMPLEXES AND MAKE SURE THE APARTMENT COMPLEXES. I AM SURE THE -- MR. VIERA'S DISTRICT BUT EVEN CALLING ME, PEBBLE WOOD. THEY KNOW WHAT THE RULES ARE AND WHAT -- WHAT THE ORDINANCES ARE. IS THAT POSSIBLE TO DO SOMETHING LIKE THAT?

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: YES, ABSOLUTELY. IT IS ALWAYS POSSIBLE FOR US TO CREATE DIFFERENT WAYS TO GET THE INFORMATION OUT TO THE PUBLIC. WE CAN DEFINITELY GO AND CREATE A FLIER. segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: I SEE WHAT YOU ARE SAYING. ONCE A COMPLAINT IS MADE, ONCE CODE ENFORCEMENT COMES OUT -- LITERATURE TO GIVE TO THE APARTMENT MANAGER IS WHAT YOU ARE SAYING.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: OR JUST -- IT DOESN'T HAVE TO BE A COMPLAINT. ON THEIR REGULAR DUTIES AS THEY ARE DRIVING AROUND AND HAVE AN APARTMENT -- AN APARTMENT COMPLEX IN THEIR AREA TO HAND IT AND -- GIVE THAT TO THEM SO THEY WILL KNOW. MAYBE SOMETIMES THE APARTMENT PEOPLE ARE OUT OF STATE AND SOMEBODY IN THE OFFICE WHO ARE RUN IT AND THEY DON'T KNOW FOR THEMSELVES. TO HAVE SOMETHING TO KNOCK ON THE DOOR OF THE APARTMENT COMPLEX MANAGER. HERE IS THE ORDINANCE. SO THEY WILL BE INFORMED.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: WE WILL WORK ON THAT. SO THE CODE ENFORCEMENT CAN GO TO AN APARTMENT COMPLEX MORE THAN FOUR UNITS?

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: YES. THEY CAN GO. BUT IN ORDER FOR THEM TO -- IN ORDER FOR CODE ENFORCEMENT TO GO INTO AN APARTMENT, SOMEONE HAS TO INVITE THEM INTO THE APARTMENT. SO IF A TENANT IS IN THE APARTMENT AND THEY HAVE A COMPLAINT ABOUT THEIR UNIT BEING SUBSTANDARD, THEY HAVE TO INVITE CODE ENFORCEMENT IN. CODE ENFORCEMENT WILL DOCUMENT IT. segments.text: THANK YOU. I JUST WANT TO SAY THE REASON WE DON'T TELL IS WE KNOW WE DON'T RECEIVE JUSTICE. WE DON'T GET JUSTICE HERE. WE DON'T GET JUSTICE IN THE COURTHOUSE. SO THE COMMUNITY HAS TRIED TO FIGURE OUT HOW TO SOLVE OUR OWN ISSUES. ONE OF THE BIGGEST PROBLEMS THAT I WANT TO SPEAK TO QUICKLY IS THAT THE CITY SHOULD NOT BE THE ONE THAT CONTROLS THE LEGAL COUNSEL OF AN INDONESIA PENITENTIARY COUNSEL. THE CITY AND THE MAYOR MADE A DETERMINATION EVEN WHEN CHOOSING WHO THE NAACP WOULD SEND TO REPRESENT THE INTEREST OF OTHER COMMUNITIES. SHE WOULD HAVE A HAND IN THAT AS WELL SO NO INDEPENDENT AROUND -- INDEPENDENCE AROUND THE BOARD OR THIS COMMUNITY. THE VERY LEAST THIS COUNCIL CAN DO IS LIFT YOURSELF UP WITH REAL TRANSPARENCY AND SAY THAT THE CITIZENS OF THIS COMMUNITY SHOULD HAVE A RIGHT TO UP OR DOWN THAT VOTE AND THAT COUNSEL SHOULD BE INDEPENDENTLY CHOSEN BY THE CITIZEN REVIEW BOARD. NOTHING ON YOUR AGENDA TODAY SPOKE TO THE NAACP ISSUE. I WOULD HAVE RESPECTED THE ADMINISTRATION THAT IF YOU DIDN'T WANT TO GIVE THE NAACP ANY MONEY, DON'T GIVE IT TO THEM. FOR SO LONG THE NAACP HAS BEEN ABLE TO WORK WITH A LIMITED BUDGET NOW WHAT WE SEE THAT THE ADMINISTRATION HAS HIRED OUTSIDE SOURCES TO DO THEIR WORK. IT'S AWFUL. IT IS HYPOCRITICAL. THE COMMUNITY IS SUFFERING. WHETHER IT IS AROUND THE ISSUE OF NOT HAVING AFFORDABLE HOUSING IN EAST TAMPA. ALL WE SEE IN EAST TAMPA IS A TEMPERATURE IN OF HOUSES THAT WE WILL NEVER BE ABLE TO PURCHASE. $300,000, $450,000 NEXT TO HOUSES THAT NEED, WHAT? REAL REHAB. AND WE CAN'T GET THAT DONE. FOR THE LAST FIVE YEARS, WE HAVE BEEN TRYING TO PUSH A REHAB BILL THAT IS ALMOST LIKE A SHELL GAME. IT IS NOT BEING DONE. SO WHAT I AM BELIEVING NOW IS AN UNDERMINING ATTEMPT TO REMOVE ALL POOR PEOPLE OUT OF EAST TAMPA. BECAUSE IT IS THE CLOSEST THEY CAN BE TO YBOR, THE RIVERWALK, FRIENDS OF THE RIVER, WHATEVER YOU WANT TO CALL IT, WE ARE NOT ENJOYING ANY OF THE BENEFIT. THE PERSON WHO HAS HAD THE SHORTEST MEMBERSHIP OF THE NAACP RECEIVED A SUSPENSION FROM THE NATIONAL. YOU KNOW WHY? BECAUSE THE NATIONAL IS NOT GOING TO ALLOW A MICHELLE PATTY TO CONTROL THE NAACP. THAT IS REAL. SO HOPEFULLY YOU DON'T GET CAUGHT UP IN IT AND LET THE NAACP AND ITS LEGAL COUNSEL DEAL WITH THE MICHELLE PATTYS OF THIS WORLD. THANK YOU.

segments.text: THANK YOU.

FIRST OF ALL, I AM JOE ROBINSON, AND I AM NOT A CONVICTED FELON. DO NOT LET CONVICTED FELONS SIT HERE AND TELL YOU WHAT TAXPAYERS WHO PAY THEIR TAXES WANT OR DON'T WANT. I AM NOT A CONVICTED FELON. NOW LET'S GET TO WHY I'M HERE. I'M HERE BECAUSE I AM ASK CITY COUNCIL MAKE A MOTION THAT TAMPA CITY COUNCIL WILL REQUEST FOR A FORENSIC AUDIT OF THE NAACP, HILLSBOROUGH COUNTY BRANCH FINANCES AND BE PROVIDED TO THEM BY THE NAACP, HILLSBOROUGH COUNTY BRANCH AT THE BRANCH'S EXPENSE. AND SAMPLE CITY COUNCIL SHOULD REQUEST AN AUDIT BE PERFORMED OF THE CITY AUDITORS OF FUNDS GIVEN TO THE NAACP HILLSBOROUGH COUNTY BRANCH OF THE LAST THREE YEARS OF THE 2023 BUDGET OF $100,000 BE RELEASED TO THE HILLSBOROUGH COUNTY BRANCH. THE REASON ABOVE REQUEST FOR THE CITY COUNCIL FROM THE FINANCES OF THE NAACP BRANCH AND THAT IT BE AN AUDIT FOR THE LAST THREE YEARS IS BECAUSE THE TAMPA CITY COUNCIL'S POSSIBILITY FOR TRANSPARENCY AND ACCOUNTABILITY, MR. CARSON, FOR TAXPAYER DOLLARS BEING BUDGETED, RECEIVED, AND SPENT BY NONPROFIT PUBLIC CHARITY ENTITIES WITH FUNDS COME FROM THE CITY'S BUDGET. THE ATTACHED PRESS RELEASE DATED NOVEMBER 16 THAT YOU ALL HAVE AND ASKED TO BE RECEIVED AND FILED GIVE MORE REASONS WHY THIS SHOULD BE CONDUCTED. IF YOU LOOK AT THE PAGE -- THE SECOND PAGE OF THE AUDIT'S REQUEST IN YELLOW. IN YELLOW. IN YELLOW. IN YELLOW. IT SAYS, QUOTE, WE ARE NOT ENGAGED TO AND DID NOT CONDUCT AN AUDIT FOR REVIEWING ENGAGEMENT THAT IS EXPRESSION OF AN OPINION OR CONCLUSION. NO AUDIT DONE. REVIEW IN ITS ISSUES, AND I TAKE OFFENSE IS EVERYBODY TALKING OF JOE ROBINSON AND THE FBI. NOW THE FBI WILL BE INVOKED NOW BY SAYING WHAT YOU SAID, YOU ACCIDENTALLY COME TO INVESTIGATE. PLEASE DO NOT BE PART OF PUBLIC CORRUPTION OR AIDING AND ABETTING IN THE EFFORT BY THE NAACP. NOT THE ORGANIZATION, THE PUBLIC CORRUPT INDIVIDUALS.

FIRST OF ALL, ESTUARIES PROVIDE A NURSERY AND HABITAT FOR IMPORTANT FISH AND SHELLFISH SPECIES. COMMON SENSE TO SEE THAT STOPPING THE DISCHARGE OF A LARGE AMOUNT OF WATER FLOW INTO TAMPA BAY THAT HAS BEEN OCCURRING FOR MANY, MANY YEARS TO CRITICALLY HARM OUR ESTUARY SYSTEM. A DISTURBING PART OF SENATE BILL 64 IS THAT IT DOESN'T EVEN ADDRESS ESTUARINE BODIES OF WATER THAT ARE IMPACTED. NEW LEGISLATION MUST BE PROPOSED TO ADDRESS THESE MISSING ELEMENTS. ALSO ITEM TWO, WE LIVE IN FLORIDA. EXCUSE ME. ITEM 7, BUT ANOTHER POINT IS THAT WE LIVE IN FLORIDA AND WE ALL KNOW A LARGE FLUCTUATION OF RAINFALL THROUGHOUT THE YEAR CREATING VARIATIONS IN OUR HYDROLOGIC MISSIONS. AS WELL, WE ALSO KNOW OUR WATER QUALITY FLUCTUATES DEPENDING ON MANY FACTORS BOTH INDUSTRY AND ENVIRONMENTAL WITH A AMOUNT OF YOU HAVE FLUENT TO BE REPURPOSED, AND CONDITIONS SUCH AS SUMMER RAINS BLESS THE MILLIONS OF GALLONS OF WATER MAY OVERWHELMING A SURFACE WATER SYSTEM THAT ASSISTS THAT EXTRA WATER. IN COMMON SENSE TO ALLOW FOR VARIATIONS IN QUANTITY FEES OF EFFLUENT DISCHARGE TO BE REROUTED OR REUSED. AND I AM ASKING YOU TO PLEASE SUPPORT LEGISLATION THAT INCLUDES THESE RECOMMENDATIONS IN YOUR 2023 LEGISLATIVE PRIORITIES. AND THANK YOU FOR YOUR TIME.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: THANK YOU, SIR. NEXT ITEM I HAVE FOR YOU -- THANK YOU, GUYS. THANK YOU. THE NEXT ITEM I HAVE FOR YOU IS A WALK-ON ITEM. I HAVE SPOKEN TO EACH OF YOU ABOUT THIS ITEM. THIS IS A MEMORANDUM OF AGREEMENT BETWEEN BELMAR PARTNERS LLP AND THE CITY OF TAMPA. WHAT IT IS THAT WE ARE ASKING -- THIS PROPERTY HAS A HISTORIC STRUCTURE ON IT. IT IS A NATIONAL LANDMARK. IT IS NOT LOCALLY DESIGNATED. NOT IN A HISTORIC DISTRICTS HERE. BUT BECAUSE OF THAT, IN ORDER FOR THE PROPERTY TO BE REDEVELOPED, THERE HAS TO BE A NOTICING PERIOD FOR 30 DAYS THAT THE DEMOLITION OF THE HISTORIC STRUCTURE WILL TAKE PLACE AND THAT THE REDEVELOPMENT OF THAT PROPERTY WOULD BE 100 AFFORDABLE HOUSING UNITS. THIS CAME TO YOU BACK IN 2021 IN YOUR ACTION PLAN. IT RECENTLY CAME IN AN AGREEMENT SOME TIME OVER THE SUMMER. FOR YOU TO -- FOR THAT FUNDING AGREEMENT AND OUR PARTICIPATION IN THIS AFFORDABLE HOUSING PROJECT. SO I -- WE DON'T LIKE TO DO WALK-ONES UNLESS THEY ARE TIME SENSITIVE AND CONSIDERATION THAT THIS IS AFFORDABLE HOUSING PROJECT AND THE WEEK OF THANKSGIVING, WE FOUND OUT THE DATE OF THE CLOSING IN JANUARY. SO IN ORDER TO KEEP THAT CLOSING DATE, WE ARE RESPECTFULLY ASKING FOR YOUR APPROVAL OF THIS MEMORANDUM OF AGREEMENT SO WE CAN CONTINUE TO MOVE FORWARD WITH THIS REDEVELOPMENT PROJECT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH, MISS TRAVIS. AS MUCH AS I HATE DEMOLISHING ANYTHING OF HISTORIC SIGNIFICANCE, I DRIVE BY THIS. THIS HAS -- THIS WILL NOT AFFECT MY DECISION. I DRIVE BY THIS EVERY DAY WHEN I TAKE MY STEPDAUGHTER TO SCHOOL. ONE OF THOSE MIDCENTURY STRUCTURES. I EVEN PHOTOGRAPHED IT AND PUT IT ON SOCIAL MEDIA OF ALL THE CHURCHES IN THE AREA. A HEAVILY POPULATED AREA; HOWEVER, I DON'T KNOW HOW THE -- HOW THE -- THE STORY OF THE CHURCH, THE CONGREGATION, HOW LONG IT HAS BEEN OPERABLE, WHATEVER. BUT THE BRIGHT SIDE IS -- AGAIN, YOU KNOW, I AM ABOUT HISTORIC PRESERVATION, BUT THE BRIGHT SIDE IS 100 -- EVEN THOUGH WE NEED 10,000 OR MORE AFFORDABLE UNITS, THIS MOVES US FORWARD TOWARD THAT GOAL OF PROVIDING AFFORDABLE HOUSING IN AN AREA THAT IS VERY DESIRABLE. IN A GOOD PART OF TOWN. SO, YOU KNOW, I UNDERSTAND THE TRADE-OFF HERE AS MUCH AS. AS I AM HESITANT AND I HATE TO SEE ANYTHING DEMOLISHED BUT FOR A GOOD PUBLIC PURPOSE. AND SEVERE NECESSITY THAT WE OBVIOUSLY NEED WITH AFFORDABLE HOUSING.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: TO YOUR POINT, I AM A PRESERVATIONIST AT HEART. SO THE MEMORANDUM OF AGREEMENT HAS STIPULATIONS IN IT THAT REQUIRES BEFORE BELMAR IS ABLE TO DEMOLISH THE PROPERTY, THEY HAVE TO DOCUMENT, ARCHIVE, TAKE PICTURES. THE HISTORIC PRESERVATION -- THE STATE HISTORIC PRESERVATION OFFICE HAVE STRICT REQUIREMENTS IN DOCUMENTING HISTORICAL STRUCTURES. I HAVE BEEN THROUGH THAT SEVERAL TIMES ON OTHER PROJECTS, AND IT IS VERY DETAILED. YOU HAVE TO HAVE A SPECIFIC PHOTOGRAPHER TYPE OF -- TYPE OF CAMERA TO DOCUMENT THE HISTORY OF THE CHURCH BEFORE IT IS ULTIMATELY DEMOLISHED. THAT IS OUTLINED -- THOSE STIPULATIONS ARE OUTLINED AND HAVE TO BE COMPLETED BEFORE THEY CAN DEMO THE PROPERTY. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: ANY OTHER COMMENTS OR HE IS QUESTIONS? FROM THE PUBLIC?

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: PLEASE COME TO THE FRONT AND ASK YOUR QUESTION SO IT CAN BE HEARD FOR THE RECORD. MR. SHELBY.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: YES, SIR. IF THIS IS GOING TO BE SOMETHING THAT THE COUNCIL IS GOING TO BE VOTING ON, THAT IS IS A WALK-ON. WAS IT ON THE ADDENDUM. IT WAS ON THE ADDENDUM, SO THE PUBLIC DID HAVE HAVE AN OPPORTUNITY -- WAS IT AVAILABLE?

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: LET HIM SPEAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: SECOND.

segments.text: AT THIS TIME WE WILL TAKE PUBLIC COMMENT CONCERNING THE WALK-ON AGENDA ITEM. THIS FEELS LIKE THIS IS BEING SLIPPED UNDER THE DOOR A LITTLE BIT TO ME. I AM NOT AWARE OF THE SITUATION. BUT IT IS A HISTORIC PROPERTY, I ASSUME OR -- OR THERE MAY BE TREES ON THIS PROPERTY THAT MAY NEED TO BE TAKEN INTO CONSIDERATION. THERE IS AN AWFUL LOT OF QUESTIONS THAT THE PUBLIC WOULD HAVE BEFORE WE ACTUALLY JUST PRESS AHEAD AND SAY, NO, I DIDN'T LIKE THE LOOKS OF THAT, SO LET'S TAKE IT DOWN. IF THIS DEEMS TO BE IMPORTANT ENOUGH TO HAVE SOMEBODY COME INSIDE AND ARCHIVE IT FOR HISTORY, PERHAPS THERE IS SOME HISTORY THAT NEEDS TO BE PRESERVED BY KEEPING THIS BUILDING OR CONSIDERING -- AT LEAST PUT INTO CONSIDERATION SO WE -- SO WE CAN ACTUALLY GIVE IT SOME REAL GOOD THOUGHT AND SOME PUBLIC SCRUTINY. THAT IS ALL I ASK.

segments.text: MISS TRAVIS.

segments.text: YES, COUNCIL. THANK YOU. I AM GOING TO PUT ON THE ELMO AN AERIAL OF THE PROPERTY. THIS IS SIMPLY THE MEMORANDUM OF AGREEMENT THAT REQUIRES THE DEVELOPER TO TAKE PICTURES OF THE HISTORIC STRUCTURE PRIOR TO THE DEMOLITION. IT HAS ALREADY BEEN APPROVED AS A DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT. IN YOUR ACTION PLAN, YOU HAVE APPROVED THE DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT ALREADY. THIS IS JUST A PROCEDURAL OF THE DOCUMENTATION AS WE DON'T WANT TO JUST -- THE STATE IS ASKING US TO DO THIS, TO DOCUMENT THE BUILDING OTHERWISE THE BUILDING WILL BE DEMOLISHED WITHOUT ANY PHOTOGRAPH. SO THERE IS NO SLIPPING ANYTHING THROUGH.

segments.text: GOOD MORNING, COUNCIL, ADRIANA COLINA DIRECTOR OF LOGISTICS AND ASSETS MANAGEMENT. TO EXTEND THE CONTRACT WITH MANCON OF OUR FLEET DIVISION. I PROVIDED YOU WITH A DETAILED MEMO OF OUR REQUEST AND SPOKEN TO EACH OF YOU INDIVIDUALLY. BUT I WANTED TO MAKE MYSELF AVAILABLE TO YOU TODAY TO ANSWER ANY QUESTIONS THAT YOU HAVE. SIMPLY PUT, IT IS EXTENDING THE CONTRACT. A CONTRACT THAT ORIGINATED IN 2014. IT WAS A FIVE-YEAR CONTRACT WITH FIVE ALLOWABLE ONE-YEAR EXTENSIONS. I AM ASKING FOR YOU TODAY TO APPROVE THE SECOND TO THE LAST EXTENSION AND ALSO ASKING FOR AN ALLOWABLE 8.5% CIP INCREASE WHICH IS ALLOWED IN THE CONTRACT AND RIGHT SIZE IT TO ADD ONE CUSTOMER SERVICE PERSON IN OUR FLEET, MAINTENANCE AREA THAT SERVICES OUR FIRE TEAM.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO

segments.speaker: BRAD BAIRD
segments.text: GOOD MORNING, COUNCIL. BRAD BAIRD, DEPUTY ADMINISTRATOR OF INFRASTRUCTURE. HERE TO REQUEST APPROVAL FOR FOUR CONTRACTS ASSOCIATEDED WITH CITYWIDE HYDRANT AND VALVE INSTALLATION AND REPLACEMENT TO THE TUNE OF $6.5 MILLION EACH FOR A TOTAL OF $26 MILLION. I VERY MUCH APPRECIATE COUNCILMAN GUDES ASKING ME TO SPEAK BRIEFLY ON THIS, BECAUSE, AS YOU KNOW, THE ADMINISTRATION HAS PUT A FOCUS ON DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND INCLUSION AND THE ENTIRE ADMINISTRATION HAS WORKED VERY HARD ON INCREASING EBO PARTICIPATION. AND THIS CONTRACT SHOWS THE FRUITS OF OUR LABOR. WE HAVE AS MUCH AS 26.1 PARTICIPATION ON ONE OF THE CONTRACTS AND THE OTHER CONTRACTS ARE VERY -- HAVE VERY GOOD PARTICIPATION AS WELL. SO I APPRECIATE YOUR SUPPORT ON IT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: FIRST, THANK YOU. AND I AM GLAD TO SEE THIS AND THE NUMBERS THAT YOU ARE JUST DISCUSSING. I AM GLAD COUNCILMAN GUDES BROUGHT THIS UP, BECAUSE THIS -- THIS IS EQUITY. A HAND-UP IN THE COMMUNITY AND LIFTING PEOPLE UP BECAUSE -- IS THIS PART OF PEER -- PIPES. NOT THE -- NOT THE 2016 STORMWATER, BUT MULTI BILLION DOLLAR PROGRAM. THERE IS MORE THAN ENOUGH MONEY TO GO AROUND TO HELP BENEFIT MINORITY GROUPS, PEOPLE IN THE COMMUNITY AND A PERFECT EXAMPLE. AND THIS IS NOT THE FIRST BECAUSE YOU HAVE BROUGHT UP CONTRACT AFTER CONTRACT WITH INCREASED AND IMPRESSIVE MINORITY PARTICIPATION NUMBERS. SO WE ARE VERY GRATEFUL FOR THAT. THANK YOU FOR LISTENING TO US, BECAUSE I KNOW THIS WAS BROUGHT UP BY THE COUNCIL. AND THESE ARE THE RESULTS THAT PEOPLE WANT TO SEE. SO THANK YOU, SIR.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I WANT TO ECHO COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO'S CONGRATULATIONS ON THIS. BUT I ALSO WANT TO HIGHLIGHT THE FACT THAT YOU ARE USING FOUR. WHEN WE TALKED ABOUT IN THE BRIEFING, AND YOU SAID THAT YOU DIDN'T THINK THAT YOU WOULD GET THIS MANY PEOPLE PARTICIPATING, BUT I LOVE IT. BECAUSE IT MEANS THAT WHEN SOMETHING HAPPENS IN THE MIDDLE OF THE NIGHT, WE HAVE NOT JUST ONE PERSON TO CALL BUT FOUR DIFFERENT ENTITIES. I THINK THAT IS REALLY WONDERFUL. YOU WORKED WITH ALL FOUR OF THOSE BEFORE SO WE ALREADY KNOW THE QUALITY OF THE WORK. I WANT TO SAY THANK YOU FOR NOT ONLY INCLUDING OR GETTING GREAT EBO NUMBERS BUT INCLUDING MORE ORGANIZATIONS IN THIS. I THINK THAT IS A REALLY EXCITING WAY TO GO FORWARD AND I WANT TO SAY CONGRATULATIONS.

segments.speaker: BRAD BAIRD
segments.text: THANK YOU.

segments.text: COUNCILMAN GUDES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: BRAD, WE WANT TO SAY THANK YOU. WE ARE WATCHING UP. I WAS INVITED TO A GALA LAST NIGHT OF DIVERSITY AND INCLUSION OF CONTRACTORS AND DEVELOPERS. THEY INVITED ME TO THIS GALA LAST NIGHT AT THE ITALIAN CLUB, ACTUALLY. AND I DON'T KNOW WHY HILLSBOROUGH WASN'T THERE, BUT ORANGE COUNTY CAME HERE. SOME OF THEIR ELECTED OFFICIALS. SOME OF THEIR CONTRACTORS. THEIR EBO OFFICE, OSCEOLA, PINELLAS. AS THEY WERE DOING THEIR SCHEMATIC AND TALKING ABOUT WHAT THEY WERE DOING, I MEAN, WE ARE CATCHING UP TO THEM, BUT STILL A LITTLE BIT BEHIND AND I AM GLAD THAT WE ARE MOVING. BUT I CAN TELL YOU ORANGE COUNTY, THEY HAVE THE BEST PLAN I HAVE EVER SEEN AND I WAS GOING TO TALK TO OCEA ABOUT THAT. MISS HURTAK WAS SAYING, THEY COMBINE A LOT OF DIFFERENT AGENCIES TO GET IT DONE. THEY COMBINE A LOT OF DIFFERENT AGENCIES TO GET IT DONE AND GET THOSE NUMBERS AND THEY DON'T -- AND -- ONCE ALL THE VERBIAGE, NOT THAT GOOD FAITH. IT IS SHALL. NOT GOOD FAITH. IT IS SHALL. AND PEOPLE KNOW WHEN THEY GO TO PUT IN THAT BID, THEY MAKE SURE THAT THEY HAVE WHAT IT TAKES TO DO IT. AND WHAT I LIKE ABOUT IT, THEY HAVE TRAINED TO MAKE SURE FOLKS -- I TALKED ABOUT IT, SOMETIMES SMALLER BUSINESSES, THEY DON'T KNOW THE LINGO AND LANGUAGE, BECAUSE THEY DON'T HAVE THE MONEY FOR THE BIG-TIME ATTORNEYS AND GET LEFT OFF ALL THE TIME. A TRAINING PROGRAM TO HAVE SMALL BUSINESSES COME IN TO LEARN HOW TO DO THE CONTRACTS, THE REMEMBER BADGE, THE LANGUAGE, HOW TO GET A PROCUREMENTS, WHAT THAT REALLY MEANS. THE BIGGEST THING THEY TALKED ABOUT AND I HAVE SAID THIS SINCE DAY ONE, SOMETIMES A CHALLENGE AND HAVE BEEN WORKING ON THAT AND A FEW OTHER COUNTIES WERE TALKING ABOUT THIS, THE BOND ISSUE. BECAUSE THE SMALL GUY CAN'T GET THE NICE BIG JOB BECAUSE HE CAN'T GET THE BOND. HE DOESN'T HAVE THE CAPITAL. SO THAT IS SOMETHING THAT I AM GOING TO PREACH FOR US TO WORK ON. HOW CAN WE FINDS A PROGRAM. I KNOW MR. MIRANDA HAS BEEN WORKING ON THIS -- TO DEAL WITH THAT SO WE CAN BE ABLE TO HELP THE SMALLER GUYS AND HAVE A MULTITUDE OF THOSE FOLKS TO GET WHAT I CALL NOT BE HUNGRY AND THIS VISIT AT THE TABLE. I AM GRATEFUL TO PUT THIS FOOT FORWARD AND I AM GLAD OF THE NUMBERS THAT ARE HAPPENING AND THE COMMUNITY IS HAPPY AND BY THE WAY, GENTLEMEN -- I DID GO BY -- I WAS INVITED TO THE HANNA PROJECT. I THINK ALL OF YOU WILL TAKE A TOUR OUT THERE. WE HAD A LOT OF CONTROVERSY. I KNOW SOME THINGS WASN'T DONE THE WAY THE WAY THIS BOARD THINKS IT SHOULD HAVE BEEN DONE. THAT IS JUST THE WAY IT IS, BUT I THINK THE COMPANY IS UNDERSTANDING AND TRYING TO GET THERE TO WHAT THIS COUNCIL WANTS. I SAW A LOT OF HBCU GRADUATES OUT THERE WORK ON THIS PROJECT IN MAJOR POSITIONS. AND I WAS IMPRESSED UPON THAT -- I AM A COOKMAN GUY, BUT A LOT OF HBCU WORKERS DOING -- WHAT I CALL WORK THAT I CAN'T DO, THAT YOU NEED TO HAVE EXPERTISE TO DO. I WAS HAPPY TO SEE THAT COMPANY, DPR, IS HEARING AND GOT -- AND HAVE SOME OF THOSE GUYS ON -- AFFORDABLE PROJECTS OUT THERE. TO SEE HOW IT IS BEING DEVELOPED AND TO COME BACK IN THE BACK END. MANCON. AND A WHOLE MINORITY FIRM. I'M HAPPY ABOUT THAT AND GLAD THAT THE ADMINISTRATION IS LISTENING AND WE NEED TO KEEP MOVING AND SEE WHAT OTHER PEOPLE ARE DOING SO MATCH EVERYBODY ELSE. AGAIN, THANK YOU FOR THE EFFORTS.

segments.text: COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.text: AID COUPLE OF CALLS OF ONE OF THE DEVELOPERS HAVING COFFEE AT WEST TAMPA SANDWICH SHOP AND LOOKING AT THE WORK BEING DONE. TO THEIR SURPRISE, WHOEVER IS THERE COME HERE, I WANT TO SHOW YOU, HOW THE PIPE GOES IN INCH AT A TIME THROUGH THE OTHER PIPE AND THEY WERE AMAZED THEY GOT A 20 TO 30-MINUTE SESSION LEARNING AND SEEING HOW A PIPE PUTTING A PIPE INSIDE A PIPE. AND THEY WERE JUST -- AT AWE THAT THESE THINGS CAN HAPPEN. I SAID THAT IS TECHNOLOGY, BABY. THAT'S ALL THAT IS. I APPRECIATE EVERYTHING. AND WHOEVER THE DEVELOPER WAS, COME THANK YOU ON BEHALF THE CITIZENS OF WEST TAMPA HAD THE TIME SHOW THEM WHAT THEY ARE DOING AND HOW THEY DO IT.

segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO.
