segments.text: A QUESTION TO COUNCILMAN GUDES BECAUSE WE CAN'T TALK PRIVATELY. YOU WENT TO THE HANNA PROJECT. WHAT DID YOU NOTICE WITH REGARDS TO -- WE HAVE THE APPRENTICESHIP ORDINANCE AND WHATNOT. WHAT DID YOU NOTICE AS FAR AS THE PARTICIPATION THERE.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: I DIDN'T GO TOO FAR THERE. THEY HAD FOLKS OUT THERE. I KNOW WE ARE WITH THE APPRENTICESHIP PROGRAM, GRATEFUL FOR THAT. I SEE GUYS WALKING DOWN THE STREET AND I WANT PEOPLE TO COME TO APPLY TO WORK. GET THEM ON THE SITE AND GET THEM WORKING AND THEN BE ABLE TO BE A PART OF THE STATE CERTIFICATION AND THOSE TYPE OF THINGS. I DON'T WANT A GUY WHO IS HUNGRY TO WAIT TO FIND A WAY TO GET CERTIFIED. IF WE GOT PEOPLE WHO NEED TO WORK, LET THEM WORK AND PUT THEM IN AN APPRENTICESHIP PROGRAM SO THAT WAY THEY CAN FEED THEIR FAMILIES AND I AM IMPRESSED SO THEY ARE STARTING TO GET FOLKS -- AND I TOLD THEM -- I DON'T KNOW THE CITY PORTION OR WHATEVER, BUT I TOLD THEM THAT PEOPLE ARE VISUAL LEARNERS SOMETIMES. WHEN THEY SEE THINGS, THEY BELIEVE THINGS. I TOLD THEM THEY NEED TO FILM THE PROJECT. FILM SOME OF THE HBCU GUYS DRAWING UP THE PLANS, DRAWING UP THE STUFF. SHOW PEOPLE WHO ARE PEOPLE FROM THE COMMUNITY, STRATEGISTS, WHATEVER THEY CALL THEM, THAT THEY HAVE THE COMPUTERS AND WORKING. THEY ARE ACTUALLY MAKING SURE THAT THAT PART IS SUPPOSED TO FIT WHERE IT IS SUPPOSED TO GO AND THINGS LIKE THAT. I WAS AMAZED WHAT I SAW. AND FILM THAT YOU HAVE PEOPLE FROM THAT COMMUNITY ON THE PROJECT WORKING. WHY ARE WE NOT DOING THAT TO SHOW WHAT IS HAPPENING AND A LOT OF THE SCUTTLE BUTT GOING ON. AND THE FACES TO SAY I KNOW HIM I TAUGHT HIM AT SCHOOL. WALKING DOWN THE STREET AND HE IS WORKING ON THE BIG PROJECT. WE CAN'T BE AFRAID TO SHOW THOSE TYPE OF THINGS AND WE HAD NEED TO HAVE  CCTV OUT THERE SHOWING THAT YOU HAVE ALL TYPES OF MINORITIES WORK ON THIS PROJECT AND WE ARE STARTING TO GIVE EVERYBODY AN OPPORTUNITY AND A PIECE OF THE PIE.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO

segments.speaker: BRAD BAIRD

BASICALLY A SUMMARY OF ACTIVE STORMWATER PROJECTS BEING DONE THAT COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO MENTIONED A FEW MINUTES AGO APPROVED IN 2015. [INAUDIBLE]

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MR. VIK BHIDE, ARE YOU THERE?

segments.speaker: VIK BHIDE
segments.text: YES, SIR, GOOD MORNING, COUNCIL. VIK BHIDE, DIRECTOR OF MOBILITY DEPARTMENT. SORRY ABOUT THAT. I'M HERE TO ADDRESS ANY QUESTIONS THAT YOU MAY HAVE ABOUT ITEM 5, A MEMO WITH THE QUARTERLY REPORT WAS SUBMITTED TO COUNCIL FOR REVIEW, AS WAS REQUIRED BY A MOTION A FEW YEARS BACK.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN GUDES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: GOOD MORNING, VIK. THE ONLY QUESTION I HAVE -- I WANT YOU TO BE ABLE TO FIX A PROBLEM THAT THERE IS. WHEN WE GO OUT AND TEAR UP THESE ROADS AND GO TO PUT THEM BACK, CAN WE MAKE SURE THE ROAD IS DRIVABLE? BECAUSE I CALLED YOU -- I WANTED TO FILE A LAWSUIT SAYING YOU FOR MY CADILLAC AND I RODE DOWN THAT FLORIDA AVENUE -- AND I ALMOST FOR THE CAR UP. I DON'T UNDERSTAND -- I DON'T KNOW WHAT THE PROCESS IS, BUT I DO HAVE A PROBLEM. WE GO AND TEAR UP A ROAD. AND WE DON'T FIX THE ROAD. I MEAN, WE JUST DO A -- A PATCH-UP JOB OF A ROAD. I HAVE A PROBLEM WITH THAT. SO I DON'T KNOW IF THAT NEEDS TO BE INCORPORATED IN THE DOLLARS AND CENTS WHEN YOU TEAR UP THE ROAD THAT THE ROAD IS DRIVABLE SO PEOPLE DON'T TEAR THEIR CARS UP. A SERIOUS PROBLEM AND ANY TIME WE GO DOWN THESE PLACES AND GO AND DO GREAT WORK ON THE UNDERGROUND, BUT THE TOP LAYER -- I HAVE A PROBLEM WITH THAT. I THINK WE -- WE GOT TO ADDRESS THAT. WE NEED TO MAKE SURE THAT THE ROAD IS PROPERLY PAVED AND DONE RIGHT. HAS TO BE INCLUDED IN THE PROJECT, BUT I AM JUST SORRY, WE HAVE TO FIX THAT PROBLEM.

segments.speaker: VIK BHIDE
segments.text: COUNCILMAN, I UNDERSTAND AND I REMEMBER THE CONCERN YOU HAD CITED ON FLORIDA AVENUE REGARDING THAT. THE CHALLENGE THERE WAS THAT WITH -- SO FIRST TO CLARIFY, WITH EVERY STORMWATER PROJECT, IF WE DO TEAR UP A ROAD, IT IS IN THE SCOPE TO PUT IT BACK AND RESURFACE IT. USUALLY THE ROAD IS OPENED UP FOR LAYING PIPE AND SO ON, WHICH WAS THE CASE ON FLORIDA. SOMETIMES WHAT HAPPENS IS THERE IS AN ADDITIONAL WORK THAT NEEDS TO BE DONE. AND SO WE DON'T COMPLETELY REPAVE OR RESURFACE THE STREET UNTIL ALL THE WORK THAT NEEDS TO BE DONE ON THE PAVEMENT IS DONE. BUT WE WILL BE MINDFUL OF YOUR CONCERN. AND I WILL MAKE SURE THAT I WORK WITH BOTH OUR PROJECT'S TEAM AND CONTRACT ADMINISTRATION TO MAKE SURE THAT THE ROADWAY SURFACE, EVEN IF IT IS INTERIM, IS SAFE AND GOOD TO DRIVE.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: EVEN THE NONARTERIALS, I MEAN -- I THINK -- I MEAN, TO ME, THAT CREATES HAZARDS FOR PEOPLE WITH BUMPS AND GOUGES. IT NEEDS TO BE PROPERLY DONE REGARDLESS IF YOU HAVE TO COME BACK. LET'S MAKE SURE AT LEAST WE HAVE A SMOOTH SURFACE, YOU MAY NOT GET ALL THE LAYERS SO PEOPLE CAN DRIVE ON THE CAR AND DON'T HAVE AN ACCIDENT. I CAN TELL YOU I WAS ON THE ROAD -- I CALLED YOU RIGHT AWAY. I SAID THIS IS UNACCEPTABLE. BUT THANK YOU FOR AT LEAST ADDRESS IT.

segments.speaker: VIK BHIDE
segments.text: YES, SIR.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO.

A GENTLEMAN -- A GENTLEMAN NAMED NEAL SENT SOME PHOTOS A COUPLE OF HOURS AGO. AND MY AID WAS ALREADY GOING TO CONTACT THE CONTRACTOR AND REACH OUT TO EVERYBODY. I KNOW EXACTLY WHAT YOU ARE TALKING ABOUT. RIGHT IN FRONT OF THE BAKERY.

segments.text: MAN.

BHIDE SAID, THEY DID THE ROAD WORK, THEY PAVED HOW THEY PAVED IT IN ORDER TO OPEN THE ROAD BUT ONCE IT IS COMPLETE, IT WILL BE FIXED. AGAIN, MY AID WAS REACHING OUT TO THE CONTRACTOR A LITTLE WHILE AGO TO GET THAT TAKEN CARE OF.

segments.text: ONE OTHER THING. WATER TO TAP, PISS WATER WHATEVER YOU WANT TO CALL IT INTO THE MISSISSIPPI RIVER. NEW ORLEANS, I WOULD IMAGINE THEY GET SOME OF THE WATER FROM THE MISSISSIPPI OR HAVE GREEN GRASS FOR THE REST OF THEIR LIFE. I DON'T KNOW WHAT IS GOING ON. WE GET OUR WATER FROM WHERE THE -- IF I REMEMBER FOLLOWS INTO THE HILLSBOROUGH RIVER AND COMES ON DOWN. SO IT IS UP TO EVERY DEPARTMENT IN THE WATER DISTRICT AND THE WHOLE UNITED STATES TO DEAL WITH WATER TO MAKE IT SIMPLY THE BEST AND SIMPLY THE CLEANEST, SIMPLY THE MOST HEALTHIEST SO ALL OF US CAN CONSUME THAT WATER. THERE HAS BEEN A WATER WAR BETWEEN COLORADO AND I FORGET WHATEVER STATE FOR 100 YEARS AND I DON'T THINK IT IS OVER YET. WHEN YOU LOOK AT SOME OF THE THINGS IN THE BEAUTIFUL PARKS THAT WE HAVE OUT WEST, THE FLOWS USED TO BE -- NOW 35 FEET. 35 FEET FROM THE WATER USED TO HAVE. WE HAVE A SERIOUS PROBLEM IN THIS COUNTRY AND THE PROBLEM IS US. WE ALREADY HAVE SEVEN BILLION PEOPLE LIVING ON EARTH AND EIGHT BILLION JUST AROUND THE CORNER. SO WE BETTER UNDERSTAND WHAT WE ARE TALKING ABOUT. AND REALIZE WHAT WE HAVE AND TO BE ABLE TO SUPPLY YOURSELF WITH THAT WATER. WE DID SO MANY THINGS. THE CITY OF TAMPA IS ONE OF THE WORST IN FLORIDA TO HAVE THE WATER. AND STILL HAS -- IF I REMEMBER CORRECTLY, WE STILL HAVE SOMEBODY GOING TO RUN A WATER -- I AM NOT HERE TO EMBARRASS EVERYBODY. IF I WAS TO ASK ANY ELECTED OFFICIAL HERE IN THE STATE WHAT YOU WANT TODAY, 80% OF THEM DON'T KNOW. SO WHAT THE HELL ARE WE TALKING ABOUT? segments.speaker: REBECCA JOHNS
segments.text: GOOD MORNING, REBECCA JOHNS, LEGAL DEPARTMENT. WE WERE ASKED TO LOOK AT THE FEASIBILITY OF AN ORDINANCE THAT WOULD PROTECT A TENANT FROM EVICTION FOR RENT THAT WAS PAST DUE FOR MORE THAN SIX MONTHS. EVICTIONS ARE GOLF ERWINED BY FLORIDA STATUTES CHAPTER 83. CHAPTER 83 ALLOWS A LANDLORD TO EVICT A TENANT FOR A BREACH OF A LEASE WHICH WOULD INCLUDE ANY FAILURE TO PAY RENT DUE UNDER THE LEASE. RENT IS DEFINED UNDER THE STATUTE AS ANY PAYMENT DUE TO THE LANDLORD PURSUANT TO THE LEASE. THERE IS NO LIMITING LANGUAGE AS TO A TIME PERIOD. IF WE WERE TO ENACT AN ORDINANCE LIMITING THAT, WE WON'T HAVE THE AUTHORITY TO DO IT BECAUSE WE WOULD BE LIMITING LANDLORDS' RIGHTS UNDER THE STATUTE. HOWEVER, BE AWARE, THAT PRIOR TO AN EVICTION PROCEEDING, A LANDLORD HAS TO PROVIDE WRITTEN NOTICE TO A TENANT LETTING THEM KNOW TO REPRESENT THE LANDLORD CLAIMS IS BACK DUE. IF THE TENANT IS DISPUTING THAT AMOUNT OF RENT, THE EXACT CASE THAT WE HAD THE BAY AREA LEGAL CONTRACT IN PLACE FOR. SO WE CAN REFER TO TENANT TO BAY AREA FOR LEGAL REPRESENTATION TO DISPUTE LANDLORD'S CLAIM OF RENT OWED. I KNOW THAT COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK ASKED FOR A MEMO ON THIS, SO I WILL PROVIDE THAT TO ALL OF YOU. ANY QUESTIONS?

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: ANY QUESTIONS, COUNCILMAN VIERA.

YOU KNOW THE -- AND I OBVIOUSLY UNDERSTAND THE -- SOME OF THE ISSUES YOU TALKED ABOUT THERE. AND IF YOU HAVE A CONTRACT AND IT IS ENFORCEABLE. JUST THAT THERE IS A RIGHT WAY TO DO IT AND THAT IS WHERE THE BULLY PULPIT COMES IN WITH A LOT OF THESE PROPERTY OWNERS TO MAKE SURE THEY DO THINGS THE RIGHT WAY AND I THINK TIMBER FALLS IS A GOOD EXAMPLE OF THAT IN TERMS OF HOW PEOPLE MOVE DIGNITY FORWARD IN THAT REGARD. I WILL LOOK AT THIS A LITTLE FURTHER TO SEE IF THERE IS ANYTHING WE CAN DO ON THIS. THERE MAY BE. THERE MAY NOT BE IN TERMS OF AN ORDINANCE. I WANT TO THANK YOU FOR YOUR WORK.

WHEN THEY DO A WORKSHOP AND TRAINING OF BEST PRACTICES, ALL THE PIOs THAT ARE IN DIFFERENT DEPARTMENTS, THERE IS ONE IN JEAN'S GROUP, OCEA, WE ALL COME TOGETHER TO MAKE SURE WE ARE ALL ON THE SAME WAGE WITH BEST PRACTICES AND HOW TO MOVE FORWARD. NEXT, WE HAVE THE ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT TEAM. I'LL TELL YOU A MAJORITY OF THE 15 PROJECTS ARE WITHIN MY PORTFOLIO. THE AREAS OF EXPERTISE ARE REALLY ON ECONOMIC STRATEGY, START-UP AND SMALL BUSINESS SUPPORT, COMMERCIAL REGENERATION PROJECTS, WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT, INDUSTRY CLUSTERS. AND SO THERE ARE FOUR EXPERTS ON THE ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT TEAM. THE ONE THING THAT YOU SHOULD KNOW ABOUT THE BLOOMBERG TEAM IS THAT THEIR TEAM INCLUDES FORMER SENIOR LEADERS AT CITY AND STATE ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENTS. THEY ARE FOCUSED ON IMPLEMENTATION AND HOW DO WE GET THINGS FROM CONCEPT TO IMPLEMENTATION. THEIR COMMITMENT IS TOWARDS HELPING ENSURE THAT ALL RESIDENTS, PARTICULARLY THOSE FROM DISADVANTAGED GROUPS HAVE ACCESS TO ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITY. THIS IS SOMETHING WE'VE BEEN WORKING REALLY CLOSE, AND WHEN I SHOW YOU SOME OF THE PROJECTS THAT WE'VE BEEN WORKING ON, PRIMARILY EAST TAMPA, YOU'LL SEE. AND A LOT OF THE WORK THAT WE'RE DOING IS DATA DRIVEN.  SO OFTENTIMES BLOOMBERG STAFF WILL ASSIST US IN LOOKING AT THE BEST PRACTICES OR EVEN TALKING THROUGH WHAT TYPE OF METRICS WILL HELP US -- COUNCILMAN CARLSON HAS TALKED ABOUT THE METRICS AND HOW WE MEASURE OUR ECONOMIC SUCCESS AND NOT NECESSARILY DOING THAT AT MSA LEVEL, BUT HOW IS THE CITY OF TAMPA PERFORMING ON THESE PARTICULAR METRICS. IT'S BEYOND JUST THE POVERTY AND EDUCATION METRICS. LIKE, ONE OF THE THINGS THAT I WANT TO -- I'LL GO OFF ON A TANGENT NOW. BUT ONE OF THE THINGS I WANT TO ENSURE IS THAT WHEN WE PROVIDE RESOURCES AND FUNDING FOR DIFFERENT PROJECTS OR PARTNER WITH AGENCIES, WE REALLY SHOULD BE FIGURING OUT WHAT IS THE RETURN ON THE INVESTMENT? WHAT ARE THE CITIZENS GETTING FROM THAT? ARE WE REALLY IMPROVING ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITY FOR OUR MOST DISADVANTAGED CITIZENS? AND HOW DO WE MEASURE THAT? AND SO THOSE ARE THINGS THAT JUST KIND OF WORKING THROUGH THAT WITH BLOOMBERG. THE LAST THING I'LL SAY ABOUT THE TEAM IS THAT THE LONG-TERM COMMITMENT HELPS US TO SEE THIS OVER TIME AND TO PUT STRATEGIES IN PLACE THAT WE CAN MEASURE THE SUCCESS. SO THE PROJECTS THAT WE'RE WORKING ON, DEVELOPING A SHORT-TERM ECONOMIC ROAD MAP. THE MAYOR'S WORKFORCE COUNCIL, EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE. YOU'LL SEE THAT THEY JUST HAD A WORKFORCE SUMMIT WITH BRENT McKENZIE, THAT WORKING THROUGH THE DIFFERENT STRATEGIES FOR HOW ARE WE ENSURING OUR WORKFORCE THAT WE ARE CAPTURING AND TRAINING THE WORKFORCE, THAT'S WORKING WITH HIGHER INSTITUTIONS TO MAKE SURE THAT WE'RE TRAINING OUR STUDENTS AND GOING EVEN FURTHER INTO THE MIDDLE SCHOOL, HIGH SCHOOL LEVELS TO MAKE SURE THAT WE'RE TRAINING OUR STUDENTS TO STAY WITHIN OUR COMMUNITY AND WHAT THAT WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT LOOKS LIKE. EAST TAMPA, 22nd AND LAKE DEVELOPMENT, WE'RE LOOKING FOR CATALYTIC PROJECTS IN EAST TAMPA TO REALLY REJUMP-START THE DEVELOPMENT. I'LL WEAR MY REDEVELOPMENT HAT FOR A MINUTE. PRIVATE INVESTMENT FOLLOWS PUBLIC DOLLARS. THEY FOLLOW PUBLIC INVESTMENT. AND SO REALLY WE WANT TO JUMP-START -- EAST TAMPA NEEDS A CATALYTIC PROJECT. EAST TAMPA NEEDS A BIG WIN TO SHOW PRIVATE INVESTORS THAT THEY SHOULDN'T BE AFRAID OF INVESTING INTO THE EAST TAMPA AREA. SO THAT'S WHAT WE'RE WORKING THROUGH WITH BLOOMBERG, LOOKING AT DIFFERENT TAX CREDIT FUNDING PROJECTS THAT HAVE BEEN SUCCESSFUL IN OTHER COMMUNITIES, BUT WE'RE WORKING ON THAT WITH THEM. AND ALSO USING OPPORTUNITY ZONES, WHICH IS A FINANCE MECHANISM TO REALLY LOOK AT PROJECTS WITHIN EAST TAMPA TO BE ABLE TO FOCUS ON THAT. THERE IS AN INTERNAL MANAGEMENT TEAM. THIS TEAM, THEIR EXPERTISE IS REALLY INTERNAL OPERATIONS AND REPORTING PROJECT MANAGEMENT. WE HAVE FOUR BLOOMBERG EXPERTS ON THIS THAT'S WORKING WITH OCEA WYNN'S GROUP. THIS IS AROUND THE PROJECT OF CUSTOMER-CENTRIC PERFORMANCE SYSTEMS. THEY RECENTLY HAD SOME DEPARTMENT INTERVIEWS, DIFFERENT DEPARTMENTS WITHIN MY PORTFOLIO WERE INTERVIEWED TRYING TO FIGURE OUT NOT ONLY EXTERNAL CUSTOMER INTERFACE, BUT INTERNAL CUSTOMER INTERFACE AND HOW DO WE MEASURE OUR PERFORMANCE AND RESPONSIVENESS. SUSTAINABILITY TEAM. ASSESS SUSTAINABILITY BUT A LOT OF IT IS REALLY AROUND HOUSING AFFORDABILITY, THE HOUSING SUPPLY INVENTORY. THE PROJECTS THAT WE HAVE THAT WE'RE ACTIVELY WORKING ON. COMMUNITY LAND TRUST THAT ALIS DRUMGO IS LEADING AND HAS A PRESENTATION COMING TO YOU IN THE BEGINNING OF 2023 ON THIS. AND THEN THE HOUSING NEEDS ASSESSMENT, WRITING THE SCOPE AND MAKING SURE THAT -- CRAFT A SCOPE FOR IN TRYING TO UNDERSTAND WHAT EXACTLY THE DATA THAT WE WANT TO CAPTURE TO MAKE SURE THAT THOSE THINGS WERE WRITTEN INTO THE SCOPE. SO WE HAVE THE EXPERTISE TO WRITE SCOPE, BUT THEY HAVE PROVIDED US WITH BEST PRACTICES AND OTHER MAJOR CITIES THAT HAVE DONE NEEDS ASSESSMENT AND WHAT INFORMATION WAS HELPFUL THEN FOR THEM TO ACCELERATE CERTAIN MULTIFAMILY DEVELOPMENT AND WE INCLUDED THOSE THINGS IN OUR RFP. THE TRANSPORTATION TEAM, ROAD SAFETY, PEDESTRIAN INFRASTRUCTURE IMPROVEMENTS, BIKE SHARE AND CYCLING INFRASTRUCTURE. THERE ARE FOUR BLOOMBERG EXPERTS ON THIS. THEY WORK WITH VIK DIRECTLY ON THESE PROJECTS. WE'RE LOOKING AT PROJECTS THAT ARE TRAFFIC CALMING AND PEDESTRIAN SAFETY PROJECTS IN FOUR DIFFERENT AREAS. ALSO LOOKING AT THE POTENTIAL OF STREET EXPANSION AND FUTURE INTEGRATION OF BIKE LANES AROUND WHITING STREET AND ALSO BEING PROACTIVE IN OUR TRAFFIC-CALMING AND PEDESTRIAN SAFETY AROUND ANY FUTURE DEVELOPMENT THAT MAY HAPPEN AROUND THE TAMPA UNION STATION. THE BLOOMBERG URBAN PLANNING TEAM HAS THREE EXPERTS. THEY DO A LOT OF THE LONG-RANGE PLANNING, ZONING, NEIGHBORHOOD IMPROVEMENT DISTRICTS, WATER REVITALIZATION AND ACCESS. THEY HAVE THREE EXPERTS ON THAT TEAM. AGAIN, THIS IS IN MY PORTFOLIO. THEY WORK VERY CLOSELY WITH STEPHEN BENSON. I'VE TALKED TO SOME OF YOU ABOUT IT IS THE LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE. YOU'VE HEARD IT FROM DEVELOPERS. YOU FEEL IT AT YOUR EVENING MEETINGS. WE HAVE A 30-YEAR LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE THAT HAS BEEN ADDED TO AND FIXED TO. AND ONE OF THE THINGS THAT NOT JUST FOR THE DEVELOPMENT COMMUNITY, BUT FOR THE RESIDENTS AND CITIZENS TO UNDERSTAND WHAT THE LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE IS, HOW TO USE IT, HOW TO FOLLOW IT. WE REALLY NEED TO UPDATE OUR LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE. IT HASN'T BEEN TOUCHED IN 30 YEARS. I SEE WHY. ONE OF THE THINGS I SHARE -- I SHARED THIS WITH SOME OF YOU, BEFORE TAKING THIS POSITION, SOMEONE THAT USED TO WORK HERE CALLED ME AND SAID, YOU'RE VERY AMBITIOUS BUT DON'T TRY AND SAVE THE WORLD. LEAVE THE LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE ALONE. IT'S A BEAST. DON'T TAKE IT ON. MY RESPONSE WAS, HOLD MY BEER. WATCH ME DO IT, RIGHT? I JUST KNOW THAT THE LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE IS SUPPOSED TO WORK NOT JUST FOR DEVELOPERS BUT THE CITIZENS IN THE COMMUNITY AND WHAT THEY WANT TO SEE WITH THE FUTURE, WHAT THEY WANT IT TO LOOK LIKE. I CHALLENGED THE BLOOMBERG TEAM TO, NUMBER ONE, GO IN AND DO A FORENSIC AUDIT OF OUR CODE. WHERE ARE THERE INEFFICIENCIES IN THE CODE? ARE WE BRINGING THINGS TO COUNCIL THAT THEY ARE AUTOMATICALLY APPROVING AND JUST BRINGING IT BECAUSE THE CODE IS NOT EFFICIENT? SO THEY ARE GOING THROUGH AND DOING A FORENSIC AUDIT. THAT'S ONE OF THE FIRST THINGS WE'RE DOING. THEY ALSO STARTED PHASE ONE OF THE PUBLIC -- STAKEHOLDER INTERVIEWS, PEOPLE WHO USE THE CODE A LOT TO HEAR ANY RECOMMENDATIONS. HOW DO THEY FEEL ABOUT THE CODE? SO WE COULD USE THAT INFORMATION TO GUIDE US IN NEXT STEPS ON HOW DO WE APPROACH REHAULING THE LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE. I COMPLETELY WENT OFF ON A TANGENT ON THAT. MUNICIPAL INTEGRITY IS ONE OF THE OTHER TEAMS. THERE IS A GROUP OF THREE BLOOMBERG EXPERTS ON THERE. THEY SPECIALIZE IN CONTRACT, TRANSPARENCY, PROCUREMENT PROCESS, OPTIMIZATION, INTERNAL OPERATIONS AND REPORTING. THIS IS WHERE WE TALK ABOUT THE LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE FORENSIC AUDIT. THIS IS WHERE THEY ARE GOING THROUGH REZONING WAIVERS SUBMITTED IN 2021 TO FIND THE DEFICIENCIES. THE THINGS I SPOKE TO YOU ABOUT, IT'S REALLY THE MUNICIPAL INTEGRITY TEAM THAT'S DOING THAT PIECE WHILE WE'RE DOING THE STAKEHOLDER INTERVIEWS. ALL OF THIS IS IN AN EFFORT TO MAKE OUR CODE EASIER FOR THE CITIZENS AND FOR YOU AND YOUR LONG EVENING MEETINGS. AND THEN LASTLY, THE LAST TEAM WE'RE WORKING WITH IS THE REAL ESTATE TEAM. THOSE ARE TWO BLOOMBERG EXPERTS ON THAT. AGAIN, THIS IS IN MY PORTFOLIO. WE REALLY NEED TO BE LOOKING AT REAL ESTATE AS A STRATEGIC PLANNING DEPARTMENT AND TO NOT BE REACTIVE TO REAL ESTATE TRANSACTIONS AND DEALS. THEY ARE ALSO CONDUCTING INTERDEPARTMENTAL INTERVIEWS BECAUSE THE REAL ESTATE DEPARTMENT HANDLES A LOT OF THE TRANSACTIONAL THINGS FOR STORMWATER, MOBILITY, IF WE NEED RIGHT-OF-WAY. A LOT OF THOSE TRANSACTIONAL THINGS, THEY DO DAY TO DAY, BUT WE ALSO NEED TO BE VERY STRATEGIC IN OUR LAND ACQUISITION AND DISPOSITION AND HOW WE MANAGE ALL OF THOSE ASSETS. SO WE'RE MOVING FORWARD WITH LOOKING AT BEST PRACTICES AND ALSO SYSTEMS THAT'S IN PLACE TO MANAGE OUR ASSETS AND NOT AN EXCEL SPREADSHEET, RIGHT? THIS IS JUST A BRIEF OVERVIEW OF THE PROJECTS WE'RE WORKING ON THAT STAFF WOULD OTHERWISE NOT HAVE TIME TO WORK ON THESE TYPES OF INITIATIVES. I HAVE TO TELL YOU THAT IT MAKES MY JOB A LITTLE EASIER BECAUSE A LOT OF THE DAY TO DAY IS REACTIONARY TO WHO IS CALLING NOW, WHAT'S HAPPENING NOW, AND SOME OF THE STRATEGIC, FORWARD-FOCUSED, PLANNING-FOCUSED, I DON'T HAVE THE TIME DAY TO DAY TO WORK ON THAT. THIS ALLOWS US EXTRA HANDS TO DO THAT.

I JUST WANTED TO GET THAT OUT OF THE WAY FIRST. SO WE DO HAVE COPIES IN FRONT OF US.

segments.text: THE WAY THAT THESE ARE STRUCTURED, SECTION 1 ACTUALLY HAS THE CHARTER LANGUAGE AND A STRIKE-THROUGH AND UNDERLINE SHOWING THE CHANGE. SECTION 2 HAS THE QUESTION THAT WILL BE POSED TO THE ELECTORATE. SO FOR THE ORDINANCE CHANGING SECTION 5.01, YOU'LL SEE THAT THIS ORDINANCE WAS REVISED TO PROVIDE FOR THE HIRING OF LEGAL COUNSEL WHO IS NOT A CITY EMPLOYEE TO SERVE -- I'M SORRY, TO ADVISE THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT. THAT IS WHAT THE LANGUAGE SHOWS. IN ADDITION TO THAT, ONE OTHER POINT THAT WAS DISCUSSED WITH BOTH THE CITY ATTORNEY AND THE CITY COUNCIL ATTORNEY IS THAT THERE IS AN EXISTING ORDINANCE THAT DEALS WITH THIS BOARD. IT'S CODIFIED IN SECTION 18-8 OF THE CITY'S CODE, AND IT PROVIDES FOR LEGAL COUNSEL FOR THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD. SO SHOULD THIS BOARD DESIRE, THEY COULD ALSO AMEND THAT ORDINANCE TO PROVIDE FOR THE DESIRED LEGAL COUNSEL THAT YOU WANT, AND THAT WOULD ALLOW QUICKER AMENDMENT AND POTENTIALLY IN LESS TIME AND WITH LESS EXPENSE THAN GOING TO REFERENDUM, OR YOU CAN GO FORWARD WITH THE CHARTER AMENDMENTS THAT'S BEFORE YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COMMENTS OR QUESTIONS?

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: THE ONLY, IF I MAY, IT SAYS PROVIDE LEGAL COUNSEL, BUT IT DOESN'T SAY WHAT THE START, WHAT THE HOURLY RATE IS GOING TO BE. IT DOESN'T SAY ANYTHING ABOUT THE COUNSEL WE'RE GOING TO HIRE, WHOEVER THAT PERSON MAY BE.

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: CORRECT, CORRECT.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: A BLANK CHECK? I DON'T KNOW WHAT IT IS.

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: NO, IT'S JUST THAT THE CITY WILL PROVIDE THAT COUNSEL TO THE BOARD. AS IT'S WRITTEN, IT JUST SAYS THAT THE CITY SHALL PROVIDE COUNSEL. IN MANY OTHER INSTANCES, YOU MAY HAVE THE CITY THAT PROVIDES COUNSEL ON A UNIQUE ISSUE. FOR INSTANCE, TODAY, WITH ME STANDING BEFORE YOU AND HAVING DRAFTED THESE ORDINANCES, THIS WAS DONE UNDER SPECIAL COUNSEL AGREEMENT THAT WE HAVE WITH THE CITY.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: I REALIZE FROM TIME TO TIME THE CITY DOES HIRE OUTSIDE COUNSEL, IF THEY HAVE A CONFLICT OR SOMETHING OF THAT NATURE, BUT THIS IS JUST WIDE OPEN. I DON'T KNOW. I'M JUST ASKING A QUESTION.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I AGREE WITH SOME OF THE SPEAKERS FROM EARLIER THIS MORNING BECAUSE THE ENTIRE POINT, IS THE CITY NOT PROVIDING, CRB ABLE TO RETAIN. I ACTUALLY HAVE THE LANGUAGE FOR THE OTHER THAT I ASKED THIS TO BE BASED ON. THE MIAMI CITY CODE, THE ORANGE COUNTY CODE, AND THE KEY WEST CHARTER. AND THE MIAMI CITY CODE STARTS THE CIP BECAUSE THEY CALL IT THE CITIZENS -- I CAN'T REMEMBER THE EXACT NAME. BUT THEY CALL IT THE CIP INSTEAD. IT SAYS THE CIP SHALL HIRE AN EXPERIENCED AND COMPETENT ATTORNEY AT LAW. IN THE ORANGE COUNTY CODE, IT SAYS THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD IS AUTHORIZED TO RETAIN LEGAL COUNSEL. KEY WEST CHARTER SAYS THE CRB MAY RETAIN THE SERVICES OF AN ATTORNEY. SO IN FOLLOWING THAT VEIN, MAKING SURE IT IS NOT THE CITY RETAINING BUT THE BOARD TO KEEP IT COMPLETELY AUTONOMOUS. SO I WOULD LIKE TO MAKE THAT CHANGE. I ALSO WOULD LIKE TO TAKE OUT FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT BECAUSE THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD IS ABOUT THE POLICE DEPARTMENT. I DON'T SEE A NEED TO HAVE THOSE. BUT I ALSO WOULD LIKE TO TAKE OUT THE LANGUAGE FOR THE POLICE DEPARTMENT IN THE ACTUAL AMENDMENT BECAUSE, AGAIN, THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD, THAT'S WHAT IT DOES ALREADY. THOSE ARE THE CHANGES I WOULD MAKE.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN VIERA.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: THANK YOU. AND, YEAH, I'M FINE WITH THAT. I MEAN, I LIKE COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK, I THINK YOU SAID THE SECOND PART, WHERE IT SAYS THE CRB IS AUTHORIZED TO, SO THEY MAY DO SO. AND WITH REGARDS TO COUNCILMAN MIRANDA'S POINT, WHICH IS VERY WELL TAKEN ON HAVING IT BE A BLANK CHECK, I MEAN, WHAT THIS IS ESSENTIALLY IS GIVING THE CRB. I USE THE TERM OUTSIDE COUNSEL AND EVERY ATTORNEY SHOULD BY VIRTUE OF THEIR BEING A MEMBER OF THE FLORIDA BAR BE INDEPENDENT AND HAVE LOYALTY AND WHATNOT TO THEIR CLIENT OR CLIENTS. BUT OUTSIDE COUNSEL FOR THE MEETINGS, WHICH I THINK SHOULD BE PRETTY NARROWLY TAILORED, BECAUSE THE MEETINGS DON'T RUN TOO DARN LONG. THE RESEARCH ISSUES, IF ANY, WOULD BE VERY, VERY LIMITED. THE GOING GOVERNMENT RATE IS PROBABLY -- I DON'T WANT TO SAY A NUMBER, PROBABLY $200 FOR SOMETHING LIKE THIS, 205, 210, SOMETHING OF THAT NATURE. BUT IN OTHER WORDS, I'M FINE WITH THOSE CHANGES.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: THANK YOU. THAT'S ACTUALLY A REALLY GOOD POINT IS WE'RE NOT HIRING A FULL-TIME ATTORNEY. THEY DON'T -- THEY JUST NEED SOMEONE WHEN THEY NEED SOMEONE. SO, YES. THANK YOU FOR BRINGING THAT UP.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: I'M CONFUSED A LITTLE BIT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN GUDES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO.

segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH. AND I AGREE WITH WHAT'S BEEN SAID, SPECIFICALLY COUNCILMAN VIERA AND THE MAKER OR THE POTENTIAL MOTION THAT'S COMING. THESE MEETINGS HAVE BEEN ONCE A MONTH. IT'S NOT A HUGE AMOUNT OF MONEY BEING BILLED. I DON'T KNOW WHAT THE HOURLY RATE WOULD BE, BUT THE MEETINGS MIGHT BE OVER IN TWO HOURS, MIGHT BE AN HOUR, I DON'T KNOW. SO THIS WOULD PROVIDE FOR THE BOARD TO BE TRULY INDEPENDENT. HAVE A TRULY -- THERE IS NO PERCEPTION OF A BIAS. THERE IS NO PERCEPTION OF, WELL, THE CITY CHOSE THIS ATTORNEY. IT KEEPS IT SEPARATE. IT MAKES IT INDEPENDENT. I DON'T SEE ANY HARM IN THIS. I THINK IT HELPS KEEP THE BOARD AS INTENDED TO BE, AND THAT IS INDEPENDENT OF THE OPINIONS OF WHOMEVER. THEY RETAIN THE ATTORNEY, AND THAT'S IT. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: ANY OTHER QUESTIONS? AND IT IS NOT DEFINED, BUT I'M CURIOUS TO COUNCILMAN MIRANDA'S QUESTION, WHOSE BUDGET IS THIS COMING OUT OF? IS THIS COMING OUT OF CITY COUNCIL'S BUDGET? IS IT COMING OUT OF THE CITY OF TAMPA'S BUDGET? BUT BEFORE -- MS. ZELMAN, YOU'LL GET UP AND ANSWER THAT, BUT I WOULD LIKE AN OPINION FROM THE LEGAL REPRESENTATIVE. THERE IS A CLAUSE THAT WE HAVE, AND IT STATES THAT THE CRB CAN ASK FOR AN OUTSIDE INDEPENDENT ATTORNEY FOR A CONFLICT OF INTEREST. HOW DOES THIS DIFFER FROM CONFLICT OF INTEREST, AND CAN THAT CONFLICT OF INTEREST -- CAN THE ATTORNEYS THAT ARE ON THE LIST THAT THE CITY HAS FOR OUTSIDE INDEPENDENT ATTORNEYS BE USED FOR THAT CONFLICT OF INTEREST? DON'T WE ALREADY HAVE THAT IN PLACE NOW? YOUR CITY ATTORNEY.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU, THEN. MS. ZELMAN.

segments.speaker: ANDREA ZELMAN
segments.text: JUST TO CLARIFY, ANDREA ZELMAN, CITY ATTORNEY. A COUPLE OF THINGS, LET ME ADDRESS ONE OF THE MORE MINOR ISSUES FIRST. TO THE PEOPLE THAT WERE ASKING WHY THE WORDS "FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT" WAS IN THERE, THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD ORDINANCE, SECTION 18-8 OF THE CITY CODE, THE TITLE IS, "CITIZEN REVIEW BOARD FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT." SO THAT LANGUAGE WAS JUST PUT IN TO MIRROR THE WAY THE CURRENT CODE READS. THERE WAS NOTHING INTENDED. -- NOT INTENDED TO MISLEAD ANYONE. I THINK THE ISSUE WE'RE GETTING AT IS IN THE CITY OF TAMPA, THE ONLY ENTITY THAT CAN ENTER INTO CONTRACTS IS THE CITY OF TAMPA. THE CITY COUNCIL CAN'T INDEPENDENTLY CONTRACT WITH AN ATTORNEY NOR CAN ANY BOARD, INCLUDING THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD. SO I THINK WHAT JENNIFER AND MARTY AND I DISCUSSED, WHAT WAS TRYING TO BE ACCOMPLISHED HERE IS WHAT WE THOUGHT WAS COUNCIL'S INTENT WAS SIMPLY THE ATTORNEY WOULDN'T BE AN EMPLOYEE OF THE CITY OF TAMPA, SO THEY WOULD HAVE THAT LEVEL OF INDEPENDENCE. THEY CAN'T TRULY BE INDEPENDENT OF THE CITY OF TAMPA BECAUSE THE CITY OF TAMPA HAS TO CONTRACT WITH THEM, PAY THEM, AND THEY ARE REPRESENTING A BOARD OF THE CITY OF TAMPA. PERHAPS A SUGGESTION MAY BE IF WHAT CITY COUNCIL IS TRYING TO GET AT IS THAT THEY WANT TO SAY IN PICKING THE ATTORNEY RATHER THAN OUR DEPARTMENT PICKING THE ATTORNEY, WE COULD HAVE A PROCESS LIKE WHAT YOU ALL DID WITH THE SEARCH FOR YOUR CRA DIRECTOR, WHERE THE BODY ITSELF AND IN THIS CASE, I GUESS IT WOULD BE THE CRB COULD INTERVIEW THE ATTORNEYS THAT THEY ARE SELECTING. AGAIN, AT THE END OF THE DAY, IT'S STILL GOING TO BE A CONTRACT WITH THE CITY OF TAMPA. AND I THOUGHT THAT THE KEY THING THE COUNCIL WAS TRYING TO ACCOMPLISH WAS THAT IT NOT BE AN EMPLOYEE OF THE CITY OF TAMPA. MY OFFICE, THE POLICE DEPARTMENT, WHATEVER. AND THEN ONE FINAL COMMENT TO JENNIFER'S POINT, THIS COULD BE ACCOMPLISHED BY AMENDING SECTION 18-8 WITHOUT TOUCHING THE CHARTER, WHICH, AS SHE SAID, WOULD BE A LOT FASTER. WOULDN'T REQUIRE A REFERENDUM. WOULDN'T REQUIRE A RUSH TO GET IT ON THE BALLOT, JUST AS AN ASIDE. AGAIN, I THINK THAT'S WHAT YOU'RE TRYING TO ACCOMPLISH. AND THEN, FINALLY, AS TO THE OTHER ORDINANCES THAT YOU MENTIONED FROM ORANGE COUNTY AND KEY WEST, KEY WEST, THEY HAVE A WHOLE CHARTER PROVISION FOR THEIR CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD. WE DON'T. IF YOU LOOK AT SOME OF THOSE OTHER ONES, LIKE THE ORANGE COUNTY ONE, IT BASICALLY SAYS THAT THE PANEL CAN SELECT THE ATTORNEY WITH THE FUNDING TO BE PROVIDED BY THE COUNTY COMMISSION. SO I MEAN AT THE END OF THE DAY, IT'S STILL AS IT WOULD BE HERE. STILL THE CITY ATTORNEY WHO IS HIRING OR PAYING OR CONTRACTING WITH THE ATTORNEY BUT IF YOU WANTED THE BOARD TO HAVE MORE SAY IN THE SELECTION PROCESS, THERE WOULD BE A WAY TO WRITE THAT IN. PROBABLY BETTER TO PUT IT IN THE ORDINANCE THAN IN THE CHARTER, BUT THAT'S FOR YOU ALL TO DECIDE.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THAT GOES BACK TO MY QUESTION. WE ALREADY HAVE THAT IN PLACE.

segments.speaker: ANDREA ZELMAN
segments.text: YOU HAVE IT IN PLACE FOR CONFLICT COUNSEL, CORRECT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THEN THE BOARD CAN SAY, SORRY, WE DON'T WANT YOU TO GET INVOLVED BECAUSE OF THIS CONFLICT OF INTEREST. NOW WE'LL GO AND GO OUTSIDE WITH THE LIST OF ATTORNEYS WHO ARE ALREADY THERE.

segments.speaker: ANDREA ZELMAN
segments.text: RIGHT. NOW, THE WAY IT IS CURRENTLY WRITTEN, IT IS THE CITY ATTORNEY WHO HAS TO MAKE THAT DETERMINATION. JUST TO BE FAIR. I THINK WHAT YOU'RE TRYING TO ACCOMPLISH IS YOU WANT THE BOARD TO MAKE THE DETERMINATION.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: STEP ASIDE, WE HAVE A CONFLICT HERE. THAT'S HOW I'M READING IT.

segments.speaker: ANDREA ZELMAN
segments.text: NO, NO.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THAT'S WHAT I'M TRYING TO GET CLARIFICATION ON.

segments.speaker: ANDREA ZELMAN
segments.text: WELL, CURRENTLY, THAT ONLY APPLIES TO WHEN THERE IS A CONFLICT. I THINK WHAT MS. HURTAK IS TRYING TO SAY IS WHAT THIS PROPOSAL WAS INTENDED TO ADDRESS WAS JUST FULL-TIME. JUST FULL-TIME, KIND OF AN OUTSIDE COUNSEL AS THE BOARD'S ATTORNEY, NOT ONLY IN CASES OF CONFLICT.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: FULL-TIME MEANING WHENEVER THEY ARE NEEDED, NOT FULL-TIME, FULL-TIME.

segments.speaker: ANDREA ZELMAN
segments.text: CORRECT.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: WOULD THIS LANGUAGE BE BETTER, THE CRB SHALL RETAIN LEGAL COUNSEL WITH FUNDING TO BE PROVIDED BY THE CITY WHO IS NOT A CITY EMPLOYEE TO SERVE AS A LEGAL ADVISOR TO THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD. IF WE ADDED THAT LANGUAGE JUST TO CLARIFY, YES, WITH FUNDING TO BE PROVIDED BY THE CITY.

segments.speaker: ANDREA ZELMAN
segments.text: I'M SORRY. CAN YOU SAY THAT AGAIN?

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: THE CRB SHALL RETAIN LEGAL COUNSEL WITH FUNDING TO BE PROVIDED BY THE CITY WHO IS NOT A CITY EMPLOYEE TO SERVE AS THE LEGAL ADVISOR TO THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD.

segments.speaker: ANDREA ZELMAN
segments.text: AGAIN, RETAIN IMPLIES THEY WOULD BE CONTRACTING WITH THAT ATTORNEY, BUT ONLY THE CITY CAN CONTRACT WITH THE ATTORNEY. SO I THINK SOMETHING LIKE SHALL SELECT, SOMETHING.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: SELECT. I'M HAPPY WITH THAT WORD "SELECT."

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO.

segments.text: IF THIS WORKS, THIS WORKS. I WAS GOING TO SUGGEST IF THIS WERE NOT TO WORK THAT THE CRB NOMINATE AN ATTORNEY OF THEIR CHOOSING THAT'S NOT A CITY ATTORNEY AND THEN IT COMES TO CITY COUNCIL FOR FINAL APPROVAL, BEING THEY ARE CONTRACTING WITH THE CITY AND THE MONEY IS COMING FROM US SO THERE IS A LEVEL OF OVERSIGHT. I DON'T KNOW IF THAT'S NECESSARY OR POSSIBLE IF THIS DOESN'T FLY.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: YEAH, THAT'S --

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO

segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH. AND WHEN I SERVED ON THE CIVIL SERVICE REVIEW BOARD, WE HAD AN ATTORNEY WHO WAS AN OUTSIDE ATTORNEY. OURS WAS DELANO STUART, GOD BLESS HIM, A GREAT GUY. IT WASN'T A FULL-TIME JOB. PROBABLY DID IT TWO, THREE, FOUR HOURS A MONTH. THAT'S KIND OF WHAT I THINK A REASONABLE INTERPRETATION OF THIS IS. AND ALSO REMEMBER, AGAIN -- AND I'LL LEAVE IT TO THE MAKER OF THE MOTION -- THAT THE CRB DOESN'T NECESSARILY HAVE TO DO THIS. IT'S IF THEY CHOOSE TO DO THIS. IN TERMS OF THE CONFLICT ISSUE, THERE'S WHAT I CALL SMALL C CONFLICT AND BIG C CONFLICT. THE BIG C CONFLICT IS IF THERE IS A DIRECT CONFLICT OF INTEREST THAT GOES ON THAT IMPLICATES SOMETHING THAT STARTS A LEGAL, ETHICAL PROCESS. I THINK WHAT THE LARGER POINT THAT PEOPLE HAVE BEEN MAKING HERE FOR A YEAR AND A HALF OR TWO YEARS ON THIS ISSUE IS THAT THERE IS FOR A BOARD LIKE THIS, THERE OUGHT TO BE AN OUTSIDE COUNSEL WHO IS NOT A CITY EMPLOYEE. I THINK THAT IS A VERY REASONABLE POSITION TO TAKE. I ALWAYS SUPPORTED THAT, ET CETERA, ET CETERA. TO ENTER THE CHAIRMAN'S POINT, CHAIRMAN CITRO, THE POINT OF CONFLICT, THIS IS NOT WHAT THIS IS ADDRESSING. THIS IS ADDRESSING THE APPEARANCE OF NONINDEPENDENCE AND FOR A BOARD LIKE THIS, APPEARANCE IS SOMETHING THAT'S VERY RELEVANT. SO THAT'S WHY I SUPPORT THIS AS SUCH. THANK YOU.

segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: SECOND.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: WE HAVE A MOTION MADE BY COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK, SECONDED BY COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO. IS THERE ANY FURTHER DISCUSSION? MR. SHELBY.

segments.text: LOGISTICALLY, COUNCIL, I WANT TO BE CLEAR HOW THIS IS HANDLED SO WE CAN PUT IT WITHIN THE TIME FRAME NECESSARY TO GET IT TO THE SUPERVISOR OF ELECTIONS. I SPOKE WITH THE CLERK THIS MORNING. WE ALSO HAVE AN ISSUE OF PUBLICATION FOR SECOND READING. AND THE DATE FOR PUBLICATION, THE WAY THE PUBLICATIONS WORK IS DECEMBER 21st, IS THAT CORRECT? THAT WOULD BE THE SOONEST IT COULD BE ADVERTISED.

segments.text: [INAUDIBLE]

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: OKAY. ON DECEMBER 23rd IT GETS PUBLISHED. THEN IT WOULD COME BACK TO COUNCIL JANUARY 5th BECAUSE SHORTLY THEREAFTER I BELIEVE IT'S THE CUT OFF TIME. IT HAS TO BE AT THE SUPERVISOR OF ELECTIONS I BELIEVE BY THE 20th. I'LL DOUBLE CHECK. IT DOES HAVE TO GO OVER TO THE MAYOR AND THE ADMINISTRATION AS WELL.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: WE HAVE A MOTION MADE BY COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK. SECONDED BY COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO. ANY FURTHER DISCUSSION? COUNCILMAN CARLSON. I THOUGHT YOU WERE GOING TO PUT ON YOUR LIGHT.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: JUST ONE MORE CLARIFICATION, THEN, AND I GUESS THAT WOULD GO TO YOU, MS. COWAN. IF THESE CHANGES ARE MADE TODAY TO THIS OR ANY OF THE OTHER ORDINANCES, WHEN WOULD IT THEN BE APPROVED BY COUNCIL FOR FIRST READING CONSIDERATION?

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: THIS IS CHANGE TO THE LANGUAGE --

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I WAS TOLD WE COULD MAKE CHANGES BETWEEN FIRST AND SECOND READING. THAT WAS THE WHOLE POINT OF THIS IS THAT SOME OF THIS WE WERE GOING TO FLESH OUT HERE BETWEEN FIRST AND SECOND READING.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: I WANT TO BE ABSOLUTELY CLEAR. IF THAT IS THE UNDERSTANDING, I WANT TO BE ABSOLUTELY CLEAR ABOUT THIS SO THERE'S NO MISUNDERSTANDING. DOES -- IF IT REQUIRES A TITLE CHANGE --

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: IF YOU CHANGE THE LANGUAGE TO TAKE OUT "FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT," IT WILL REQUIRE ANOTHER FIRST READING BECAUSE YOU'LL CHANGE THE TITLE BLOCK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: OKAY. THEN WE CAN KEEP "FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT." THAT'S FINE. OR I'M SORRY.

segments.text: COUNCILMAN CARLSON.
