segments.text: IF WE DO THAT THEN BECAUSE THERE'S BEEN WHAT I CONSIDER A LOT OF MISINTERPRETATIONS OF THE CHARTER, I THINK WE SHOULD CLARIFY SOMEWHERE THAT IT -- THAT THIS ATTORNEY WOULD WORK WITH THE CRB, NOT WITH THE POLICE DEPARTMENT. IT'S AN OBVIOUS THING, BUT WE HAD FOUR YEARS OF SETTLEMENTS SIGNED OUTSIDE THE CHARTER REQUIREMENTS. YOU NEVER KNOW WHAT WILL HAPPEN.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN GUDES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: I DON'T UNDERSTAND. I JUST DON'T UNDERSTAND. YOU HAVE A LEGAL DEPARTMENT THAT IS HERE RIGHT NOW. YOU HAVE A LEGAL DEPARTMENT UPSTAIRS RIGHT NOW. HOW MANY TIMES HAVE WE BEEN HERE AND THEY MADE A CHANGE AND BROUGHT THE ORDINANCE RIGHT BACK DOWN THE SAME DAY AND WE MAKE THE CORRECTIONS THE SAME TIME. HOW MANY TIMES HAVE WE DONE THAT? I DON'T UNDERSTAND THIS TIME FRAME BECAUSE IF SHE'S MAKING THE CHANGES NOW, BY THE TIME WE GET DOWN TO PROBABLY THE LAST CHARTER, NUMBER 23 OR WHATEVER, THEY CAN HAVE THAT BACK TO US WITH THAT CHANGE.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: THAT MAY BE TRUE, SIR. BUT IN THIS CASE, AN OUTSIDE ATTORNEY'S FIRM WAS RETAINED TO BE ABLE TO DO THIS, NOT THE LEGAL DEPARTMENT.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: EXCUSE ME. YOU CAN'T TELL ME SHE DOESN'T HAVE THE CAPABILITY --

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: THEY ARE ACROSS THE STREET. I'M NOT DISAGREEING OR DISPUTING WHAT YOU'RE SAYING. I'M SAYING THE LOGISTICS -- CLEAR ON IT. THE CLERK HAD ANOTHER VERY INTERESTING WAY WE MAY BE ABLE TO HANDLE THAT. IF WE READ THE TITLE AND DONE THIS RECENTLY, READ THE TITLE AS CORRECTED, THE CHANGES COULD BE MADE BETWEEN FIRST AND SECOND READING TO COMPORT IT, IS THAT CORRECT?

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: WHAT I'LL TELL YOU IS NORMALLY WHEN YOU HAVE AN ORDINANCE AND YOU GO AHEAD AND YOU PUBLISH THE AGENDA TO THE PUBLIC AND IT'S A FIRST READING AND YOU CHANGE THAT ORDINANCE, SO LONG AS THE CHANGES YOU MAKE ARE NOT SIGNIFICANT ENOUGH TO CHANGE THE TITLE BLOCK, YOU DON'T HAVE A PROBLEM MOVING FORWARD. WHEN YOU CHANGE THE TITLE BLOCK IS NORMALLY WHEN YOU HAVE TO COME BACK AND KIND OF START ANEW WHERE YOU HAVE YOUR FIRST READING AGAIN AND THEN YOUR SECOND READING.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN CARLSON.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: NEVER MIND. SORRY.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN VIERA.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH, SIR. JUST REALLY QUICKLY, I SAY WE GO WITH THE ADVICE OF COUNSEL. WE EITHER CHANGE SOMETHING THAT CHANGES THE TITLE, WHICH -- UNLESS IF I MISUNDERSTOOD COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK, SHE WISHED TO GO AS WE HAVE IT NOW, OR WE CHANGE IT AND WE WAIT ANOTHER WEEK. WE HAVE LAND USE HEARINGS IN TWO AND A HALF HOURS. SO I SUGGEST THAT WE GO. I DEFER TO THE ADVICE ON COUNCIL -- ADVICE OF COUNSEL ON THE CHOICES WE HAVE.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: IF THIS IS PART OF THE TITLE BLOCK, THEN THAT IS -- THE PROBLEM IS SEVERAL OF THESE ORDINANCE HAVE STUFF IN THE TITLE BLOCK THAT SHOULDN'T BE THERE. THAT'S QUITE HONESTLY AS I'M LOOKING THROUGH THESE AND AS WE WILL BE DISCUSSING THEM, THERE ARE QUITE A FEW OTHER THINGS THAT SHOULDN'T BE IN THE TITLE BLOCK. WE'RE GOING TO HAVE TO MAKE SOME CHANGES ANYWAY. SO THE ISSUE IS --

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MR. SHELBY.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: IF I COULD FINISH THAT SENTENCE AND NOT TO INTERRUPT. BUT THE ISSUE IS CAN THESE CHANGES BE MADE AND DONE IN A FORM OR FASHION THAT COMPORTS WITH THE FLORIDA LAW THAT REQUIRES HOW THIS WILL BE PASSED AND ADOPTED, IF THAT'S COUNCIL'S PLEASURE, ON SECOND READING AND MAKE PUBLICATION. SO WHY DON'T WE PROCEED AND THEN WE CAN SEE HOW THINGS CAN WORK LOGISTICALLY. YOU MENTIONED NEXT WEEK'S MEETING. NEXT WEEK IS A CRA MEETING UNLESS YOU HAVE SOMETHING THAT YOU WANT TO DO A SPECIAL CALL OR ADD TO THE NIGHT MEETING AGENDA. THERE ARE MANY WAYS TO ADDRESS THIS. BUT LET'S SEE WHAT COUNCIL'S PLEASURE IS AND WE WILL SEE WHAT WE CAN DO TO MAKE IT HAPPEN.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN CARLSON.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: CONSIDERING HOW THESE DATES HAVE BEEN PUSHED AND PUSHED AND PUSHED, I WOULDN'T -- I WOULD PREFER NOT TO PUSH IT AGAIN. I DON'T WANT TO PUT LANGUAGE THAT MIGHT BE MISLEADING OR CONFUSING IN THERE. BUT CONSIDERING THAT THE CHARTER WAS CHANGED SUBSTANTIALLY WITH TWO PARAGRAPHS IN A 2018 MEMO BY A CITY ATTORNEY, I WOULD REQUEST -- YOU MADE A MOTION ON THIS, RIGHT? MAYBE I WOULD ASK FOR YOU TO AMEND YOUR MOTION TO ADD THAT YOU WOULD REQUEST -- IF YOU INCLUDE THE TITLE BLOCK THE WAY IT IS, THAT YOU WOULD ADD TO IT A REQUEST THAT THE CITY -- THE CURRENT CITY ATTORNEY WRITE US A MEMO FOR THE FILE THAT WOULD SAY THAT HER INTERPRETATION IS THAT THIS ATTORNEY WORKS FOR THE CRB, NOT FOR THE POLICE DEPARTMENT. BECAUSE SOME FUTURE CITY ATTORNEY MAY MISREAD THAT THE WAY OTHERS HAVE.

segments.speaker: ANDREA ZELMAN
segments.text: IF I MAY, THE NAME OF THE BOARD IS THE CITIZEN REVIEW BOARD FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT. FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT MODIFIES CITIZEN REVIEW BOARD. IT DOESN'T MODIFY WHO THE ATTORNEY WORKS FOR. THE ATTORNEY WORKS FOR THE CITIZEN REVIEW BOARD FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT. THAT'S THE NAME OF THE BOARD. WRAPPED AROUND THE AXLE ON SOMETHING ALREADY IN THERE. THAT IS THE NAME OF THE BOARD.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: I THINK IT WOULD BE HELPFUL TO PUT IT IN WRITING SO SOMEBODY IN THE FUTURE COULD REFER TO IT. WE HAVE SO MANY WHAT I WOULD CALL MISINTERPRETATIONS LIKE THE LINE THAT SAYS SOMETHING LIKE THE CITY ATTORNEY HAS THE FINAL WORD ON LEGAL OPINIONS. AND THAT'S BEEN MISCONSTRUED TO PRETEND -- TO IMPLY THAT THE CITY ATTORNEY IS THE SUPREME COURT OF THE CITY AND MAKES JUDGMENTS AND RULINGS, AND THEY ARE JUST LEGAL ADVISORS. THERE'S EMPHASIS THERE ON FINAL OPINION VERSUS LEGAL ADVICE. AND SO I JUST WANT TO MAKE SURE THAT NOBODY IN THE FUTURE MISCONSTRUES THIS BECAUSE A LOT OF POWER HAS BEEN TAKEN AWAY FROM CITY COUNCIL. IT'S NOT ABOUT TAKING POWER AWAY FROM THE ADMINISTRATION. ABOUT TAKING AWAY POWER -- RESTORING POWERS OF CITY COUNCIL AND MAKING SURE NOBODY TAKES POWERS AWAY IN THE FUTURE. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN GUDES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: MR. SHELBY, THIS -- [INAUDIBLE] LIKE I SAID BEFORE, THIS IS WHERE YOU COME IN. I DON'T CARE ABOUT THE CITY ATTORNEY RIGHT NOW. I CARE ABOUT YOU TELLING THIS BOARD AND GIVING US THE DIRECTIVE TO MOVE ON. THAT'S WHAT WE RELY ON YOU FOR. NOT TO BE NICE OR PLAY NICE. THEY ARE GOING TO DO WHAT THEY DO. I NEED YOU TO BE THE COACH TO DO YOUR JOB TO TELL US WHAT YOU BELIEVE MS. HURTAK ARE THE RIGHT DIRECTIVE TO MOVE, FOR THIS LADY TO KNOW WHAT SHE NEEDS DO, FOR THIS COUNCIL TO KNOW WHAT WE NEED TO DO. THAT'S WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR. I'M LOOKING FOR YOUR LEADERSHIP TO SAY, COUNCIL, IN MY OPINION, THIS IS THE ROUTE WE SHOULD GO TO GET WHAT YOU WANT ACCOMPLISHED TO GET IT ON THE BALLOT, WHATEVER NEEDS TO BE MOVED TO GET THE JOB DONE. I NEED YOU TO BE THE COACH.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: YES, SIR. WITH REGARD TO WHAT MS. ZELMAN SAID WITH THE NAME OF THE TITLE BEING IN THERE FOR THE TAMPA POLICE DEPARTMENT, THAT IS SOMETHING THAT I ACTUALLY BROUGHT UP BECAUSE OF THE FACT THAT IS THE TITLE WITHIN THE CITY CODE. THERE IS, AS WAS STATED BY THE CITY ATTORNEY, THE OTHER JURISDICTIONS THAT WERE CITED, MIAMI, KEY WEST, THOSE ARE PERHAPS A DIFFERENT FORM OF GOVERNMENT THAN WE HAVE IN THE CITY OF TAMPA. THAT IS OPERATING UNDER A DIFFERENT CHARTER. OUR CHARTER HAS NOTHING ANYWHERE IN IT NOW THAT USES THE WORD CIVILIAN REVIEW BOARD, CRB. AND THAT COMES FROM HOW THIS BOARD WAS CREATED. AND RATHER THAN, AND IF WE HAD MAYBE MORE OF THE LUXURY OF TIME TO BE ABLE TO DO THIS, IF IT WERE COUNCIL'S PLEASURE AND THE PROCESS WENT THROUGH AND IT WAS VETTED PROPERLY, MAYBE SOMETHING LIKE WHAT YOU WANT TO HAVE, THAT HAVE BEEN CITED BY MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC, RELATING TO A CIVILIAN REVIEW BOARD, MAYBE THE NEXT CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION OR HOWEVER COUNCIL WANTS TO DO IT.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: BUT WHAT ABOUT NOW?

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: NOW I HAVE DIFFERENT VARIABLES THAT I AM CONCERNED WITH. IF YOU WANT TO CHANGE IT, IF YOU FEEL STRONGLY THAT YOU DON'T WANT THAT IN THERE, IF YOU DON'T WANT -- WHAT YOU DON'T WANT TO HAVE IS YOU DON'T WANT TO HAVE ANY MISPERCEPTIONS OR MISCOMMUNICATIONS BY THE PUBLIC. YOU DON'T WANT ANYTHING MISLEADING ON YOUR CHARTER BALLOT OR THE BALLOT SUMMARY. THAT IS THE MOST IMPORTANT THING. YOU'RE ALSO LIMITED JUST BY WAY OF BACKGROUND, THE NUMBER OF WORDS THESE THINGS CAN HAVE IN IT. SO THAT ALL BEING SAID, MY MAIN CONCERN IS THERE IS ONE PUBLICATION IN THE CITY THAT DOES LEGAL ADVERTISING THAT THIS CITY RETAINS. IT PUBLISHES ONE DAY A WEEK. WE'RE IN A HOLIDAY SEASON, AND THAT'S WHY I'M CONCERNED THAT THIS HAS TO BE SUCH THAT IF YOU'RE GOING THROUGH THESE EFFORTS AND YOU WANT TO MAKE IT ON THE BALLOT, WE HAVE TO MAKE SURE THAT WE FOLLOW THAT TIME. MY SUGGESTION IS, MY RECOMMENDATION IS, STATE WHAT IT IS YOU THINK IS NECESSARY AND THEN WE HAVE TO FIGURE OUT HOW WE'RE GOING TO HANDLE THE TIME FRAME TO BE ABLE TO GET IT BACK TO YOU. SO MY SUGGESTION WOULD BE IF THAT LANGUAGE, IF YOU FEEL IT IS MISLEADING AND IF MS. ZELMAN WOULD AGREE WITH THAT, I SAY THEN EVEN THOUGH I BELIEVE IT IS IN THIS SECTION BECAUSE THERE'S NO OTHER SECTION WHERE IT COULD FIT INTO, IT FITS INTO THAT DEPARTMENT. SO MY SUGGESTION IS, AS A COUNCIL, WITH YOUR LEADERSHIP, YOU TELL US WHERE YOU WANT IT TO GO AND IF IT MEANS US HAVING TO WORK WITH BRYANT MILLER AND OLIVE TO GET IT TO YOU IN A TIMELY FASHION, IF IT MEANS CHANGING THE COUNCIL SCHEDULE TO GET IT INTO A TIMELY FASHION, WE WILL DO WHAT IT TAKES IN ORDER TO BE ABLE TO COMPLY WITH FLORIDA LAW TO GET THIS ON THE BALLOT IN A TIMELY FASHION.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: OKAY. THEN I WILL STICK WITH MY MOTION AS STANDS.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: WE HAVE A MOTION MADE BY COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK. SECONDED BY COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO. ANY FURTHER DISCUSSION? ROLL CALL VOTE, PLEASE.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: [INAUDIBLE]

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: NO.

segments.speaker: THE CLERK
segments.text: THE MOTION CARRIED WITH CITRO VOTING NO.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 20. COUNCILMAN CARLSON.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: DOES THAT MEAN WE NEED TO SCHEDULE ANOTHER FIRST READING OR ARE WE OKAY? IF WE SCHEDULE SOMETHING, WE HAVE TO DO IT NOW, TODAY.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: WE'RE NOT AT FIRST READING YET.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: BUT WE COULD HAVE A FIRST READING POSSIBLY ON THE 8th.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: WE COULD. I HAVE NOT TALKED WITH COUNCIL ABOUT IT. WE MAY EVEN BE ABLE TO HAVE A FIRST READING THIS EVENING IF SUCH A THING WERE POSSIBLE. I DON'T KNOW. OR WE COULD HAVE IT NEXT WEEK. BUT WE HAVE NOT GOTTEN TO THAT POINT. AND WE DON'T KNOW WHAT'S AHEAD OF US IN TERMS OF --

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I THINK WE'RE PUTTING THE CART BEFORE THE HORSE HERE.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MS. COWAN.

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: OKAY. THE NEXT ORDINANCE BEFORE YOU IS AMENDING SECTION 2.02, QUALIFICATIONS. AND IT'S ADDRESSING THE TERM LIMITS OF THE CITY COUNCIL MEMBERS. CITY COUNCIL'S MOTION WAS VERY CLEAR ON THIS, TO DELETE THE END OF THE SECTION THEREBY LIMITING ALL MEMBERS OF THE CITY COUNCIL TO SERVE TWO CONSECUTIVE FULL TERMS, ELIMINATING THE ABILITY TO IMMEDIATELY RUN AND SERVE AS A COUNCIL MEMBER FOR A DIFFERENT DISTRICT. I BELIEVE IT WAS REFERRED TO AS DISTRICT HOPPING, AND THIS PROPOSED LANGUAGE REMOVES THAT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: ANY QUESTIONS? COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: SO I WAS THINKING ABOUT THIS, AND I THINK IT WAS MAYBE TEN YEARS AGO OR SO THE COUNCIL MADE A MOTION IF PEOPLE RESIGNED FROM COUNCIL TO RUN FOR OTHER SEATS, AND IF THEY LOST, THEY COULDN'T COME AND JUMP -- YOU KNOW, IF IT WAS A NOVEMBER ELECTION THEY WERE RUNNING FOR, THEY COULDN'T LOSE AND THEN COME BACK FOR THE MARCH ELECTION. THEY WOULD HAVE TO SIT OUT FOUR YEARS. I THINK THIS GOES WITH THAT, ALONG THE LINES OF THAT. WE HAVE TERM LIMITS. WE HAVE TWO FULL CONSECUTIVE TERMS AND THEN A COUNCIL MEMBER IS ALLOWED TO GO TO ANOTHER SEAT IF IT'S OPEN, BUT THIS WOULD LIMIT IT BEGINNING IN 2027. I DON'T SEE ANY HARM OR FOUL IN THIS. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: ANY OTHER COMMENTS OR QUESTIONS? COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: THANK YOU, MR. CHAIRMAN. LET'S SAY YOU HAVE AN OPPORTUNITY TO JUMP SHIP OR GO SOMEWHERE ELSE, WHEREVER YOUR MIND MAY TAKE YOU, YOUR BODY WANTS TO GO, RUN FOR ANOTHER OFFICE OUTSIDE THE CITY OF TAMPA. YOU WANT TO RUN FOR A JUDGE, RUN FOR STATE ATTORNEY, YOU WANT TO RUN FOR COUNTY COMMISSION, YOU WANT TO RUN FOR WHATEVER, THAT IF YOU LEAVE BEFORE YOUR TERM IS ENDING IN THE CITY, THAT'S WHAT IT MEANS, THAT YOU HAVE TO WAIT OUT FOUR YEARS BEFORE YOU CAN RUN. THAT'S WHAT THAT MEANS, THIS SECTION HERE. THIS IS A LITTLE DIFFERENT. AND I'M NOT TALKING ABOUT MYSELF. I'M SAYING THAT IF YOU DO THIS, YOU'LL HAVE VERY FEW INDIVIDUALS THAT UNDERSTOOD WHAP TEN YEARS PAST THE TIME YOU'RE TALKING ABOUT SOMETHING. LEAVE LIKE THE LIBRARY IS EMPTY. YOU GO FOR SOMETHING, NOT GOING TO FIND IT. IN FACT, THERE'S A THING THAT I REALLY BELIEVE IN AND ADMIRE, THAT EVERY FOUR YEARS YOU MAY WANT TO RUN BUT YOU MAY NOT GET ELECTED. THE PUBLIC HAS THE RIGHT TO DO THAT AND THE PUBLIC DOES IT IN A WELL-MANNERED SITUATION. SO I'M NOT GOING TO VOTE FOR THIS. IT'S JUST THE PUBLIC HAS A RIGHT TO VOTE YOU UP OR VOTE YOU DOWN. AND I'M NOT GOING TO TAKE THAT RIGHT AWAY FROM THE PUBLIC.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN CARLSON.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: YEAH, I KNOW THERE WAS SOME DISCUSSION A COUPLE OF MONTHS AGO ABOUT IDEAS THAT HAVE BEEN SUGGESTED DURING THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION AND WERE REJECTED. AND I DID NOT PROPOSE THIS ONE, BUT DURING JUST FOR DISCLOSURE, DURING THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION, I RECOMMENDED FOUR, NO MORE THAN FOUR CONSECUTIVE TERMS. SO THAT WOULD ALLOW SOMEONE TO BE IN THE DISTRICT AND ROLL TO A CITYWIDE. I'M FINE WITH THIS ONE, BUT I WOULD LIKE EITHER THE FOUR TERMS OR TWO TERMS, BECAUSE I LIKE TO SEE TURNOVER OF PEOPLE.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I'M OPEN TO THAT AMENDMENT AS WELL. THAT'S FINE WITH ME. BECAUSE I THINK THAT BALANCES KNOWLEDGE AND TIME. SO I'M ABSOLUTELY FINE WITH THAT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MIRANDA BRINGS UP A GOOD POINT. ELECTIONS HAVE CONSEQUENCES AND THERE ARE ELECTIONS EVERY FOUR YEARS. IF PEOPLE DON'T LIKE THE INDIVIDUAL, THERE IS ANOTHER CANDIDATE. THE OTHER CANDIDATE WINS. I DON'T KNOW.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MR. SHELBY.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: JUST A REMINDER, COUNCIL, AND FOR WHATEVER WEIGHT THIS HAS, I MAKE NO COMMENT. BUT JUST A REMINDER THAT TAMPA IS UNIQUE IN ONE GREAT SENSE; IT DOES NOT HAVE STAGGERED TERMS. I'M NOT FAMILIAR, FRANKLY, WITH ANOTHER JURISDICTION IN THE STATE THAT DOES NOT HAVE STAGGERED TERMS. EVERYBODY IS ON THE BALLOT EVERY FOUR YEARS. WHAT THE PRACTICAL EFFECT MIGHT HAVE IN TERMS OF INSTITUTIONAL MEMORY OR THINGS DOWN THE ROAD, I CAN'T SAY AND I'M NOT GOING TO SPECULATE, BUT JUST SO YOU KNOW THAT IT'S UNLIKE ANY OTHER JURISDICTION THAT I'M FAMILIAR WITH.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: WHEN I HEARD THE WORD, ALL THIS DOES NOT COME FROM A CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION. WE SOME YEARS BACK AND, IN FACT, PUT THE INDIVIDUALS IN THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION, AND IT WAS OVER TEN. I FORGET -- I KNOW THE MAYOR HAD SO MANY, THE COUNCIL HAD SO MANY AND THEN WHATEVER. THEY BROUGHT THE RECOMMENDATIONS TO US. IN THIS CASE, IT'S US BRINGING THE RECOMMENDATIONS TO OURSELVES. AM I CORRECT? SO THAT'S FLAWED IN ITSELF. THAT'S NOT TRANSPARENCY. THAT'S NOT COMING FROM SOMEBODY OTHER THAN THAT US. NOT COMING FROM A GROUP OF PEOPLE THAT WE APPOINTED TO DO A RESPONSIBLE -- AND THEY DID A FANTASTIC JOB. IN FACT, FOUR OF THEM ARE HERE. THREE HERE AND ONE THERE, IF I CAN COUNT TO FOUR. AND THOSE ARE THE THINGS I SEE. SO THAT COMMISSION IS NOT INVOLVED IN THIS. IN ORDER TO HAVE TRANSPARENCY AND CLARITY, SOMEBODY OTHER THAN US DOING THIS. WE'RE DOING THIS OURSELVES. THAT'S IN REVERSE OF WHAT THE WHOLE THING WAS WAY BACK FOUR, FIVE YEARS AGO, IF I REMEMBER CORRECTLY. THAT'S ALL I'M GOING TO SAY.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: I RESPECT WHAT MR. MIRANDA HAS JUST STATED, BUT WHEN THAT COMMISSION WAS PUT IN ORDER, NO CITY COUNCIL MEMBERS WERE ON THAT COMMISSION. SO THEY DON'T UNDERSTAND WHAT WE WENT THROUGH AND THE INFORMATION WE WAS GIVEN AND NOT GIVEN. NOW SITTING AS A CITY COUNCIL MEMBER, I CAN SEE SOME OF THE FLAWS IN THAT PROCESS. AND THERE ARE A FEW. AND NOW SITTING AS THE ACTUAL BOARD MEMBER, SEEING SOME OF THE THINGS WE DIDN'T GET A CHANCE TO REALLY REVIEW ALL OF THE CHARTER, BUT SEEING SOME OF THE INTERPRETATIONS OF HOW WE OPERATE AND THIS BOARD WORKS, WE MISSED SOME THINGS. I'M GLAD THAT I HAD THE OPPORTUNITY TO BE HERE THIS TERM TO SEE, TO BE ABLE TO ARGUE SOME POINTS, BUT, AGAIN, THE CHARTER IS NOT PERFECT. IT DOES HAVE SOME VAGUENESS TO IT AND INTERPRETATIONS TO ONE. SO I THINK BEING CLEAR ON SOME OF THE ISSUES MAKES IT BETTER. SO PEOPLE HAVE A CLEAR UNDERSTANDING OF WHAT IT MEANS VERSUS BROAD PERSPECTIVE. I CAN LOOK AT A POLICE SOP. SOME THINGS ARE CLEAR CUT. SOME THINGS LEFT TO INTERPRETATION. THE WRITER WHO IS GIVING DISCIPLINE OR WHOEVER WRITES. ANYBODY CAN BE DISCIPLINED. DEPENDS ON HOW THE WRITER IS AND HOW THEY PERCEIVE THE PERCEPTION OF IT. NO DIFFERENT THAN SOME OF THE STUFF THAT'S IN THIS CHARTER. SO, AGAIN, I SAT ON THAT BOARD. SEVERAL MEMBERS HERE. MS. ZELMAN SAT ON THE BOARD. I STILL CONTEND THAT SOME OF THE STUFF IN OUR CHARTER WE MISSED AND HAD WE KNOWN WHAT WE KNOW NOW, THINGS MIGHT HAVE BEEN DIFFERENT AND MAYBE WE HAD SOME OF THE CONCERNS FROM THE PREVIOUS COUNCIL THAT TELLS WHAT THEY THOUGHT WE MIGHT ADDRESS SOME OF THE ISSUES. I DON'T KNOW. BUT I THINK WE MISSED A FEW THINGS. BUT GLAD THAT WE ARE LOOKING AT WHAT WE'RE LOOKING AT NOW JUST TO MOVE FORWARD.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK AND THEN WE'LL GO TO COUNCILMAN VIERA.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: SO IF WE WANTED TO FOLLOW WHAT COUNCIL MEMBER CARLSON SAID, WE COULD SIMPLY TAKE AWAY THE LINES THAT ARE GONE THROUGH IN THIS LANGUAGE UP AT THE TOP OF WHAT YOU'RE LOOKING AT AND JUST ADD, TO NOT EXCEED A TOTAL OF 16 YEARS OR FOUR FULL TERMS. SO THE LANGUAGE WE STRUCK THROUGH, EXCEPT FOR THAT PERSON WHO IS REPRESENTED, WE COULD KEEP ALL OF THAT AND THEN JUST ADD TO NOT EXCEED A TOTAL OF 16 YEARS OR FOUR FULL TERMS. WOULD THAT WORK? COULD WE DO THAT INSTEAD?

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: YOU CAN CERTAINLY MAKE THAT CHANGE. IT WILL CHANGE EVERYTHING. YOU'LL HAVE TO GO BACK TO FIRST READING ON THIS AS WE'VE TALKED ABOUT BEFORE.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: BUT THEN DOWN AT THE BOTTOM, THE ONLY ISSUE I HAVE IS THE ACTUAL LANGUAGE WOULD BE CHANGED TO ELIMINATING THE ABILITY OF MEMBERS OF CITY COUNCIL WHO HAVE SERVED FOUR CONSECUTIVE FULL TERMS TO IMMEDIATELY RUN.

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: NO, I THINK THERE WOULD BE SOME OTHER LANGUAGE THAT WOULD BE CHANGED IN THAT BECAUSE WE WANT TO BE REALLY CLEAR TO THE VOTERS WHAT IT WAS VERSUS WHAT IT'S BECOMING. SO THERE WOULD BE SOME ADDITIONAL LANGUAGE PROPOSED FOR THAT.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: COUNCIL MEMBER CARLSON, DOES THAT WORK FOR YOU? DO YOU LIKE THAT AMENDMENT? IF NOT, I CAN TAKE IT OUT. I'M FINE WITH FOUR TERMS, FOR THE INSTITUTIONAL MEMORY OF LIKE COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN CARLSON.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: THE ONLY THING IS THERE ARE SITUATIONS LIKE LOUIS FACED WHERE HE FILLED TWO YEARS OF AN EXISTING TERM. IF HE GOT ELECTED, THE FIFTH TIME, THEN HE WOULD HIT 16 YEARS AND CAPPED OUT IN THE MIDDLE OF A TERM.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: YOU'RE CORRECT. TO NOT EXCEED A TOTAL OF FOUR FULL TERMS.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: FOUR CONSECUTIVE TERMS.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: FOUR FULL CONSECUTIVE TERMS.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN VIERA.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: THE REASON I VOTED FOR THIS THE FIRST TIME, IT MAKES THIS CONSISTENT WITH WHAT APPLIES TO THE MAYOR, AND I THINK THAT'S GOOD. I THINK TO CAP IT AT 16 YEARS WOULD BE MISGUIDED. IT WOULDN'T BE A POISON PILL FOR ME BUT THINK MISGUIDED. THERE IS SOMETHING FOR INSTITUTIONAL KNOWLEDGE. I THINK WHEN YOU ALLOW SOMEONE TO HAVE A FOUR-YEAR BREAK, LIKE WE DO WITH MAYOR, YOU OPEN THE FIELD, WHICH I THINK THE WHOLE IDEA OF TERM LIMITS. THERE'S ALWAYS ARGUMENTS FOR AND AGAINST TERM LIMITS AND WHATNOT. A LOT OF PEOPLE ARGUE FOR IT IN CONGRESS, BUT FOR 34 YEARS, FROM 63 TO 97 WE WERE REPRESENTED IN TAMPA BY SAM GIBBONS, ONE OF THE GREATEST MEMBERS OF CONGRESS AROUND WHO DID SUCH GREAT WORK FOR TAMPA. SO THERE'S ALWAYS GOOD, THINGS ON BOTH SIDES. I THINK TO CAP IT AT 16 YEARS OR WHATEVER THAT MAY BE WOULD BE MISGUIDED, AGAIN, NOT A POISON PILL FOR ME. BUT I DO THINK THAT IS MISGUIDED. WHAT I WAS LOOKING AT, IN SUPPORTING THIS THE FIRST TIME WAS TO HAVE THIS CONSISTENT WITH WHAT WE DO FOR THE EXECUTIVE.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO AND THEN COUNCILMAN MIRANDA -- COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO AND THEN COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: I WAS THINKING OVER THE PAST 60, 70 YEARS. HOW MANY COUNCIL MEMBERS SERVED MORE THAN THE TWO OR THREE TERMS. I CAN ONLY THINK OF FOUR. SAUL-SENA, MIRANDA, COPELAND, AND DUNCAN. COPELAND AND DUNCAN HAVEN'T BEEN IN OFFICE SINCE THE '70s. WHATEVER. YOU KNOW WHAT I'M SAYING. THE CHANCES ARE SLIM -- YOU KNOW WHAT I'M SAYING? AGAIN, ELECTIONS HAVE CONSEQUENCES, AND WE'VE HAD MANY COUNCIL MEMBERS OVER THAT TIME PERIOD AND A VERY SMALL GROUP HAS CONTINUED. IS IT EVEN NECESSARY? I DON'T KNOW.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH, MR. CHAIRMAN. WHEN YOU LOOK AT ELECTIONS, LOOK AT THE CONGRESS AND THE PRESIDENT. THE PRESIDENT CAN ONLY SERVE TWO TERMS, PERIOD. AS I UNDERSTAND IT, YOU CAN SERVE FOUR AND LOSE. I GUESS YOU CAN COME BACK. BUT THE HOUSE, THE SENATE, AND THERE'S WITH THAT KNOWLEDGE STAYS. WHETHER YOU LIKE THEM OR NOT, WHETHER YOU LIKE PELOSI OR NOT, WHETHER YOU LIKE THE GENTLEMAN FROM KENTUCKY OR NOT, THEY ARE THERE. AND LIKE THE NEW SPEAKER COMING UP NOW, THAT'S ANOTHER -- WHOEVER WILL BE PRESIDENT OF THE SENATE, THAT'S THERE. AND THOSE DON'T CHANGE BECAUSE THE GOVERNMENT CAN CHANGE, BUT THAT'S THE LEADER. THE REST OF THEM THEY HAVE TO NEGOTIATE. SAME THING WE DO HERE IN A WAY. SO WE WANT TO CHANGE IT AND THAT'S WHY I'M NOT GOING TO SUPPORT THIS. NEVER WENT BEFORE THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMITTEE THAT I KNOW NOW. WE'RE MAKING CHANGES NOT QUITE FOUR YEARS OUT WITH THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION MADE THE RECOMMENDATION TO US AND SUPPOSED TO BE OPEN ONCE EVERY TEN YEARS, IF I RECALL. I HAVEN'T READ IT, BUT THAT'S WHAT I RECALL. NOW WE'RE DOING IT IN THREE AND A HALF YEARS, AND THAT'S WHAT -- THERE'S NO CHARTER COMMISSION. WE'VE TAKEN OVER THAT. NOT DIRECTLY, BUT INDIRECTLY.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN CARLSON.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: JUST A COMMENT ON THAT. THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION CREATED THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION TO COME BACK, SO THE PROCESS BEFORE THAT WAS CITY COUNCIL WOULD MODIFY AND THE LAST CITY COUNCIL, WHICH SEVERAL OF YOU SERVED ON CREATED THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION. SO WE'RE ACTUALLY FOLLOWING THE ORIGINAL RULES, NOT THE ONES CREATED BY THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION. AND WE HAPPEN TO HAVE UNIQUE EXPERTISE. I WON'T REVISIT ALL THE THINGS THAT I HAD PROPOSED, BUT THE INTENT OF WHAT I WAS SAYING A MINUTE AGO ABOUT THE FOUR CONSECUTIVE TERMS IS IF YOU CHANGE THIS AND JUST TAKE OUT TWO AND PUT FOUR, THEN ALL IT WOULD SAY IS AFTER FOUR CONSECUTIVE TERMS YOU HAVE TO SIT OUT FOR FOUR YEARS. SO IT DOESN'T -- I THINK THE PROPOSAL I HAD SEVERAL YEARS AGO WITH THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION WAS FOUR TERMS TOTAL EVER. BUT IF WE WERE GOING TO MODIFY THIS, WHAT I WOULD PUT IS THAT IT WOULD BE FOUR CONSECUTIVE FULL YEAR TERMS AND THE REST SAME. AFTER FOUR CONSECUTIVE TERMS, SIT OUT FOR FOUR YEARS BEFORE THEY CAN RUN AGAIN. I DON'T CARE. I DON'T PLAN ON RUNNING MORE THAN TWO ANYWAY.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I'M FINE WITH KEEPING IT AS-IS OR DOING FOUR. IT'S REALLY THE PLEASURE OF MY COLLEAGUES. I AGREE WITH COUNCILMAN CARLSON. I'M FINE WITH EITHER.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: ANY OTHER COMMENTS OR QUESTIONS? I JUST HAVE TO MAKE THIS STATEMENT. I MEAN TO OFFEND NO ONE, ESPECIALLY OUR OUTSIDE INDEPENDENT ATTORNEY THAT IS HERE WITH US. THERE ARE FOUR MEMBERS ON THIS COUNCIL THAT SERVED ON THE CHARTER REVIEW, ESPECIALLY WITH OUR CITY ATTORNEY THAT SERVED ON THE CHARTER REVIEW. 13 MONTHS -- 13 MONTHS WE SPENT DELIBERATING ON THE CHANGES THAT THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION PUT TOGETHER. AS WE'VE ALREADY SEEN HERE, WE'RE MAKING CHANGES NOW AS WE'RE GOING. I'M FEELING THAT THIS ALSO MIGHT BE RUSHED. AGAIN, I MEAN NOTHING -- NO OFFENSE TO ANYBODY, ESPECIALLY OUR OUTSIDE INDEPENDENT COUNSEL THAT WE HAVE NOW. WE HAVE THE MAYOR. WE HAVE CITY COUNCIL, ALL ELECTED AT THE SAME TIME. I'VE HEARD MANY TIMES, WOULDN'T IT BE GREAT TO HAVE A NEW MAYOR AND ALL NEW CITY COUNCIL. WE CAN START AFRESH. YOU'RE GOING TO HAVE 8 PEOPLE WHO HAVE NOTHING, NO KNOWLEDGE OF INSTITUTIONAL WITHIN THE CITY. AND THAT MAYOR MAY COME IN AND WIPE OUT THE WHOLE STAFF, WHICH WOULD MEAN YOU'RE GOING TO HAVE TO HAVE AT LEAST TWO YEARS OF GAINING THAT INFORMATION. I CAN'T SUPPORT THIS AS WRITTEN. I COULD SUPPORT THE FOUR TERMS, BUT NOT THIS. WHAT IS THE PLEASURE OF COUNCIL?

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: MY MOTION WILL BE TO STRIKE THE STRIKES AND ADD TO NOT EXCEED A TOTAL OF FOUR FULL CONSECUTIVE TERMS. AND THEN THE LANGUAGE AT THE BOTTOM WOULD JUST CHANGE SERVE FOUR CONSECUTIVE FULL TERMS TO TWO CONSECUTIVE FULL TERMS TO FOUR CONSECUTIVE FULL TERMS.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: WE HAVE A MOTION MADE BY COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK. SECONDED BY COUNCILMAN CARLSON. ROLL CALL VOTE.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: NO.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: NO.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: NO.

segments.speaker: THE CLERK
segments.text: THE MOTION CARRIED WITH MANISCALCO, MIRANDA, AND CITRO VOTING NO.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 21.

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: THE NEXT ITEM BEFORE YOU IS AN ORDINANCE ADDRESSING SECTION 9.01, AND THIS IS THE CREATION OF STANDING BOARDS. BASICALLY, THE ONLY CHANGE IN THIS ORDINANCE WAS CLARIFYING THAT THE STANDING BOARD WILL BE CREATED BY CITY COUNCIL BY ORDINANCE WITHOUT THE MAYOR'S RECOMMENDATION.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH. I THINK THIS GOES BACK TO THE SUMMER OF 2015 WHEN THE ISSUE OF CREATING THE CITIZENS REVIEW BOARD THAT WE DISCUSSED EARLIER TODAY, AND THERE WAS DISCUSSION BACK AND FORTH BETWEEN THE CITY ATTORNEY, THE CITY COUNCIL ATTORNEY, THE CITY COUNCIL AND THE MAYOR REGARDING WHO HAS THE AUTHORITY TO CREATE BOARDS. AND THE OPINIONS WE GOT, FROM WHAT I REMEMBER, NOW SEVEN AND A HALF YEARS AGO, WAS THAT ONE OF THE OPINIONS WAS THAT THE CITY COUNCIL HAD THE AUTHORITY TO CREATE THE BOARD. THE MAYOR AT THE TIME DISAGREED WITH THAT PER THE RESPONSE FROM THE CITY ATTORNEY THAT ONLY THE MAYOR HAD THE AUTHORITY TO CREATE THE BOARD. IT BECAME A HEATED DISCUSSION. I THINK WE CAME TO A STALEMATE AND THE BOARD WAS CREATED BUT IT CAME DOWN TO THE MAYOR AND THE CITY COUNCIL AS TO HOW MANY APPOINTEES TO THAT BOARD THEY WOULD HAVE IN ORDER TO KEEP IT BALANCED. WE CAME TO AN AGREEMENT. WE'VE AMENDED THAT SINCE, AND NOW WE'RE TALKING ABOUT THE CRB IN REGARDS TO INDEPENDENT ATTORNEY NOW. SO THIS IS A SEVEN AND A HALF YEAR DISCUSSION, BUT FROM WHAT I REMEMBER, THERE WERE DIFFERING OPINIONS. ONE ATTORNEY SAID THAT WE HAD THE AUTHORITY. THE CITY ATTORNEY SAID THAT, NO, AND THAT ONLY THE MAYOR COULD CREATE THE BOARD. I'LL WAIT TO HEAR WHAT OTHER COUNCIL MEMBERS HAVE TO SAY ABOUT THIS, BUT THAT'S WHAT I REMEMBER AND MY UNDERSTANDING.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: ANYONE ELSE? COUNCILMAN GUDES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: WELL, MY UNDERSTANDING OF THIS NOW, THIS COUNCIL DOESN'T NEED THE MAYOR'S APPROVAL TO CREATE A BOARD. WE CAN DO IT BY ORDINANCE. AM I RIGHT, MR. SHELBY?

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: THE FULL ANSWER IS UNDER THE HOME-RULE AUTHORITY, YOU AND YOUR LEGISLATIVE ABILITY HAVE THE RIGHT TO CREATE AN ORDINANCE. THE QUESTION IS, WHAT IS THE MAYOR'S REMEDY IF SHE DOES NOT LIKE THAT ORDINANCE? THE ANSWER IS IN THE CHARTER AND THAT IS THE VETO. AND THERE IS A PROCESS FOR A VETO AND THERE IS A PROCESS FOR IN THE EXERCISE OF ITS LEGISLATIVE AUTHORITY IF IT DISAGREES WITH THE MAYOR'S VETO, CITY COUNCIL CAN OVERRIDE IT. THERE'S BEEN A QUESTION OF INTERPRETATION AS TO WHAT THIS CHARTER -- EXCUSE ME, WHAT THIS PROVISION IN THE CHARTER DOES. DOES IT THEN LIMIT THE ABILITY OF THE CITY COUNCIL TO CREATE A BOARD WITHOUT THE PRIOR APPROVAL, THE PRE-APPROVAL, IN A SENSE, OF THE MAYOR, BUT EVEN AFTER THAT CASE, THE MAYOR STILL HAS THE RIGHT TO VETO IT. OR DOES IT MEAN THAT, FOR INSTANCE, THIS IS UNDER A SECTION OF 9.01, IS THAT THE SECTION? THAT'S UNDER THE SECTION OF BOARDS. IT DOES NOT SAY CITY COUNCIL BOARDS. IT DOES NOT SAY MAYORAL BOARDS, BUT THE QUESTION IS, DOES THAT APPLY EQUALLY TO THE MAYOR AND TO THE CITY COUNCIL? AND THERE WAS QUESTION AS TO WHETHER IT DID AND THERE WAS A QUESTION -- AND THIS WAS RAISED IN I BELIEVE 2018, WAS IT? WHAT THE INTERPRETATION OF THAT SENTENCE WAS AND HOW WAS IT APPLIED? AND THAT WAS THE SUBJECT OF AN INTERPRETATION QUESTION. AND THIS WAS A PROPOSED FIX TO BE ABLE TO MAKE IT CLEAR AS TO WHAT THE AUTHORITY IS RELATED TO STANDING BOARDS AND AD HOC BOARDS.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: AGAIN, GOING BACK TO MY QUESTION, WHAT I'M READING RIGHT NOW, THIS PROVISION RIGHT NOW, IF PASSED TODAY, THIS COUNCIL HAS THE AUTHORITY TO PUT A STANDING BOARD IN PLACE AS ORDINANCE, CORRECT? I NEED MY YES-NO ANSWER. IF IT'S NOT CLEAR, THEN THAT'S THE POINT. LET'S MAKE IT CLEAR IF WE'RE GOING TO PASS IT. I DON'T WANT TO PASS SOMETHING AND STILL WE'RE NOT CLEAR ON THE INTERPRETATION.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: WELL, WHO HAS THE FINAL SAY TO THE INTERPRETATION IN THE CITY IS THE QUESTION? IS IT THE VOTERS OR IS IT -- THE QUESTION ULTIMATELY IS --

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: I WANT TO MAKE SURE. IF I PUT SOMETHING TO THE VOTERS, THE VOTERS KNOW WHAT THEY ARE CHECKING, YES, NO, SAYING THIS COUNCIL HAS THE AUTHORITY TO MAKE ANY BOARD THEY WANT IN THE CITY BY ORDINANCE. THAT'S WHAT I'M ASKING. I WOULDN'T CARE IF THE MAYOR DISAGREED WITH MY BOARD OR NOT, IF THIS COUNCIL WANTED TO ENACT SOME TYPE OF BOARD, AND WE PASSED IT, HE OR SHE COULD VETO ALL DAY LONG, BUT THIS BOARD COMES BACK WITH A FIVE VOTE COUNT POINT, THAT BOARD PASSES, CORRECT?

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: THAT WOULD BE MY INTERPRETATION. THAT MIGHT BE A QUESTION YOU WANT TO POSE TO THE CITY ATTORNEY.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: I DON'T NEED TO POSE IT TO THE CITY ATTORNEY RIGHT NOW. IF THIS BOARD RIGHT NOW, THIS YOUNG LADY RIGHT HERE TO WRITE OUR CHARTER ORDINANCE TO PUT ON THE BALLOT, IS IT STANDING? IF YOU CAN'T ANSWER THAT -- MA'AM, I'LL ASK YOU THAT QUESTION. IF THIS BOARD IS ASKING YOU TO WRITE IN THE CHARTER FOR THE VOTERS THAT THIS COUNCIL HAS THE RIGHT TO ENACT ANY BOARD WE WANT IN ORDINANCE AND THE VOTERS VOTED THAT, WOULD THAT BE THE STANDING INTERPRETATION?

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: AS THE LANGUAGE IS CHANGED, IT WOULD BE THAT, YES, THAT THE BOARD COULD BE CREATED BY ORDINANCE.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN --

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: YES, THE BOARD WOULD BE CREATED BY ORDINANCE AND THEN HOWEVER YOUR ORDINANCE PROCEEDS. I'M NOT FAMILIAR WITH YOUR VETO POWERS AND ALL OF THAT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: I THINK I CAN CLARIFY. I HOPE I CAN CLARIFY. SO MY OPINION, THIS IS NO ATTORNEY'S OPINION. WHAT I'VE UNDERSTOOD FROM BEING HERE IN 2015 WHEN THIS DISCUSSION CAME UP, CITY COUNCIL HAS THE AUTHORITY TO CREATE THE BOARD. THE MAYOR HAS THE AUTHORITY TO VETO THAT. WITH A FIVE VOTE SUPERMAJORITY, THE CITY COUNCIL CAN OVERRIDE THE VETO. WHAT HAPPENED IN THAT SITUATION WAS THE LAST ADMINISTRATION NEVER USED THE VETO POWER. IN THE SITUATION WITH THIS BOARD, AN AGREEMENT WAS MADE. THE MAYOR PROPOSED WHO, WHAT AND WE SETTLED IT. WHY? BECAUSE I SPOKE TO THE MAYOR. I KNOW SOMETIMES I WASTE MY TIME IN MEDIATING, BUT I WANTED TO BRING IT TO A CLOSE. I SPOKE TO THE MAYOR. I REMEMBER HE CALLED ME. WHAT ABOUT IF, YOU KNOW, YOU APPOINT THIS MANY. WHAT ABOUT THAT? I SAID I'M SURE COUNCIL COULD GO WITH THAT AND SETTLED IT. IT NEVER GOT TO THAT VETO, OVERRIDING THE VETO ANYWAYS. SO WE DON'T KNOW WHAT WOULD HAVE HAPPENED BECAUSE IT NEVER GOT THERE. WE REACHED A SETTLEMENT. BUT THE POINT I'M TRYING TO MAKE IS, I DON'T THINK THIS IS NECESSARY. BECAUSE FROM SPEAKING WITH A VARIETY OF ATTORNEYS AT THE TIME, MR. SHELBY, THE CITY ATTORNEY THAT WAS THERE AT THE TIME, CITY COUNCIL HAS THE AUTHORITY TO CREATE THE BOARDS. WE DON'T NEED TO DO THIS.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: YOU'RE SAYING IT'S NOT NECESSARY BECAUSE YOU NEVER EXERCISED YOUR POWER BECAUSE YOU FELT WHAT -- I WAS HERE THAT WHOLE TIME WHEN IT ALL WENT ON, THAT THIS BOARD AND THE INTERPRETATION OF MS. MANDELL WAS THE MAYOR HAD ALL THE POWER TO DO WHATEVER. THAT'S WHAT I INTERPRETED FROM MS. MANDELL AT THAT PODIUM. YOU MAY HAVE MADE A BACK DEAL WITH THE LAST MAYOR. MS. MANDELL AT THAT PODIUM SAID THE MAYOR HAD THE AUTHORITY AND THIS COUNCIL DID NOT. AM I RIGHT, MR. SHELBY?

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: THAT'S MY RECOLLECTION.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: CORRECT. TO SAY WE DON'T NEED IT. MR. MANISCALCO, I DISAGREE. THAT'S WHY I ASKED THIS YOUNG LADY WHAT IS THE INTERPRETATION FROM THE ACTUAL, HER VERBIAGE. THAT WAY THERE'S NEVER ANY DOUBT ABOUT IF THIS BOARD HAS THE AUTHORITY OR NOT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN CARLSON.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: YEAH, THAT'S THE WAY I REMEMBER IT, TOO, MS. MANDELL SAID THAT -- AND I WAS AN ACTIVIST THEN, BUT ONLY THE MAYOR HAD THE RIGHT TO IN WHATEVER WAY CREATE OR APPROVE THE CREATION OF COMMITTEES. AND THEN THEY HIRED AN OUTSIDE ATTORNEY, GLEN YOUNG, WHO WAS A GREAT PERSON. BUT ATTORNEYS, THIS IS WHY I HAD THE WHOLE DISCUSSION IN THE CHARTER ABOUT ATTORNEYS. IF YOU HIRE AN ATTORNEY, THE ATTORNEY WORKS FOR YOU. AND TECHNICALLY THE CITY ATTORNEY WORKS FOR CITY COUNCIL AND THE MAYOR. IT'S DESCRIBED IN THAT DETAIL IN THE CHARTER. BUT IN REALITY, ESPECIALLY AT THAT TIME, THE ATTORNEYS DIDN'T. AND I DON'T THINK THEY DID ANYTHING WRONG. I THINK THEY WERE TOLD DO CERTAIN THINGS. MARTY'S OPINION AT THE TIME WAS THAT IT WAS OUR POWER AND COULD DO IT. THEIR OPINION WASN'T. THEY HIRED AN OUTSIDE ATTORNEY AS IF THAT AGAIN WAS THE SUPREME COURT, AND IT'S NOT. THAT'S THE KIND OF SITUATION WHY WE NEED A CONFLICT RESOLUTION PROCESS, BECAUSE WE MAY BE HEADED TO ANOTHER ONE OF THOSE AGAIN. HOPEFULLY, MS. ZELMAN HAS A DIFFERENT VIEW THAN SOME OF HER PREDECESSORS WHO MISINTERPRETED THE CHARTER. IN THIS CASE WE ABSOLUTELY NEED THIS. THERE ARE SO MANY MISINTERPRETATIONS OF THE CHARTER, WHETHER CITY ATTORNEYS IN THE PAST WERE BULLIED, WHATEVER, THEY MISINTERPRETED THE CHARTER AND IT'S PUT THE CITY AT RISK OF LAWSUITS AND LIABILITY, AND WE NEED TO MAKE SURE THAT WE CAP SOME OF THESE HOLES AND MAKE IT SPECIFIC. SO THIS DOESN'T DO ANY HARM. IT DOESN'T TAKE AWAY ANYTHING FROM THE STRONG MAYOR FORM OF GOVERNMENT. IT JUST CODIFIES WHAT IS THE REALITY FROM THE POINT OF VIEW OF THE CITY COUNCIL. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: THANK YOU. I LOOK BACK, TRYING TO FIND IN MY MIND HOW MANY TIMES A CITY COUNCIL HAS OVERRIDDEN THE MAYOR IN A 5-2 VOTE. I CAN REMEMBER ONCE. I'M TRYING TO THINK IT OUT HOW MANY OTHER TIMES, MAYBE SOME OTHER PLACE I REMEMBER. WHAT I'M TRYING TO SAY IS THAT WE'RE LOOKING HERE TO CHANGE THE POWER OF WHAT WE HAVE IN THE MAYOR'S OFFICE. I'M NOT SAYING THAT WE'RE RIGHT OR WRONG. I UNDERSTAND BOTH SIDES. BUT IF YOU WANT A STRONG MAYOR OF GOVERNMENT WHERE ONE PERSON IS RESPONSIBLE FOR THE ADMINISTRATIVE STUFF AND SEVEN INDIVIDUALS FOR THE LEGISLATIVE STUFF, THEN YOU HAVE TO HAVE -- MAKES YOU WORK TOGETHER. AND IN THE FUTURE, IT MAY BE SOMETHING THAT THE COUNCIL AND THE MAYORS CAN WORK OUT TOGETHER, CANNOT WORK OUT TOGETHER AND THE COUNCIL MAY WANT TO CREATE ALL KIND OF BOARDS WITHOUT THE MAYOR'S PERMISSION, BLESSING, WHATEVER YOU WANT TO CALL IT. BUT THAT MAYOR STILL HAS VETO POWER. IT'S ONLY BEING USED, I REMEMBER, AND YOU CAN CHECK HISTORY, ONE TIME, IT WAS IN 1974. AND IT WAS A RAISE BETWEEN THE FIRE DEPARTMENT AND THE ADMINISTRATION. AND IT WAS SETTLED ON THE COUNCIL OVERRIDING THE MAYOR. THAT WAS THE ONLY TIME I CAN REMEMBER. I'M NOT SAYING THERE'S NOT ANOTHER TIME, BUT THAT IS THE ONLY ONE THAT I REMEMBER. IT WAS LOCAL 755 AND SAM WAS THE PRESIDENT; ARTHUR WAS THE VICE PRESIDENT. I CAN TELL YOU WHAT HAPPENED. THE OFFICE OF THE CLERK USED TO BE THE MAYOR'S OFFICE. NOBODY REMEMBERS THAT. I HATE TO TELL YOU THIS, THERE WAS NO PHONE BACK HERE. THERE WERE NO OFFICES FOR COUNCIL MEMBERS. THERE WAS A SLOT, LIKE A PIGEON COMES IN. THAT'S WHERE YOU GOT YOUR MAIL AND ONE PHONE. THAT WAS DIFFERENT TIMES. ONLY ONE TIME I CAN REMEMBER WAS THE COUNCIL EVER OVERRIDING THE MAYOR. AND MAYBE THERE WAS ANOTHER TIME ON THE BUDGET, BUT I THINK IT WAS SETTLED. I'M NOT SURE. THANK YOU VERY MUCH. BUT I'M NOT GOING TO SUPPORT ANYTHING THAT LEAVES THE MAYOR OUT BECAUSE IT STARTS TO WITHER AWAY AND STARTS WITH TWO DIFFERENT BODIES NOT COMMUNICATING WITH EACH OTHER AND I DON'T WANT THE PUBLIC TO GO THROUGH THAT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MR. SHELBY.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: I'M CONCERNED THAT THERE IS THE OPPORTUNITY FOR MISINTERPRETATION HERE. I THINK WHAT THIS DOES IS GOES TO WHAT COUNCILMAN GUDES HAD TO SAY WITH ENSURING, AND YOU WERE ABLE TO DO THAT WITH DIRECT QUESTIONING OF INDEPENDENT OR AN OUTSIDE COUNSEL AS TO WHAT THE INTERPRETATION WOULD BE. THIS CAME TO FORE WHEN THE CITY ATTORNEY PREPARED THIS LAST TIME AROUND FOR CHANGES TO THE CRB, AND PREPARED A MEMO FOR COUNCIL WHICH WAS RESPONDED TO BY AN ATTORNEY FOR THE ACLU. AND IN THAT DISCUSSION, THERE WAS A DECISION ABOUT HOW THIS PARTICULAR PROVISION WAS CHANGED. MY CONCERN AS CITY COUNCIL ATTORNEY, WITH REGARD TO THE CREATION OF THE BOARD TO GO BACK TO 2015 TO CLARIFY WHAT COUNCILMAN CARLSON WAS TALKING ABOUT WAS THAT THE ISSUE CAME UP WHEN THE PREVIOUS MAYOR WAS QUOTED IN THE NEWSPAPER AS SAYING THE CITY COUNCIL DOES NOT HAVE THE POWER TO CREATE THIS BOARD AND MY CITY ATTORNEY AGREES WITH ME, OR WORDS TO THAT EFFECT. AND THAT QUESTION IN MY MIND, AS YOUR ATTORNEY, YOUR HOME RULE AUTHORITY TO BE ABLE TO CREATE AN ORDINANCE AND TO BE ABLE TO CREATE A BOARD BY ORDINANCE BECAUSE THE MAYOR'S REMEDY, AS COUNCILMAN MIRANDA CORRECTLY STATES, IS IN THE VETO. IN THIS SITUATION THERE WAS A PERIOD OF TIME WHEN THERE WAS A GREAT DEAL OF DISCUSSION BETWEEN COUNCIL AND THE CITY ATTORNEY IN WHICH I WAS INVOLVED, DOES THE MAYOR HAVE THE ABILITY TO REMOVE THAT HOME-RULE POWER UNDER THIS CHARTER BY PREEMPTING COUNCIL, BY CREATING AN EXECUTIVE ORDER, DOING SOMETHING BEFORE COUNCIL HAS THE OPPORTUNITY TO ACT, WHICH WAS THE PROCESS IN 2015. BEFORE COUNCIL HAD AN OPPORTUNITY TO ACT, AN EXECUTIVE ORDER WAS CREATED AND THEREFORE THE ARGUMENT WAS MADE THAT COUNCIL LOST ITS LEGISLATIVE HOME-RULE AUTHORITY. THE SECTION THAT I RELIED UPON IN 2015 WAS CHANGED BY THE CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION. THIS SECTION, SECTION 9.01 WAS BOARDS. AND THE QUESTION ULTIMATELY BECAME, BUT FOR THE LACK OF A COMMA OR FOR THE LACK OF A PERIOD AND A CAPITAL STARTING THE NEXT SENTENCE, IT WAS SUBJECT TO TWO DIFFERENT INTERPRETATIONS. AND THAT'S WHY FOR NO OTHER REASON BECAUSE OF THE FACT THAT IT WAS SUBJECT TO INTERPRETATION, IN MY OPINION, THAT THAT INTERPRETATION OR ONE OF THEM COULD HAVE BEEN USED TO INFRINGE UPON WHAT I VIEWED AS COUNCIL'S RIGHTFUL HOME RULE AUTHORITY BY AN INTERPRETATION OF THE CHARTER. IT'S A CLARIFICATION. YOU HEARD WHAT THE CLARIFICATION IS. PLAIN READING BY AN INDEPENDENT ATTORNEY THAT COUNCIL, IT DOESN'T TAKE ANYTHING AWAY FROM THE MAYOR, BUT IT RESTORES TO CITY COUNCIL WHAT UNDER HOME RULE AUTHORITY, EVERY LEGISLATIVE BODY WITHIN THE STATE HAS THE ABILITY TO DO.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: THANK YOU FOR SUCCINCTLY SAYING EXACTLY WHAT I WAS GOING TO SAY. THAT'S THE WHOLE POINT OF THIS. THIS TAKES NO POWER AWAY FROM THE MAYOR.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: NONE.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: BECAUSE THE MAYOR STILL HAS THE POWER TO VETO IT.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: ABSOLUTELY.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: AS COUNCILMAN MIRANDA HAS SHARED WITH US, VERY FEW OVERRIDING VOTES HAVE OCCURRED. SO THIS REALLY IS ABOUT CLARIFICATION. THAT'S WHY I MADE THIS MOTION, AND I AM PRESENTING IT AS IT STANDS RIGHT NOW. FOR APPROVAL.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN VIERA.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: NO. I JUST SAY THAT WE MOVE FORWARD. I SECOND IT. LET'S TAKE A VOTE. I AGREE.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MOTION MADE. ANY FURTHER DISCUSSION? MOTION MADE BY COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK. SECONDED BY COUNCILMAN VIERA. ROLL CALL VOTE.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: NO.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: NO.

segments.speaker: THE CLERK
segments.text: THE MOTION CARRIED WITH MIRANDA AND CITRO VOTING NO. DID YOU READ THE TITLE OF THE ORDINANCE? YOU NEED TO READ THE TITLE OF THE ORDINANCE IF YOU'RE GOING TO ADOPT THE ORDINANCE.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I APOLOGIZE. LET ME READ THE TITLE OF THE ORDINANCE. APOLOGIES. AN ORDINANCE RELATING TO THE GOVERNMENT OF THE CITY OF TAMPA, FLORIDA, SUBMITTING TO THE ELECTORS OF THE CITY A PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE REVISED CHARTER OF THE CITY OF TAMPA OF 1975, AS AMENDED, TO AMEND SECTION 9.01 TO CLARIFY THAT STANDING BOARDS SHALL BE CREATED BY CITY COUNCIL BY ORDINANCE WITHOUT REQUIRING THE MAYOR'S RECOMMENDATION; PROVIDING AN EFFECTIVE DATE.

segments.speaker: THE CLERK
segments.text: MOTION CARRIED WITH MIRANDA AND CITRO VOTING NO. SECOND READING AND ADOPTION WILL BE HELD ON JANUARY 5th, 2023, AT 9:30 A.M.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: JUST ONE FINAL COMMENT. I DON'T THINK THIS MERITS A VETO. AGAIN, IT DOESN'T TAKE AWAY POWER FROM THE MAYOR. IT JUST OFFERS CLARIFICATION. I SAT THROUGH THIS DISCUSSION FROM 2015 ON, AND I APPRECIATE MR. SHELBY'S COMMENTS FROM 2015, BECAUSE I AGREE WITH WHAT HE SAID AT THE TIME. WE AVOIDED A FIGHT. WE WORKED IT OUT TO A CERTAIN DEGREE, BUT THIS DOES NOT MERIT A VETO AND HAVING TO GO THERE. IT DOESN'T HURT ANYBODY. BUT THAT'S JUST MY OPINION. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: THANK YOU. AGENDA ITEM NUMBER 22.

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: YES, BEFORE YOU NOW IS AN ORDINANCE REVISING SECTION 6.03, APPOINTMENTS, AND THIS IS ADDRESSING THE APPOINTMENTS OF CITY DEPARTMENT HEADS. THIS IS CLARIFYING THAT THE MAYOR WILL NOMINATE THE DEPARTMENT HEADS AND COUNCIL WILL APPOINT OR DISAPPROVE THE NOMINATION. IF DISAPPROVED, THEN THE MAYOR WILL SUBMIT ANOTHER NOMINEE. THE MAYOR MAY APPOINT AN EXISTING CITY EMPLOYEE TO SERVE AS AN INTERIM APPOINTMENT FOR A PERIOD OF NOT MORE THAN 180 DAYS.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: SO THIS IS ANOTHER ONE THAT I HAD THOUGHT ABOUT THESE LAST FEW DAYS AND LOOKED AT AND READ THE LANGUAGE HERE. SO FROM MY UNDERSTANDING, THE MAYOR CAN ONLY APPOINT TO A VACANCY FOR A CITY POSITION AN EXISTING CITY EMPLOYEE, IS THAT CORRECT? THE MAYOR HAS TO CHOOSE FROM THE EXISTING ACTIVE CITY EMPLOYEES.

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: THE MAYOR MAY MAKE AN INTERIM APPOINTMENT OF AN EXISTING EMPLOYEE WHO CAN SERVE FOR A PERIOD OF, YES, NOT MORE THAN 180 DAYS.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: BUT THEY CAN ONLY CHOOSE AN EXISTING CITY EMPLOYEE OR THEY CAN PICK ANYBODY FROM WHOEVER THEY CHOOSE TO?

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: THIS LANGUAGE WOULD HAVE THEM -- MAY MAKE APPOINTMENT OF AN EXISTING CITY EMPLOYEE.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: SO MY QUESTION IS THIS. WE HAVE CHIEF OF STAFF JOHN BENNETT IN THE ROOM. JOHN BENNETT WAS EMPLOYED BY THE CITY, RETIRED, AND WENT TO ANOTHER JURISDICTION TO WORK. THEREFORE, THE MAYOR, HAVING COME INTO OFFICE, WOULD NOT HAVE BEEN GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY TO APPOINT JOHN BENNETT AS INTERIM CHIEF OF STAFF BECAUSE HE WASN'T AN ACTIVE CITY EMPLOYEE BECAUSE HE HAD RETIRED. THAT'S THE THING. MY PROBLEM WITH THIS IS, YOU KNOW, ONE OF US BECOMES MAYOR, AND WE HAVE A RIGHT-HAND PERSON THAT IS WHATEVER, OUR PERSON. THERE'S NOBODY BETTER FOR CHIEF OF STAFF POSITION. I'M USING THAT AS AN EXAMPLE. THAT MAYOR CANNOT APPOINT THAT PERSON BECAUSE THEY ARE NOT AN EXISTING CITY EMPLOYEE. EVEN THOUGH THEY ARE THE GREATEST, WHATEVER, BUT IT WOULD PROHIBIT THE MAYOR FROM DOING THAT. SO A MAYOR WOULD COME IN WITH THE NEW ADMINISTRATION AND NOT REALLY BE ABLE TO APPOINT WHOEVER THEY WOULD WANT TO BECAUSE THEY HAVE TO CHOOSE EXISTING CITY EMPLOYEES. I JUST WANT CLARITY ON THAT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: THAT IS A GREAT QUESTION. ACTUALLY, THIS ONLY APPLIES TO INTERIM. YOU CAN NOMINATE ANYONE YOU WANT FROM, LIKE, THE WHOLE WORLD, BUT THEY ARE SAYING THAT IN THE INTERIM IT SHOULD BE A CITY EMPLOYEE SO THAT YOU CAN'T CHOOSE SOMEBODY, YOU KNOW, THE WORLD'S BEST OF CHIEF OF STAFF FROM FRANCE AND YOU'RE GOING TO BRING THEM OVER, AND THEN YOU'RE GOING TO MAKE THEM AN INTERIM UNTIL THEY ARE VOTED ON, WHICH BASICALLY IS SUBVERTING THE POWER GOING THROUGH COUNCIL.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: OKAY.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: SO IN THE INTERIM, YES, YOU HAVE TO CHOOSE SOMEONE FROM STAFF, BUT YOU CAN STILL HIRE YOUR BEST FRENCH CHIEF OF STAFF, BUT IT HAS TO GO THROUGH APPROVAL OF COUNCIL BEFORE THAT PERSON CAN START WORKING.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: OKAY.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I KNOW THIS WAS MR. GUDES' ORDINANCE. DID I GET THAT RIGHT?

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: IF YOU DON'T MIND, I WOULD LIKE TO GO TO MS. COWAN. WOULD YOU CONCUR WITH WHAT COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK JUST STATED?

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: YES, THAT THE MAYOR CAN NOMINATE ANYONE. THERE'S NO RESTRICTION ON WHO THEY CAN NOMINATE FOR THE HEAD OF DEPARTMENT OR ADMINISTRATIVE PERSONNEL. BUT THOSE THAT THE CITY COUNCIL WILL THEN HAVE TO MAKE THAT APPOINTMENT. THE RESTRICTION BECOMES WITH REGARD TO THE INTERIM APPOINTMENT AS THAT BEING ONLY AN EXISTING EMPLOYEE AND ONLY FOR 180 -- NO MORE THAN 180 DAYS.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN MANISCALCO, DOES THAT ANSWER YOUR QUESTION OR YOUR THOUGHTS?

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: I GUESS SO. SO THE MAYOR REALLY WOULD NOT BE PROHIBITED FROM APPOINTING WHOMEVER THEY WANTED TO.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: I GUESS SO. SO THE MAYOR REALLY WOULD NOT BE PROHIBITED FROM APPOINTING WHOMEVER THEY WANTED.

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: THE MAYOR DOESN'T APPOINT. THEY ONLY APPOINT AN INTERIM. THEY WOULD NOMINATE A PERSON THAT WOULD BE CONSIDERED BY THEN THE CITY COUNCIL FOR APPOINTMENT, AND CITY COUNCIL WOULD EITHER APPROVE THAT APPOINTMENT OR DISAPPROVE IT. IF THEY APPROVE IT, THEY ARE APPOINTED.  IF THEY DISAPPROVE IT, THEN THE MAYOR WOULD NEED TO SEND ANOTHER NOMINEE.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: WOULD THE MAYOR BE ABLE TO DIRECTLY NOMINATE SOMEBODY, THIS IS THE PERSON THAT I WANT, AND BE VOTED -- APPROVED BY COUNCIL OR NOT, CORRECT? SO IF WE HAVE INTERIM THERE, WHATEVER, BUT IF THEY HAVE, YOU KNOW, WHOEVER OF THEIR CHOOSING THAT IS NOT A CITY EMPLOYEE, THEY CAN SAY THIS IS WHO I WANT, THIS IS MY NOMINEE, AND COME TO US. NOW, IF THAT NOMINEE IS SHOT DOWN OR VOTED DOWN, THE MAYOR CANNOT RESUBMIT THAT INDIVIDUAL. CORRECT?

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: AS IT'S WRITTEN NOW, IN WHAT'S BEFORE YOU, THAT'S CORRECT. SO THEY WOULD NEED TO SUBMIT ANOTHER NOMINEE.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: I CAN UNDERSTAND THE ARGUMENT OF, WELL, COUNCIL CAN VOTE THAT PERSON DOWN, AND NOW THEY WON'T HAVE THE OPPORTUNITY TO GET TO KNOW THAT PERSON BETTER, YOU KNOW, FOR A RESUBMISSION OF THAT NOMINATION. IF YOU -- WHOEVER THAT NOMINEE IS, THEY SHOULD GO AROUND AND MEET THE COUNCIL MEMBERS AHEAD OF TIME AND ALL THAT. OKAY, THANK YOU. MY QUESTIONS ARE ANSWERED.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MR. SHELBY, YOU WERE GOING TO SAY SOMETHING?

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: I WOULD RATHER GET OTHER COUNCILMEN.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: THE INTENT OF THIS CHANGE IS SIMPLE. YOU KNOW, YOU LOOK AT OUR FEDERAL AND OUR STATE, YOU ARE A NOMINEE AND NOT APPOINTED, THEY ARE A NOMINEE. AND A NOMINEE, THE PRESIDENT NOMINATES SOMEONE TO GO BEFORE THE SENATE. AND THE SENATE HEARS FROM THAT NOMINEE TO MAKE SURE THAT NOMINEE FITS THE MOLD OF THE SENATE. AND THEN THERE'S A CONFIRMATION FOR APPOINTMENT. SO THE MAYOR CAN NOMINATE ANYBODY THEY SO CHOOSE. WE APPOINT. THAT'S THE ONLY POWER WE HAVE AT THE TIME. WE APPOINT. NOW, IF SOMEONE LEAVES OR RESIGNS, THE MAYOR CAN STILL NOMINATE SOMEBODY, BUT IF YOU ARE TALKING ABOUT WAITING TO NOMINATE TO GET AN APPOINTMENT FOR US, THERE SHOULD BE AN ACTING PERSON THAT CAN RUN THAT DEPARTMENT UNTIL THAT PERSON COMES BEFORE US WITH CONFIRMATION FOR THE APPOINTMENT, WHICH MAKES SENSE. YOU KNOW, YOU CAN'T HAVE SOMEONE ON THE OUTSIDE AND THAT PERSON HAS NOT BEEN APPOINTED TO RUN CITY BUSINESS. THAT MAKES NO SENSE. AT ALL. BUT THE NUMBER TWO PERSON, NUMBER THREE PERSON, WHATEVER, CAN RUN IT UNTIL THAT 90 DAYS. I'M GOING A LITTLE STRETCH WITH 180 DAYS. SO I AM GIVING YOU THAT ONE. NICOLE TRAVIS, ADMINISTRATOR OF DEVELOPMENT AND ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITY. THIS IS A LITTLE DIFFERENT WHEN YOU TALK ABOUT THE PRESIDENT MAKES NOMINATIONS, IT'S THROUGH THE CONSTITUTION FOR THAT PROCEEDING TO HAPPEN THAT WAY. AND SO THERE IS A BIG DIFFERENCE IN OUR GOVERNING DOCUMENTS ON HOW THIS WORKS. BUT I WANT TO TALK TO YOU. I AM NOT HERE TO TALK TO YOU ABOUT YOUR APPOINTMENTS, I AM COMING TO TALK TO YOU FROM A RECRUITMENT PERSPECTIVE, AND A PRACTICAL IMPLEMENTATION OF PERSPECTIVE. YOU COUNCIL MEMBERS HAVE TALKED ABOUT RECRUITING THE BEST AND THE BRIGHTEST. THERE IS NO C SUITE EXECUTIVE THAT WILL TAKE A POSITION THAT HAS TO WAIT ON CONFIRMATION FROM A POLITICAL BODY.  NO ONE WILL DO IT. I WOULD NOT HAVE COME AND WAIT IN THE WINGS -- NOW, I UNDERSTAND WHY THIS HAS COME TO A HEAD, AND I UNDERSTAND WHICH APPOINTMENT YOU ARE TALKING ABOUT AND WHICH -- YOU CANNOT RECRUIT THE BEST AND BRIGHTEST IN POLITICAL POSITIONS.  I WOULD NOT HAVE GOTTEN MY DEPUTY -- WE HAVE BEEN WORKING CLOSELY THE LAST SEVERAL MONTHS. I WOULD NEVER HAVE TAKEN THIS JOB IF MY -- LEAVING A GOOD EMPLOYER THAT HAD A GOOD RELATIONSHIP, CORDIAL RELATIONSHIP TO COME TO WORK, FROM A PRACTICAL STANDPOINT, I AM JUST TALKING TO YOU ABOUT THE IMPLEMENTATION OF WHAT MAY HAPPEN HERE. YOU CANNOT RECRUIT IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT WHERE THAT APPOINTMENT IS DEPENDENT ON A POLITICAL FACTOR. YOU CAN'T.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: WELL, SHE WAS SPEAKING TO ME FIRST.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN GUDES.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: SO I CAN AGREE WITH SOME OF YOUR POINT. I DON'T AGREE WITH ALL OF IT. BECAUSE IF I AM AT A JOB ALREADY, I AM APPLYING FOR ANOTHER JOB, I AM NOT GOING TO LEAVE MY CURRENT JOB UNTIL I KNOW I HAVE GOT THE OTHER JOB. I DON'T THINK ANYBODY IS GOING TO DO THAT. BE IT POLITICAL OR NOT. AND I LOVE YOU TO DEATH. BUT I'M NOT LEAVING MY JOB UNTIL I KNOW I GOT THAT JOB.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: THAT'S NOT HOW IT WORKS IN THE PRACTICAL ENVIRONMENT. I'M TELLING YOU AS A C SUITE EXECUTIVE THAT IS NOT HOW IT WORKS IN THE PRACTICAL IMPLEMENTATION. I KNOW THAT -- IF I KNOW THAT THE JOB IS WAITING IN THE WINGS, AND DEPENDENT ON A POLITICAL BODY, YOU ARE TALKING ABOUT SOMEONE LEAVING THEIR JOB, PUTTING THEMSELVES OUT PUBLICLY. YOU ARE TELLING YOUR EMPLOYER THAT YOU ARE LEAVING AND YOU DON'T HAVE CONFIRMATION, THAT YOU HAVE A JOB, YOU CANNOT RECRUIT, TALKING WITH EXECUTIVES IN THAT COMPANY, YOU CAN'T. JUST CAN'T. EVEN WHEN YOU LOOK AT CITY MANAGER SEARCHES ACROSS THE STATE, WHEN THEY HIRE RECRUITING FIRMS, THERE'S A CERTAIN LEVEL OF ANONYMITY TO A CERTAIN POINT AND THEN IT'S PUBLIC KNOWLEDGE. YOU CAN LOOK AT THOSE CITY MANAGERS THAT APPLY, THAT APPLY FOR THE POSITION THAT DIDN'T GET THE POSITION, AND SEE HOW LONG THEY ARE STILL THERE. YOU ARE PUTTING ON NOTICE YOUR EMPLOYER, I'M LEAVING, AND THEN YOU HAVE NO CERTAINTY OF MOVING FORWARD. SO I AM JUST TELLING YOU, LIKE I AM NOT WAITING -- YOU GUYS KNOW ME. I AM NOT GOING TO WEIGH IN ON THE POLITICS OF IT BUT FROM A PRACTICAL STANDPOINT WHEN YOU SAY YOU WANT TO RECRUIT THE BEST AND THE BRIGHTEST, DAVID INGRAM WHO WAS HERE WITH YOU THIS MORNING WOULD NOT HAVE COME KNOWING THAT THIS -- HE HAS TO COME BEFORE A POLITICAL BODY. AND YOU HAD A RECENT EXPERIENCE OF SOMEONE SEEING WHAT HAPPENS WITH POLITICAL BODIES. YOU HAD A RECENT EXPERIENCE WITH THAT. SO I AM TELLING YOU JUST FROM A PRACTICALITY STANDPOINT, THE REST OF THIS STUFF I AM NOT GETTING TO, BUT THIS ONE I KNOW THAT I HAVE A LOT IN MY PORTFOLIO THAT I RECRUIT FOR THE BEST AND BRIGHTEST THAT HAVE TO BE APPOINTED AND I TRY TO GET IT TO YOU MEDLEY. I PUT THE MEMO OUT AND SAY THIS IS WHO IT IS, THIS IS WHO IS COMING, AND I KNOW YOU GUYS HAVE A LOT OF TRUST AND THE RELATIONSHIP THAT I BUILT, BUT I AM HERE TO TELL YOU THAT THIS WILL BACKFIRE --

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: I JUST BELIEVE IT'S TAKING SOME OF THE COUNCIL -- YOU KNOW, COUNCIL, APPOINT SOMEONE, WE HAVE NO AUTHORITY OVER THAT PERSON, IF WE DON'T LIKE THAT PERSON, AND WHAT THEY DO, WE CAN'T FIRE THEM, WE CAN'T DO NOTHING. SO TO ME TAKING IT AWAY FROM US SAYING WE WANT TO MAKE SURE THAT THE PUBLIC IS OUTRAGED ABOUT SOME THINGS, YOU ARE SAYING FIRST NOT TO BE ABLE TO HAVE A HOME RULE.  I CAN'T SUPPORT THAT.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: BUT THERE IS AN ACCOUNTABILITY. THE MAYOR IS THE EXECUTIVE AND ADMINISTRATIVE ARM OF THIS, SO IN THE WAY THAT OUR GOVERNMENT IS SET UP, YOU HAVE THE ADMINISTRATOR, THE ADMINISTRATIVE ARM, THE CEO, THE BUCK ULTIMATELY STOPS WITH HER. AND SO WHEN SOMEONE IS CALLING, IF SOMEONE IS UPSET WITH THE -- IF SOMEONE IS UPSET WITH MR. DRUMGO OR ALIS OR ABBYE, THEY CALL ME, THEY CALL US. ULTIMATELY, YES, YOU CANNOT DO THE HIRING AND FIRING. YOU HAVE ONE THE CITY ATTORNEY. BUT THAT'S THE STRUCTURE OF GOVERNMENT. AND TO DO THIS, YOU CAN CALL FOR ACCOUNTABILITY. YOU CAN -- I UNDERSTAND. I KNOW, AND WE CAN RESPECTFULLY DISAGREE. IF I CAN'T RECRUIT THE BEST AND BRIGHTEST TO DO THE WORK HERE, THEN I DON'T HAVE THE TOOLS TO BE SUCCESSFUL HERE, AND I'M JUST TELLING YOU FROM THE THINGS THAT YOU WANT TO DO, THAT THIS IS OUR CHALLENGE.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN VIERA.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH, SIR. YEAH, I MEAN, I'M VOTING ON ALL THESE THE WAY I ORIGINALLY VOTED, UNLESS SOMETHING HAPPENS TO CHANGE MY MIND. I VOTED AGAINST THIS FOR A NUMBER OF REASONS, INCLUDING THIS. I BELIEVE THAT ULTIMATELY THIS WILL RESULT IN TOO MUCH OF A BURDEN PLACED ON THE SEARCH PROCESS. THERE'S ALREADY MECHANISMS IN PLACE. I WISH -- AND I UNDERSTAND COUNCILMAN GUDES' CONCERNS WHO MADE THIS VERY LEGITIMATE, VERY REASONABLE, VERY WELL MADE, ET CETERA. I JUST THINK AS PRESENTED WITHIN THE TIME THAT WE HAVE, IT'S FAR TOO EXPANSIVE. YOU HAVE AN EXECUTIVE THAT IS TRYING TO RECRUIT FOLKS, ET CETERA, AND THIS WOULD PLACE BURDENS UPON THAT PROCESS THAT WOULD ULTIMATELY PLAY HORRIBLY WITHIN THE PRIVATE SECTOR OF SOMEONE MAKING A LATERAL PUBLIC SECTOR MOVE. IT'S JUST SOMETHING AGAIN, I SEE THE CHALLENGES BEING ADDRESSED HERE, BUT I VOTED NO FIRST TIME AND I WILL VOTE RESPECTFULLY NO THIS TIME. THANK YOU VERY MUCH.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I FEEL THAT PEOPLE ARE CONFUSED. WE ALREADY HAVE THIS POWER. WE ARE NOT TALKING ABOUT THE POWER TO -- I MEAN, THE MAYOR ALREADY HAS TO PUT THE PERSON IN FRONT OF US. WE HAVE TO -- THAT IS NOT WHAT WE ARE TALKING ABOUT HERE. WE ARE TALKING ABOUT INTERIM POWERS. WE ARE TALKING ABOUT PEOPLE WHO ARE COMING IN AS INTERIM. AND WE ARE ALSO SAYING THAT IF YOU NOMINATE SOMEONE ONCE, YOU CAN'T NOMINATE THEM AGAIN. SO WE ARE NOT SAYING -- I MEAN, THE ACTUAL POWER OF NOMINATING SOMEONE AND THEN REQUIRING CITY COUNCIL APPROVAL, WE AREN'T CHANGING ANY OF THAT. THAT ALREADY EXIST IT. AND THAT'S ALREADY THE WAY WE ARE SUPPOSED TO DO BUSINESS.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: IF YOU ARE ASKING FOR THAT BEFORE THE EMPLOYEE --

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: NO, WE AREN'T. WE ARE JUST SAYING THAT THAT EMPLOYEE CAN COME IN AND BE AN INTERIM HEAD OF SOMETHING BEFORE THEY ACTUALLY GET HIRED BY US, BECAUSE WE ARE THE ONES THAT TECHNICALLY HIRE THEM IN THE END. THEY ARE NOMINATED BY THE MAYOR, AND THEN APPOINTED BY US.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: THAT STILL MEANS THAT THE PERSON DOESN'T HAVE THE JOB THAT YOU RECRUITED THEM FOR. WHEN I CAME, BEFORE I WAS CONFIRMED BY COUNCIL, I ESSENTIALLY WAS IN A PROCESS OF SELECTING BEFORE COUNCIL SO I TOOK A JOB, I WAS AN EXISTING EMPLOYEE, SO I WOULD BE ABLE TO BE SELECTED AS AN EMPLOYEE BECAUSE I STARTED, BUT YOU ARE SAYING THAT THAT PERSON DOESN'T HAVE THE JOB UNTIL THE CONFIRMATION IS FINAL. SO I AM LEAVING MY CURRENT JOB, TAKING A NEW JOB, AND I'M NOT CONFIRMED IN GETTING THAT POSITION. I GET IT, BUT --

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: BUT WHAT HAPPENS IF WE DON'T CONFIRM YOU? I MEAN, I'M JUST SAYING, BECAUSE WE COULD EASILY NOT CONFIRM.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: I UNDERSTAND THAT. I UNDERSTAND WHAT YOU ARE SAYING AND THERE'S A PROCESS IN PLACE. WHY CHANGE IT IF YOU HAVE THE PROCESS IN PLACE, TO CHANGE IT ANY DIFFERENTLY IS -- IT DOES NOT WORK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: SO I AM JUST GOING TO RESPOND, BECAUSE THE RESPONSE TO ME IS THE ISSUE WAS OBVIOUSLY WITH THE LAST APPOINTMENT THAT WE HAD TO DEAL WITH, I WASN'T ON COUNCIL YET, BUT THE IDEA OF US NOT MAKING THE COUNCIL NOT MAKING THAT DECISION, AND THAT QUITE FRANKLY THE DECISION WAS ALREADY MADE, AND THEN COUNCIL REALLY DOESN'T GET THE ABILITY TO ACTUALLY MAKE THE DECISION, AND THAT IS AS COUNCILMAN GUDES SAID OUR ONLY OPPORTUNITY TO REALLY MAKE SURE THAT WE HAVE CHOSEN THE RIGHT PERSON. THAT'S BASICALLY ASKING US TO -- I TOTALLY SEE YOUR POINT. BUT THERE'S VERY FEW WAYS THAT WE CAN MOVE ON IN THE NOMINATION PROCESS, OR THE APPOINTMENT PROCESS.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: BEFORE I GO TO COUNCILMAN CARLSON, CHIEF, DO YOU WANT TO BE SAY SOMETHING?

segments.speaker: JOHN BENNETT
segments.text: JUST A FEW POINTS.  SOMEBODY HAS JOINED ME. LEAVING THE INTERIM PART OUT FOR A SECOND AND SPEAKING TO COUNCILMAN GUDES, I KNOW WE AS WE WERE BRINGING ON EXECUTIVE STAFF, THERE WAS A QUESTION ABOUT THE SEQUENCING, THE PROCESSING, AND SO, IN OTHER WORDS, THE LANGUAGE WAS IN PLACE, THE NOTION EXISTS, IT STILL EXISTS LIKE COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK SAID, BUT I THINK WHAT COUNCIL WAS LOOKING FOR WAS MORE ENGAGEMENT BEFORE WE GOT TO ACTUAL VETTING, AND I THINK WHAT MS. TRAVIS IS SAYING FROM OUR SIDE OF THE HR SELECTION RECRUITMENT CONTINUUM, AND WHEN YOU GET TO THAT POINT WHERE YOU MAKE A CONDITIONAL OFFER AND YOU START FILLING IN THE BACKGROUND AND ALL THOSE SCIENCES, IF YOU WILL, THAT CANDIDATE IS THE GOING TO MAKE A DECISION, ESPECIALLY IF THEY ARE ALREADY ATTACHED TO ANOTHER EXECUTIVE POSITION AND THEIR CHILDREN ARE IN HIGH SCHOOL AND THEY LIVED THERE 15, 20 YEARS, THEY'VE MAKE A DECISION. AND SO I THINK IT REALLY IS MORE ABOUT WORKING WITH COUNCIL AS A PROCESS, BECAUSE WHEN WE GET TO CONFIRMATION, IT SHOULD BE JUST LIKE IT WAS TODAY. THERE WAS A 7-0 VOTE. HAVE BEEN EVERYBODY WAS HAPPY. NICOLE SENT IN THE RESUMÉ. SHE SET UP MEETINGS. SOME PEOPLE COULD, SOME PEOPLE COULDN'T. BUT IT WAS REALLY A GOOD PROCESS. AND WE LEARNED OVER TIME HOW TO DANCE THAT DANCE WITHIN THE LANGUAGE. SO NOBODY IS TRYING TO SAY THE CONFIRMATION SHOULDN'T HAPPEN OR ANYTHING LIKE THAT. I THINK REALLY IT'S MORE INCUMBENT ON US TO CONTROL THE PROCESS, TO MAKE SURE THAT COUNCIL IS FULLY AWARE BY THAT TIME THAT PERSON COMES AND TRYING TO HELP US WORK WITH YOU TO FIGURE OUT WHERE IN THAT PROCESS IT'S SAFE FOR THE CANDIDATE AND SAFE FOR THE CITY. AND SO THAT'S KIND OF THE BALANCING ACT THAT I AM USING FROM MY EXPERIENCE OF WORKING WITH YOU IN THIS OVER THREE YEARS, AND IT'S NOT EASY. THERE'S A LOT OF DYNAMICS IN IT. AND RECRUITING IS GETTING HARDER. WE ARE IN COMPETITION WITH FOLKS IN THE 300 AND $400,000 RANGE FOR A JOB THAT THEY ARE TAKING A CUT TO COME WORK IN CIVIL SERVICE, SO IT'S A CHALLENGE, BUT I SEE IT MORE AS WORKING TOGETHER ON THE PROCESS THAN TRYING TO CHANGE LANGUAGE THAT MAY OR MAY NOT MAKE A DIFFERENCE.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: CHIEF, THAT'S NOT MY ISSUE. I'LL USE MR. MANISCALCO BECAUSE HE WAS THE SWING VOTE ON THIS. HE ASKED LEGAL, WELL, WHAT DO WE DO IF THIS BOARD SAYS NO? THERE'S NOTHING IN THIS CHARTER THAT SAYS ANYTHING OF WHAT THIS COUNCIL DOES, NOTHING. SO IF THIS COUNCIL GOES THROUGH THE PROCESS, YOU GET WHO YOU WANT, BUT THIS COUNCIL SAYS NO.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: WHAT HAPPENS NEXT?

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: WHAT HAPPENS NEXT. I DON'T UNDERSTAND HOW THIS BODY AS A COUNCIL CAN SIT HERE AND SAY NOTHING IS WRONG WITH THIS PROCESS. I HAVE A REAL ISSUE WITH THAT. FOR COUNCIL MEMBERS TO SIT HERE KNOWING THERE IS AN ISSUE WITH THIS LANGUAGE, AND WE DO NOTHING. I HAVE A PROBLEM WITH -- I'M A POLICEMAN. I'M SORRY. I GO BY THE RULES, JOHN. THE RULES ARE THE RULES. AND WHEN A POLICY IS NOT RIGHT, WE FIX THE POLICY. WE'RE NOT FIXING THE POLICY HERE, GENTLEMEN. IF COUNCIL VOTES SOMEONE DONE, THE MAYOR CAN BRING THE PERSON BACK 50 MILLION TIMES FROM WHAT THE LAST CITY ATTORNEY. WE HAVE NO RECOURSE, BUT IT KEEPS GOING BACK AND FORTH. LIKE CHIEF BENNETT SAYS, WHERE IS THE BALANCE? HOW DO WE STOP THAT? FOR ME AND THIS COUNCIL TO SIT UP HERE AND SAY NOTHING IS WRONG WITH THIS. SHAME ON YOU. I RESPECT YOU ALL, BUT SHAME ON YOU.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: COUNCILMAN GUDES, I THINK IN RESPONSE TO WHAT I HEARD AFTER THE ONE PARTICULAR CONFIRMATION THAT KIND OF DRUMMED THIS UP, RIGHT, WAS THE LENGTH OF TIME FOR COUNCIL BEING AWARE, TO WHAT MR. BENNETT WAS JUST SAYING, THE PROCESS IN INTRODUCING THE CANDIDATE TO COUNCIL AND HAVING THAT OPPORTUNITY. AND EACH CONFIRMATION, I THINK I PROBABLY AM THE ONLY ADMINISTRATOR THAT HAS BROUGHT YOU THREE OR FOUR CONFIRMATIONS SINCE THIS PERIOD OF TIME.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: YOU DID IT THE RIGHT WAY.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: BUT I'M SAYING -- BUT WE LEARNED BECAUSE WE HEARD WHAT COUNCIL SAID ABOUT THE PROCESS AND I DIDN'T DO THAT PROCESS INDEPENDENTLY. I DID THAT PROCESS IN COMMUNICATION WITH CHIEF BENNETT, WITH HR AND SAYING, OKAY, THIS IS THE FEEDBACK THAT WE HAVE. AND SO EACH PROCESS, SO I ASKED YOU WHEN WE BROUGHT ALIS DRUMGO AND ABBYE FEELEY TO YOU, AND SOME WERE SAYING, WELL, THIS IS KIND OF QUICK, SOME WERE LIKE, OKAY, I'M LEARNING. THEN I BRING THE OTHERS TO YOU, AND WE'RE TWEAKING AND LEARNING. WE'RE CONSTANTLY EVOLVING THE PROCESS. IT'S NOT LIKE -- IT'S NOT ME DOING IT RIGHT. IT'S US LEARNING THROUGH THE PROCESS AND TRYING TO WALK THAT FINE LINE AND THAT BALANCE TO BE RESPONSIVE TO WHAT YOU'RE ASKING FOR AND THE INTERACTION THAT YOU WANT TO HAVE WITH THE CANDIDATES BEFORE NOMINATION.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN CARLSON, YOU'RE GOING TO BE NEXT. MS. COWAN, I SAW THIS LOOK ON YOUR FACE AS IF YOU HAD SOMETHING TO SAY.

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: NO. NO, I DID NOT.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: OKAY. COUNCILMAN CARLSON.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: WE CAN GO AROUND AND AROUND. I THINK WE SHOULD MAKE A MOTION AT SOME POINT AND VOTE ON IT. MS. TRAVIS MENTIONED, YOU KNOW, I AGREE WITH YOU 99.9% OF THE TIME, BUT YOU MENTIONED A FEW MINUTES AGO CEO AND KIND OF COMPARING IT TO A COMPANY. IF THIS WAS A COMPANY, WE WOULD BE THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. I THOUGHT ABOUT THIS A LOT. AND THEN WE COULD FIRE THE MAYOR. WE CAN'T DO THAT. SO IF THE CRIME RATE GOES UP, THE BUDGETS GET SQUANDERED, THINGS DON'T GO OUT FOR RFP, CITY COUNCIL MEMBERS GET ATTACKED, WE CAN'T FIRE THE MAYOR. ONLY THE CITIZENS CAN DO THAT.

segments.speaker: NICOLE TRAVIS
segments.text: CORRECT.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: IT IS A DIFFERENCE. YOU SAW WHAT HAPPENED TO BOB CHAPEK; THE BOARD DOESN'T LIKE HIM, SO HE'S GONE. THE OTHER THING IS WHAT THIS -- ALL THIS IS DOING IS PUTTING THE RIGHT WORDS IN FOR WHAT ACTUALLY HAPPENS. IF WE HAD DISAGREED WITH THE PERSON THIS MORNING, WE WOULD HAVE MAYBE GONE FOR MULTIPLE ROUNDS ANYWAY WITHOUT THIS LANGUAGE BEING CHANGED. BUT BECAUSE THE PROCESS WENT WELL AND IT WAS A GOOD CANDIDATE, DIDN'T. WHAT HAPPENED THIS ONE TIME, AND THIS WAS WITH THE POLICE CHIEF, IS THAT ONE REASON FOR THE WORD "NOMINATE" IS TO BE RESPECTFUL TO THE PUBLIC BECAUSE THE CANDIDATE WAS PRESENTED AS THE POLICE CHIEF TO THE PUBLIC. AND I THINK THE REASON WHY I SUPPORTED THIS IS BECAUSE IT WAS DISRESPECTFUL TO THE PUBLIC. NOT THAT THE PERSON DID ANYTHING WRONG, BUT JUST THE PERSON BEING PRESENTED AS THE FINAL PERSON, BECAUSE THE PUBLIC THOUGHT WHY DON'T WE HAVE ANY INPUT, WHY DOESN'T THE PUBLIC HAVE ANY INPUT. THAT POSITION HAPPENS TO BE A REALLY IMPORTANT POSITION. SO THE ADMINISTRATION PUSHED, PUSHED, PUSHED DESPITE ALL THE COMMUNITY'S CONCERNS ABOUT THINGS THAT HAD HAPPENED IN THE PAST AND THERE WERE PROMISES MADE THAT THINGS WOULDN'T HAPPEN AGAIN. SO WE WERE PUSHED AND I THINK IT WAS A 5-2 VOTE. WE'LL SEE WHETHER THAT WAS A GOOD DECISION OR NOT, BUT I THINK THERE HAS TO BE A COLLABORATIVE APPROACH. ONCE THESE, AS MR. GUDES SAID, ONCE THESE FOLKS ARE APPOINTED, WE HAVE NO POWER OVER THEM. I TRIED IN THE CHARTER TO CHANGE TO GET US TO FIRE THE CITY ATTORNEY, WHICH I THINK WE SHOULD BE ABLE TO. BUT IF CITY STAFF AS THEY ARE, WE'VE GOT DOCUMENTS ON IT, YOU KNOW, SET CITY COUNCIL MEMBERS UP AGAINST EACH OTHER OR DO OPPOSITION RESEARCH ON US, WE CAN'T FIRE THEM. WE CAN ASK THE STAFF, THE MAYOR AND THE ADMINISTRATION TO FIRE THEM, BUT THEY DON'T. I HEAR THAT MORE THAN HALF OF THE SENIOR STAFF DON'T HIT THE RESIDENCY REQUIREMENT. IT'S ULTIMATELY UP TO THE MAYOR AND HOW THE MAYOR RUNS EVERYTHING. I'M SURE THE MAYOR IS GOING TO BE ACCOUNTABLE, BUT THE PUBLIC WANTS US TO BE ACCOUNTABLE, TOO, AND TO HAVE MORE SCRUTINY OVER THINGS. I THINK CHANGING A COUPLE OF WORDS IS NOT A SIGNIFICANT CHANGE. IT ONLY IS GOING TO REFLECT WHAT IS ACTUALLY HAPPENING IN PRACTICE, EXCEPT THESE EXCEPTIONS. AND WE ALSO HAVE TO WORRY ABOUT NOT WHETHER IF YOU LIKE THE SITUATION NOW BETWEEN CITY COUNCIL AND THE MAYOR, YOU HAVE TO THINK ABOUT WHAT IF IT IS EVEN WORSE. I DON'T KNOW HOW IT COULD BE WORSE BUT WE HAVE TO THINK ABOUT THAT POSSIBILITY.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILMAN VIERA AND THEN COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: [INAUDIBLE]

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I ONLY HAVE A QUICK EDITOR'S CHANGE, SCRIVENER'S ERROR. IN THE ACTUAL CHARTER LANGUAGE, THE WORD "SECTION" SHOULD BE CAPITALIZED BEFORE 6.03 IN BOTH INSTANCES. THAT IS MY ONLY REQUEST.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: PLEASURE OF COUNCIL?

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: I'M GOING TO MOVE IT. ALL RIGHT. FILE NUMBER E2022-8 CH 2. ORDINANCE BEING PRESENTED FOR FIRST READING CONSIDERATION. AN ORDINANCE RELATING TO THE GOVERNMENT OF THE CITY OF TAMPA, FLORIDA, SUBMITTING TO THE ELECTORS OF THE CITY A PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE REVISED CHARTER OF THE CITY OF TAMPA OF 1975 AS AMENDED TO CLARIFY THAT THE MAYOR'S NOMINATIONS FOR HEADS OF DEPARTMENTS AND OTHER CITY EMPLOYEES AS SET OUT IN SECTION 6.03, MUST BE APPROVED BY FOUR VOTES OF CITY COUNCIL AND TO PROVIDE FOR INTERIM APPOINTMENTS OF EXISTING CITY EMPLOYEES BY THE MAYOR FOR A MAXIMUM OF 180 DAYS; PROVIDING AN EFFECTIVE DATE.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: SECOND.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: WE HAVE A MOTION MADE BY COUNCILMAN GUDES. SECONDED BY COUNCILWOMAN HURTAK. ANY FURTHER DISCUSSION? ROLL CALL.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: NO.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: NO.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: NO.

segments.speaker: THE CLERK
segments.text: THE MOTION CARRIED WITH VIERA, MIRANDA, AND CITRO VOTING NO. SECOND READING AND ADOPTION WILL BE HELD ON JANUARY 5th, 2023, AT 9:30 A.M.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: THAT WAS THE LAST ONE, CORRECT?

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: NO. WE HAVE ONE MORE.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: I WANTED TO CLARIFY, IF I MAY, JUST REALLY FAST ON THE TERM LIMITS ONE, WE PASSED THAT AS FOUR BECAUSE COUNCILMAN CARLSON MENTIONED SOMETHING THAT'S TRUE. FOUR CONSECUTIVE FULL TERMS, CORRECT?

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: JOSEPH CITRO
segments.text: MS. COWAN.

segments.speaker: JENNIFER COWAN
segments.text: ON TO THE LAST ITEM, AND THIS ORDINANCE BEFORE YOU IS AMENDING SECTION 10.10 OF THE CHARTER REVIEW ADDRESSING CHARTER REVIEW ADVISORY COMMISSION. THE FIRST THING IT DOES IS CORRECT A SCRIVENER'S ERROR AND THEN IT ALSO PROVIDES THAT BEGINNING IN 2025 AND EVERY FIVE YEARS THEREAFTER THERE WOULD BE A CHARTER REVIEW ADVISORY COMMITTEE ESTABLISHED. LEGAL COUNSEL, WHO IS NOT A CITY EMPLOYEE, WOULD SERVE AS LEGAL ADVISOR TO THE CHARTER REVIEW BOARD AND A PROFESSIONAL FACILITATOR WOULD BE ENGAGED BY THE CITY WITH CITY COUNCIL APPROVAL. ONE OTHER ITEM I WANTED TO JUST LET YOU KNOW IS WHEN WE WENT THROUGH THESE ORDINANCES AND IT WAS DISCUSSED WITH BOTH CITY COUNCIL, CITY COUNCIL ATTORNEY AND YOUR CITY ATTORNEY, THEY ASKED ME WHETHER OR NOT BMO CLIENTS HAD AMENDED THE FREQUENCY IN WHICH THEIR CHARTER REVIEW ADVISORY COMMITTEES MEET. AND WE SAID, YES. IN FACT, WE'VE HAD THIS BEFORE, SPECIFICALLY THE CITY OF LARGO SEVERAL YEARS AGO HAD CHARTER REVIEW EVERY TEN YEARS, AND THEN THEY CHANGED IT TO EVERY FIVE. NOW, THEIR EXPERIENCE WAS THAT, AND THE FEEDBACK THAT THEY RECEIVED FROM THEIR CHARTER REVIEW COMMISSION WAS BY THE TIME THEY HAD GONE THROUGH AN IN-DEPTH REVIEW HAD EVERYTHING DONE, BACK TO THEIR COUNCIL, AND THEN ON THE BALLOT, THERE WAS MAYBE A YEAR BREAK AND THEY WERE STARTING AGAIN. SO THEY HAVE SUBSEQUENTLY CHANGED IT FROM FIVE TO SEVEN. SO I JUST OFFER THAT AS INFORMATION TO YOU FOR YOUR CONSIDERATION, BUT THE ORDINANCE AS DRAFTED IS EVERY FIVE YEARS AS DIRECTED.

OWNS MOBLEY HOMES CUSTOM LLC. MS. MAIER IS HERE REPRESENTING HIM AS AUTHORIZED AGENT. THE REQUEST MADE TO THE VARIANCE REVIEW BOARD WAS REGARDING APPROVAL TO REMOVE A GRAND TREE. THE VARIANCE REVIEW BOARD HEARD THE REQUEST AND HELD A PUBLIC HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 13, 2022, AND DENIED THE REQUEST TO REMOVE THE GRAND TREE BASED UPON THE APPLICANT NOT MEETING THE STANDARDS SET FORTH IN CODE SECTION 27-284.2.5 SUBSECTION F-4. I PREVIOUSLY HANDED CITY COUNCIL OR PROVIDED CITY COUNCIL THE RELEVANT CODE SECTION, WHICH IS CODE SECTION 27-61 SUBSECTION J-2-A THAT PROVIDES THE PARAMETERS IN WHICH CITY COUNCIL CAN CONDUCT A REVIEW HEARING. ALSO, THE RELEVANT CODE SECTION 27-284.2.5 WHICH ARE THE FACTORS THAT CITY COUNCIL HAS TO CONSIDER IN WHETHER OR NOT TO GRANT OR DENY A REQUEST TO REMOVE A GRAND TREE. AS PREVIOUSLY STATED IN CODE SECTION 27-61 SUBSECTION J-2, THE STANDARD OF REVIEW FOR THIS MATTER IS A DE NOVO STANDARD OF REVIEW. CITY COUNCIL, WHICH MEANS CITY COUNCIL SHALL NOT BE LIMITED IN ITS REVIEW TO THE INFORMATION, DOCUMENTATION OR EVIDENCE UPON WHICH THE BOARD BASED ITS DETERMINATION. THERE ARE TWO STAFF MEMBERS PRESENT WHO WILL PROVIDE AN EXPLANATION OF THE DECISION FROM THE VARIANCE REVIEW BOARD AND WHAT THE APPLICATION WAS. THOSE STAFF MEMBERS ARE JAYNE NMADU AND STEVEN EYSTER FROM NATURAL RESOURCES. AFTER CITY COUNCIL HEARS ALL THE EVIDENCE IN THE CASE AND ALL THE DOCUMENTATION, CITY COUNCIL CAN TAKE THREE ACTIONS AT THE CONCLUSION OF THE HEARING. CITY COUNCIL CAN AFFIRM THE DECISION OF THE VRB IN CASE VRB-22-70 AND DENY THE REQUEST TO REMOVE THE GRAND TREE. CITY COUNCIL CAN REMAND THE MATTER BACK TO VRB WITH DIRECTION, OR FINALLY, CITY COUNCIL CAN OVERTURN THE DECISION OF THE VARIANCE REVIEW BOARD IN CASE VRB-22-70 AND THEREBY GRANT THE REQUEST TO REMOVE THE GRAND TREE. WITH THAT, I WILL TURN THE PRESENTATION OVER TO STAFF.
