AGAIN, WHAT YOU'RE LOOKING AT, WHAT YOU SEE IS PART OF THE BY RIGHT BUILDING ENVELOPE THAT A STRUCTURE PROPOSED HERE COULD BE BUILT ON UNDER THE ZONING. THE CANOPY TAKES UP A LOT OF THE SITE AS WELL. SO ALTHOUGH AS MS. PETTIS-MACKLE POINTED OUT THIS IS A DE NOVO HEARING, I WANT TO TALK ABOUT THE REASON THAT WE ARE APPEALING TO YOU TO ACHIEVE A DIFFERENT RESULT. THE VARIANCE REVIEW BOARD DID ERR IN MULTIPLE WAYS IN ITS DECISION MAKING. THERE WAS A LOT OF TALK AND THEY ENDED UP RELYING ON, IN LARGE PART, THE OUTDATED TREE CODE STANDARD, USING THE TERM "REASONABLE USE." THAT IS NOT EVEN IN THE CURRENT TREE CODE ANYMORE, THAT TERM. AND IT WAS USED -- MANY OF YOU ARE PROBABLY FAMILIAR WITH IT BECAUSE THIS CHANGE WAS IN 2019. BUT IT WAS USED THROUGHOUT THIS HEARING, AND IT WAS BLENDED WITH THE CURRENT CODE THAT APPLIES, BUT THE CURRENT CODE USES THE REASONABLE RECONFIGURATION STANDARD WHICH MR. PETERIKA WILL DO A BETTER JOB OF WALKING YOU THROUGH THAN I WOULD, BUT THEY MISAPPLIED THAT TERM AS WELL, AND WE'LL SHOW YOU HOW. ULTIMATELY, THEY ALSO RELIED ON INAPPROPRIATE BASES FOR DENIAL THAT WERE NOT ROOTED IN THE CODE CRITERIA IN WHICH THEY WERE REQUIRED TO APPLY. NOW, THE CURRENT CODE IS DESIGNED PURPOSEFULLY TO BE OBJECTIVE, BUT BY BLENDING THE OLD CODE AND MISAPPLYING THE NEW CODE, THEY REACHED A TOTALLY SUBJECTIVE RESULT. OUR FIRM WAS NOT AT THE VARIANCE REVIEW BOARD HEARING, HOWEVER, WHEN WE REVIEWED THE TRANSCRIPT AND SAW HOW THE CODE HAD BEEN MISAPPLIED, THE FIRST STEP WAS TO ASK RICKY PETERIKA, WHO BETTER TO ASK BECAUSE HE WAS VERY INVOLVED IN THE TRANSITION OF THE CODE AND APPLIED THEM BOTH. HIS TESTIMONY WILL BE INVALUABLE. AGAIN, TO REITERATE, THE CODE WAS OVERHAULED IN 2019 TO CREATE AN OBJECTIVE PROCESS. THERE WERE EVEN WORKSHOPS HELD TRAINING CITY BOARD MEMBERS -- OR SITTING BOARD MEMBERS HOW TO APPLY IT BECAUSE IT WAS SUCH A DIFFERENT RESULT. AGAIN, THE OLD STANDARD WAS SUBJECTIVE. IT WAS MORE FOCUSED ON ANY CONCEIVABLE STRUCTURE THAT COULD BE BUILT ON THE PROPERTY, IN THIS CASE, IT WOULD BE A SINGLE-FAMILY HOME, BUT ANY CONCEIVABLE CONFIGURATION OR SIZE OF THAT STRUCTURE THAT YOU COULD WORK AROUND THE TREE, THAT COULD BE CONSIDERED A REASONABLE USE UNDER THE CODE AND THE TREE WOULD HAVE TO REMAIN. THE NEW STRUCTURE, OR THE NEW STANDARD IS OBJECTIVE, EVALUATING THE ACTUAL STRUCTURE THAT A GIVEN APPLICANT HAS PROPOSED AND ASKING, CAN THIS STRUCTURE, NOT ANY CONCEIVABLE STRUCTURE, BUT CAN THIS STRUCTURE BE REASONABLY RECONFIGURED. I'M USING QUOTES HERE BECAUSE THAT IS A DEFINED TERM IN THE CODE SUCH THAT IT COULD SAVE THE TREE. THE TERM REASONABLE CONFIGURATION IS NOT A SUBJECTIVE STANDARD. IT IS CLEARLY DEFINED. IT EITHER IS OR IT IS NOT. I'LL TURN IT OVER FOR A FEW MINUTES TO MR. PETERIKA TO EXPLAIN THAT.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH. YES, SIR. RICKY PETERIKA, 308 EAST 7TH AVENUE. I'VE BEEN SWORN. THERE ARE FIVE CRITERIA FOR GRAND TREE REMOVAL. TABLE 284.2.5, THE TWO THAT APPLY MOST CLOSELY TO THIS CASE ARE THE SMALL LOT CRITERIA, THE TRZ AND GRAND TREE REMOVAL BY COMMISSION OR CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITY IMPACT REVIEWED BY A COMMISSION, WHICH IS THE VRB OR YOUR BODY. SMALL LOT CRITERIA WOULD APPLY TO THE LOT, BUT NOT TO THE TREE BECAUSE IT'S NOT LOCATED IN THE TRZ. SO -- WHICH IS WHY THEY WENT TO THE VRB. SO THEY ARE SUBJECT TO THE OTHER CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITY IMPACT CRITERIA, WHICH IS SHOWN ON THE SLIDE, BUT THE MAIN COMPONENT THAT I'M SPEAKING TO IS ON THE BOTTOM, NO OTHER REASONABLE RECONFIGURATION OF THE DEVELOPMENT. SO THAT IS DEFINED IN FOOTNOTE FIVE OF TABLE 284.2.5, KIND OF FIVE BULLET POINTS. IF THESE THINGS CAN BE DONE TO THE BUILDING FOOTPRINT AS PROPOSED, THEN IT SHALL BE CONSIDERED. BASICALLY IT SHALL BE CONSIDERED REASONABLE. OTHERWISE, IT WOULD BE UNREASONABLE. SO IT DOES NOT ADJUST THE STRUCTURE MORE THAN ALLOWED BY THE DESIGN EXCEPTION. SO NOT MORE THAN 25% FRONT OR BACK OR ONE FOOT SIDE TO SIDE. DOES NOT CHANGE THE NUMBER OF STORIES PROPOSED. SO IT DOESN'T GO FROM TWO TO THREE OR ONE OR TWO TO ONE AND DOES NOT ADVERSELY ALTER INTERNAL FLOW OR FUNCTION OF THE PROPOSED STRUCTURE, WHICH IS VERY FLAVORFUL STATEMENT ON WHAT INTERNAL FLOW AND FUNCTION IS, BUT I'VE ALWAYS INTERPRETED IT IS THIS THE SAME PROGRAM OF THE BUILDING THAT WE'RE PROPOSING IF JUST A DIFFERENT SHAPE OR DID WE LOSE PROGRAM, LOSE ELEMENTS OF THE BUILDING FOR TREE PRESERVATION? LIKE I SAID, THE DESIGN EXCEPTION DOES NOT SHIFT THE BUILDING FAR ENOUGH OUTSIDE FROM CAUSING THE TREE OR THE BUILDING TO BE PRESERVED, OR THE TREE TO BE PRESERVED WITHOUT THE BUILDING FOOTPRINT BEING AFFECTED. SO IT DOESN'T MEET THE DESIGN EXCEPTION ADJUSTMENT. TWO OPTIONS WERE PROPOSED AT THE VRB. BOTH BASICALLY NOTCH OUT A QUARTER OF THE BUILDING, AND THIS IS, WHICH IS SHOWN IN RED, WHICH IS APPROXIMATELY 30% OF THE BUILDING'S GROSS SQUARE FOOTAGE LESSED OUT TO ACCOMMODATE TREE A, WHICH I EVALUATED AS TREE ONE AND TERMED IT TO BE A 43-INCH TREE, B-6, NON-HAZARDOUS GRAND TREE, IN AGREEMENT WITH NATURAL RESOURCES. SO THERE'S NO DISPUTE ABOUT THE CONDITION. JUST ABOUT WHAT REASONABLE RECONFIGURATION IS. SO BOTH OF THESE EXAMPLE -- OR 30% OF THE BUILDING FOOTPRINT IS LOST TO THE TREE PRESERVATION COMMITMENT. WHICH IS ADDRESSED HERE. ADDITIONALLY, BY SHOWING THE FLOOR PLAN OF THE BUILDING -- OF THE PROPOSED BUILDING, WE CAN BETTER UNDERSTAND WHAT ACTUALLY IS LOST FROM THE INTERNAL FLOW OR FUNCTION. WHICH IN THIS CASE, THE MASTER SUITE NO LONGER HAS THE MASTER BATH OR CLOSET. THE STAIRS ARE AFFECTED, WHICH COULD AFFECT THE SECOND FLOOR. IT GETS POTENTIALLY ESOTERIC AS WE APPLY THIS, BUT I THINK AT THE EASIER REVIEW IS, IS THIS THE SAME BUILDING THAT WAS PROPOSED? COULD IT JUST BE MOVED ON THE SITE AND ACCOMMODATED? NO. COULD IT BE INCREASED IN FLOORS OR DECREASED IN FLOORS WITHOUT LOSING SQUARE FOOTAGE? NO. SO BY THOSE CRITERIA, IN MY OPINION, IT'S NO LONGER REASONABLE RECONFIGURATION. THIS WAS PROPOSED BY STAFF, THIS OPTION, WHICH IS A SLIGHT HYBRID OF WHAT WAS PROPOSED BY MR. MOBLEY, BUT IT LOCATES AN ACCESSORY STRUCTURE FOR A GARAGE. IN MY OPINION, THIS IS TECHNICALLY UNREASONABLE RECONFIGURATION BECAUSE WE LOST THE 30% ROUGHLY OF THE LOT AREA AND BY LITERALLY BREAKING THE BUILDING INTO TWO. AND ALL THAT'S BEEN BROKEN OFF IS THE GARAGE. SO THIS PROPOSAL FROM STAFF DURING THE HEARING, IN MY OPINION, WAS AN EXAMPLE OF AN UNREASONABLE RECONFIGURATION OPTION.

segments.speaker: JAIME MAIER
segments.text: JUST TO SUMMARIZE AND THANK YOU, MR. PETERIKA, FOR YOUR TESTIMONY. SO YOU HEARD HOW THE FACTORS THE BOARD WAS REQUIRED TO CONSIDER, AND YOU SAW THEM IN THE STANDARD PROVIDED TO YOU BY THE CITY ATTORNEY I'M SURE IN YOUR PACKETS. THE BOARD WAS REQUIRED TO CONSIDER FACTORS IN THE CODE SUCH AS THE IMPACT OF THE PROPOSED STRUCTURE ON THE TREE. WELL, AS YOU'VE SEEN, IT WOULD BE GOING RIGHT THROUGH THE TRUNK. OBVIOUSLY, THAT JUST CANNOT COEXIST. WHETHER IT CAN BE REASONABLY RECONFIGURED, AS THAT TERM IS DEFINED IN THE CODE, WHETHER THE SETBACKS COULD BE REDUCED TO PRESERVE IT, WHETHER ALTERNATIVE CONSTRUCTION OR THE COST OF THAT ALTERNATIVE CONSTRUCTION COULD PRESERVE IT. YOU'VE HEARD MR. PETERIKA'S PROFESSIONAL OPINION THAT THOSE FACTORS WEIGH IN FAVOR OF REMOVING THE TREE BECAUSE THESE POTENTIAL FIXES THAT THE CODE SUGGESTS AND RECOMMENDS -- OR IT REQUIRES THE BOARD TO CONSIDER DON'T WORK HERE. NOW, INSTEAD OF CONSIDERING ALL OF THE THINGS IT WAS SUPPOSED TO CONSIDER AND ULTIMATELY WHAT IT MADE ITS DECISION ON WERE THINGS THEY SHOULD NOT HAVE CONSIDERED. AGAIN, THEY DISCUSSED AT LENGTH AND ASKED QUESTIONS OF STAFF, THE REASONABLE USE STANDARD, SAYING A HOME IS A HOME. ANY HOME, SUGGESTING THAT ANY HOME, INCLUDING THE THOUSAND-SQUARE-FOOT HOME THAT WAS IT THERE BEFORE, VERY SMALL, COMPLETELY DIFFERENT, HAS BEEN DEMOLISHED, WAS REASONABLE USE. UNDER THE OLD STANDARD, THAT PROBABLY WOULD HAVE BEEN THE RESULT, BUT THAT IS NOT THE STANDARD ANY LONGER AND THAT WAS A CONSCIOUS DECISION TO CHANGE THE CODE. IT IS UNRECOGNIZABLE FROM THE STANDARD THAT EXISTED IN 2019 AND PRIOR. ALSO, AS AN EXPRESSED REASON FOR DENIAL WHEN THEY LISTED THEIR REASONS FOR DENYING THE APPLICATION, THEY EXPRESSLY STATED THAT THE NUMBER OF SPEAKERS AND LETTERS IN OPPOSITION COULD NOT BE IGNORED. THAT WAS A REASON FOR DENIAL. THERE IS WELL-ESTABLISHED CASE LAW THAT SAYS A LARGE NUMBER OF RESIDENTS OPPOSING -- OR APPEARING TO OBJECT IS NOT A VALID BASIS FOR DENIAL OF A PERMIT. AND THEY STILL USED THAT AS A BASIS OF DENIAL OF A PERMIT. IN SUMMARY, YOU'VE SEEN HOW THIS STRUCTURE CANNOT BE REASONABLY RECONFIGURED, AGAIN, AS THAT TERM IS DEFINED IN THE CODE, IT IS NOT SUBJECTIVE. IT IS NOT ANYBODY'S IDEA OF WHAT REASONABLE COULD BE. THE DICTIONARY DEFINITION OF THE TERM IS A DEFINED TERM WITH CRITERIA. AND YOU'VE SEEN WHO IT CANNOT BE REASONABLY RECONFIGURED. THEREFORE, THE STRUCTURE THAT IS PROPOSED ON THIS SMALL LOT WHERE SEVERAL TREES ARE AT ISSUE, THIS PARTICULAR ONE IS CENTRALLY LOCATED. IT IS LOCATED WITHIN THE BUILDABLE AREA THAT IS CREATED BY THE ZONING CODE. THE APPLICATION OF THE CORRECT CODE STANDARD WOULD HAVE RESULTED IN AN OBJECTIVE FINDING THAT THE TREE MUST BE REMOVED. THEREFORE, WE RESPECTFULLY REQUEST THAT CITY COUNCIL APPLY THE CORRECT CODE STANDARD AND REVERSE THE VRB'S DECISION. THANK YOU.

WE NEGOTIATED IN GOOD FAITH AND I BELIEVE CITY COUNCIL NEGOTIATED IN GOOD FAITH WHEN THEY UNANIMOUSLY APPROVED THE NEW TREE CODE. ALL THE STAKEHOLDERS COMPROMISE TO ACHIEVE PREDICTABILITY SO THAT TIME AND RESOURCES AREN'T WASTED. AND YET HERE WE ARE AGAIN WASTING TIME AND RESOURCES. THIS TREE IS NOT IN THE TRZ. MOBLEY PAID FOR IT BUT THEY NEVER GOT THE CHANCE BECAUSE IT NEVER WENT ON THE MARKET, SO THEY ARE STILL LOOKING FOR A HOME. ARBORIST DAVID RILEY EVALUATED THIS TREE BEFORE THE SALE. WE HAVE A TREE CODE THAT TOLD HIM HE COULD NOT REMOVE THIS TREE. THE VRB EVALUATED THE CRITERIA AND VOTED 5-1 THAT HE COULD NOT REMOVE THIS TREE. PLEASE UPHOLD THE VRB RULING AND DENY THE VARIANCE. IF EVERYONE KNOWS THE CITY IS GOING TO ENFORCE THE TREE CODE THAT THE STAKEHOLDERS WROTE, PEOPLE WILL START BUILDING THE RIGHT THING IN THE RIGHT PLACE AND YOUR TIME AND RESOURCES WON'T BE WASTED ON VARIANCES AND APPEALS AND YOU WILL GIVE BUILDERS THE PREDICTABILITY THAT THEY DEMANDED. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: OKAY. IF YOU COULD PLAY THE VIDEO. THERE WE GO. MY NAME IS LORRAINE PERINO. I LIVE IN SOUTH TAMPA. I'M PRESIDENT OF THE TAMPA TREE ADVOCACY GROUP. ON JULY 7th, 2022, THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE ISSUED A RARE WARNING TO TAMPA RESIDENTS TO EXERCISE CAUTION WHILE OUTDOORS AS THE HEAT INDEX THAT DAY WOULD MEET THE 93-DEGREE TEMPERATURE FEEL LIKE 110 DEGREES. DESPITE THE CLIMATE CHANGE ALARM BELLS GOING OFF DAILY, MOBLEY PETITIONED THE CITY TO RESCIND THE VRB DECISION AND ALLOW HIM TO DESTROY ONE OF TAMPA'S MOST ESSENTIAL, VALUABLE AND BEAUTIFUL TREES. HEALTHY 50-FOOT TALL GRAND OAK LOCATED OUTSIDE OF THE BUILDABLE AREA TO BUILD A LARGER STRUCTURE THAN THE CITY CODE ALLOWS. IS THIS THE BEHAVIOR OF A GOOD CORPORATE CITIZEN OR IS IT GREED? THE BUILDER ASSERTS THAT REMOVING THIS GRAND OAK WOULD ALLOW THE PROPERTY TO BE USED IN THE SAME MANNER AS OTHER LOTS OF THE SAME SIZE IN THE SAME AREA. OTHER LOTS OF THE SAME SIZE DO NOT CONTAIN A HEALTHY 50-FOOT GRAND OAK GROWING ON THEM. NOTE THAT THE CITY ARBORIST PLAN PROPOSES AN ALTERNATE DESIGN WHICH WOULD PRESERVE THE OLD GROWTH GRAND OAK FOR FUTURE GENERATIONS TO ENJOY. MOBLEY ADMITS TO PURCHASING THE PROPERTY WITH THE TREE ON IT. YET ASTONISHINGLY CLAIMS THAT THE GRAND TREE HAS CREATED A HARDSHIP ON HIM. EVEN THOUGH THE TREE IS PROTECTED BY THE CITY'S TREE ORDINANCE, HE FIGURES HE CAN BEAT THE SYSTEM AND DESTROY THE TREE BY APPEALING TO THE CITY. REMOVING A TREE OF THIS SIZE AGAINST THE WISHES OF THE NEIGHBORHOOD RESIDENTS WOULD ALSO NEGATIVELY IMPACT THEIR HEALTH AS THE LOSS OF SHADE AND COOL AIR THAT THE TREE PROVIDES WOULD CAUSE THE HEAT INDEX TO RISE DRAMATICALLY AS REFERENCED ABOVE. THE GRAND OAK'S REMOVAL WOULD ALSO INCREASE AIR POLLUTION IN THE NEIGHBORHOOD, RESULTING FROM THE RELEASE OF CO2, WHICH HAS BEEN STORED IN ITS TRUNK, AND THE CO2 WHICH IT WOULD CEASE TO CAPTURE. THE NEIGHBORHOOD WOULD ALSO BE DEPRIVED OF THE TREE'S BEAUTY AND THE PURE OXYGEN WHICH IT PUMPS OUT DAILY AND SONGBIRDS, SQUIRRELS, BUTTERFLIES, AND ALL WILDLIFE DEPENDENT ON THIS GRAND OAK WOULD BE NEGATIVELY IMPACTED. DESPITE THESE SERIOUS CONSEQUENCES, THE BUILDER FALSELY ASSERTS THAT BY REMOVING THE TREE THE RIGHTS OF OTHERS ARE NOT COMPROMISED OR INJURIOUS IN ANY WAY TO THE PUBLIC HEALTH, SAFETY, AND GENERAL WELFARE. MOBLEY HOMES ONLY CARES FOR THE SELFISH INTERESTS, NOT THE GOOD OF THE COMMUNITY. MOBLEY CLAIMS THAT THE PROPOSED VARIANCE IS IN HARMONY WITH TAMPA'S COMP PLAN. HOWEVER, THE COMP PLANS URBAN FORESTRY SECTION STATES, TREE CANOPY IS A VITAL COMMUNITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL ASSET THAT IS APPRECIATED AND DESIRED BY RESIDENTS IN NEW AND ESTABLISHED NEIGHBORHOODS ALIKE. THE PROTECTION AND SUPPLEMENTATION OF THIS CANOPY, TREE CANOPY IS A NECESSITY IN ORDER TO SUSTAIN THE RESOURCE AND MAINTAIN THE ENVIRONMENTAL BENEFITS SUCH AS COOLER TEMPERATURES THAT THE CANOPY -- MATURE CANOPY PROVIDES. THE 2011 URBAN FOREST ANALYSIS STATES, UNLIKE OTHER PUBLIC INFRASTRUCTURE COMPONENTS, PROPERLY PLANTED AND MAINTAINED TREES INCREASE IN VALUE OVER TIME. PAST TIME TO STOP CATERING TO THE SELFISH DESIRES OF BUILDERS BY NOT ALLOWING THEM TO DESTROY TAMPA'S OLD GROWTH TREES AND NEGATIVELY IMPACT OUR OWN HEALTH. INSTEAD, LET'S PRESERVE TAMPA'S GRAND TREES AND CHERISH THEM FOR THE BEAUTY, HEALTH BENEFITS, AND QUALITY OF LIFE THAT THEY PROVIDE FOR US AT SUCH A MINIMAL COST. PLEASE VOTE NO TO THE SELF-SERVING APPEAL AND VOTE TO PRESERVE ONE OF TAMPA'S MOST BEAUTIFUL OLD-GROWTH GRAND OAKS FOR ALL OF TAMPA.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: YES, MA'AM. THANK YOU VERY MUCH. NEXT SPEAKER, PLEASE STATE YOUR NAME. CAROL CAMISSA, PALMA CEIA. I'VE BEEN SWORN IN. I'M ALSO A DOG GRANNY, AND I WALK IN THIS NEIGHBORHOOD. AND ENJOY THE SHADE, THE BEAUTY OF THE TREE, THE BRICK STREETS. MR. MOBLEY HAS CLAIMED THAT HIS -- ONE OF HIS THREE MODELS THAT HE INTENDS TO PUT THERE WILL NOT CHANGE THE CHARACTER OF THE NEIGHBORHOOD AND LOOKING AT THOSE PICTURES AND MY OWN WALKS THERE, I WOULD DISAGREE WITH THAT STRONGLY. IN MY DOG WALKS, I ALSO SEE OTHER PROPERTIES CONSTANTLY BEING DEVELOPED IN SOUTH TAMPA. THE LOTS THAT HAVE A MOBLEY SIGN ON THEM GENERALLY ARE ONES THAT ARE FLAT, TREELESS, AND CAN ACCOMMODATE ONE OF HIS THREE MODELS. THERE ARE OTHER DEVELOPERS, AND I'M NOT HERE TO PROMOTE OR NOT, ANY PARTICULAR DEVELOPER, BUT I SEE PLACES WHERE TREES ARE ROPED OFF AND TRACTORS MOVE AROUND AND THAT KIND OF THING. IT IS POSSIBLE TO HAVE A REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION. THIS IS A SMALLER LOT, BUT THAT'S NOT THE MODUS OPERANDI OF THIS PARTICULAR DEVELOPER, AND I DON'T THINK THAT GIVEN THE STRENGTH OF THE COMMUNITY'S FEELINGS AND CASE THAT THEY ARE MAKING ABOUT SAVING THIS TREE, SOMEONE PUSHING SOMETHING, A WAIVER THROUGH THE SYSTEM JUST BECAUSE THEIR BUSINESS MODEL DOESN'T ACCOMMODATE THIS PARTICULAR LOT AND THIS PARTICULAR TREE IS A GOOD CASE FOR A WAIVER. SO I WOULD ASK YOU TO DENY THAT REQUEST. THANK YOU.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH. STEPHANIE POYNOR, ARE YOU UNMUTED? IS SHE LOGGED IN STILL?

segments.speaker: THE CLERK
segments.text: MS. POYNOR, JUST TURN ON YOUR CAMERA AND UNMUTE YOURSELF, PLEASE.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: WE APOLOGIZE, BUT THERE SEEMS TO BE SOME TECHNICAL ISSUES.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: YES, MA'AM. TURN YOUR CAMERA.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: WE CAN HEAR YOU, BUT WE CAN'T SEE YOU.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH. THAT CONCLUDES PUBLIC COMMENT. WE'LL GO TO ANY ADDITIONAL STAFF COMMENT, IF THERE IS ANY. YES, MA'AM.

segments.speaker: JAYNE NMADU
segments.text: GOOD AFTERNOON. JAYNE NMADU, DEVELOPMENT COORDINATION. I HAVE BEEN SWORN IN. I WOULD JUST LIKE TO PROVIDE THE COUNCIL WITH FURTHER CLARIFICATION REGARDING SOME OF THE THINGS THAT THE LEGAL REPRESENTATION FOR THE APPLICANT BROUGHT UP. SHE DID MENTION, SHE TALKS ABOUT REASONABLE USE OR REASONABLE RECONFIGURATION AS THE DISCUSSION THAT WENT ON DURING THE VRB HEARING. ULTIMATELY, THE DECISION OF THE BOARD WAS BASED ON SECTION 27-284.2.5 F. THAT IS THE PARAMETER WITHIN WHICH THE BOARD HAS TO DECIDE WHETHER OR NOT TO APPROVE A GRAND TREE. AND THEY DID TALK ABOUT REASONABLE USE AS TO THE FACT THAT THERE WAS A HOUSE PREVIOUSLY ON THIS PROPERTY AND THE APPLICANT DID SAY THAT IT'S IMPOSSIBLE TO PUT A HOME ON THIS PROPERTY WITHOUT REMOVING THE TREE. AND THAT WAS HOW THAT CONVERSATION WENT. THERE WAS ACTUALLY A HOUSE AND THE TREE WAS THERE. SHE DID TALK ABOUT THE FACT THAT -- AND I THINK IT WAS THE EXPERT THAT SPOKE ABOUT ALTERING THE INTERNAL FLOW OF THE STRUCTURE OR THE PROPOSED DESIGN. AGAIN, THAT SECTION SAYS ADVERSELY ALTERED, SO IF YOU'RE ALTERING THE INTERNAL FLOW THAT YOU CANNOT FUNCTION IN THE HOUSE IN THE WAY THAT YOU HAVE TO ALTER THE HOUSE, THEN IT'S ADVERSELY ALTERED. IT DOESN'T SAY YOU CANNOT ALTER THE FLOW OF THE INTERNAL STRUCTURE. THEY DID PROVIDE ALTERNATIVE SITE PLANS, AND I'VE BEEN DOING THE VARIANCES FOR A LITTLE BIT AS IN PRIVATE HOMEOWNERS SUBMITTING ALTERNATE SITE PLANS SHOW THAT THEY ARE ACTUALLY TRYING TO PRESERVE THE TREE. THAT IS THE WHOLE IDEA BEHIND ALTERNATIVE SITE PLANS THAT YOU SHOW US THAT YOU'RE TRYING TO SEE HOW THIS TREE CAN BE PRESERVED. WITH THE SITE PLANS THAT THE APPLICANT PROVIDED, THEIR LEGAL REPRESENTATION SAID THAT THE SITE PLANS SHOW THAT THIS TREE CANNOT BE SAVED. WHAT THE APPLICANT DID PROVIDE TO US IS THE PROPOSED PLAN WITH A CUTOUT OF THE SECTION OF THE RADIUS OF THE TREE. UNFORTUNATELY, WE DON'T SEE A LOT OF CREATIVITY OR EVEN THE INTENT TO RECONFIGURE BECAUSE BASICALLY THE SAME THING WITHOUT ACTUALLY MASSAGING THIS PLAN. AND WE, AS STAFF, WE TAKE WHAT YOU GIVE TO US. WE CAN'T SAY WE WOULD REJECT IT BECAUSE THE ALTERNATIVE SITE PLANS ARE WHATEVER YOU CHOOSE THEN TO MAKE THEM. WE SEE A LOT OF PLANS WHERE THE APPLICANT IS REALLY NOT TRYING BECAUSE THEY FEEL THEY DON'T WANT TO PUT AN EFFORT TO SAVE THE TREE. BUT WE SEE THE SITE PLANS, PART OF WHAT THE CODE SAYS IS THAT THE ALTERNATIVE SITE PLAN SHOULD ACCOUNT FOR THE PROVISION OF THE CODE TO REDUCE YOUR SETBACKS TO SAVE A TREE. AND THAT IS IN SECTION 27-284, THE SECTION THAT THE BOARD HAS TO LOOK AT, IT SAYS IN C, WHETHER THE REDUCTION OF THE REQUIRED SETBACK FOR THE PARAMETER BUILDING OR STRUCTURE AS PROVIDED IN SECTION 27-156, ALLOW THE LOCATION OF THE BUILDING SUFFICIENT TO PRESERVE THE GRAND TREE REQUESTED FOR REMOVAL. THE ALTERNATIVE SITE PLANS THAT THE APPLICANT PROVIDED, THEY DID NOT ACCOUNT FOR THE SETBACK REDUCTIONS PROVIDED BY THE CODE. SO THEY ARE NOT LOOKING TO SEE HOW THIS COULD -- AND THEY COULD GET A LOT OF LATITUDE IF THEY DID TAKE CONCENTRATION OF THE SETBACK REDUCTIONS TO SAVE A GRAND TREE THAT THE CODE HAS PROVIDED. THE OTHER THING IS THAT -- AND THEY DID MENTION THE PROPOSAL BY NATURAL RESOURCES IN HIS PROPOSAL FOR HOW THEY COULD MASSAGE THE BUILDING. WHAT HE DID WAS BASED ON THE ALTERNATIVE SITE PLANS THAT THEY PROVIDED. THAT WAS NOT HIS PROPOSAL. SO WHEN THEY SAY THAT THE NATURAL RESOURCES PROPOSAL FAILED OR NOT BE VISIBLE, HE WAS JUST WORKING OFF OF WHAT THEY HAD ALREADY PROVIDED TO US AND SAYING, IF THIS IS WHAT YOU'RE LOOKING AT AS AN ALTERNATIVE, THERE ARE WAYS TO MAKE THIS WORK. DURING THE HEARING, I BELIEVE IT WAS -- I CAN BE CORRECTED IF I AM WRONG. I BELIEVE IT WAS THE BOARD MEMBER OONA JOHNSEN WHO POSED THE QUESTION TO THE APPLICANT TO SAY WOULD YOU BE WILLING TO CONSIDER ALTERNATIVE CONSTRUCTION METHODS IN ORDER TO SAVE THE TREE. HIS RESPONSE TO THAT WAS, NO, HE DECLINED. THAT IS ONE OF THE REQUIREMENTS IN THE SECTION THAT SAYS IN E, THE COST OF ANY ALTERNATIVE CONSTRUCTION METHODS OR REDUCTION IN THE USE OF THE PROPOSED STRUCTURE, HE WAS NOT WILLING TO EVEN CONSIDER ALTERNATIVE METHODS EVEN THOUGH WITH PIER AND LINTEL CONSTRUCTION YOU COULD ENCROACH INTO THAT PROTECTED RADIUS. SO THE LAST THING I'LL SAY REGARDING WHAT THIS IS ABOUT, ZONING SETBACKS AND THE BUILDABLE AREA TO SAY THAT WHAT THEY ARE PROPOSING IS WITHIN THE BUILDABLE AREA. CORRECT, YOU'RE WITHIN THE SETBACKS FOR YOUR ZONING. HOWEVER, WE DO HAVE A CODE THAT PROTECTS GRAND TREES. SO OUR ZONING SETBACK DOES NOT NEGATE THE FACT THAT WE HAVE A TREE THAT IS PROTECTED BY THE CODE. THE CODE DOESN'T CONTRADICT ITSELF. SO WE HAVE SETBACK REDUCTIONS IN ORDER TO SAVE THE GRAND TREE. SO, YES, YOU ARE WITHIN THE SETBACKS, BUT WE ALSO HAVE A CODE THAT WE HAVE TO RESPECT THAT PROTECTS THE GRAND TREES. SO WHEN YOU COME ON THE SITE OR YOU'RE COMING TO, YOU KNOW, CONSTRUCTION, WE CONSIDER HOW YOU ARE TREATING THAT SITE IN RESPECT TO THE CODE. SO, YES, YOU ARE WITHIN YOUR BUILDING ENVELOPE OF WHAT YOU COULD POTENTIALLY BUILD ON THAT SITE, BUT WE ARE GIVING YOU ALLOWANCES WITHIN THE CODE TO MOVE YOUR BUILDING IN ORDER TO SAVE THE GRAND TREE. AND THOSE ARE THE COMMENTS I JUST HAD IN RESPONSE TO STUFF THAT THE LEGAL REPRESENTATION FOR THE APPLICANT HAD SAID AND TO WHAT THEIR EXPERT WITNESS HAD SAID. IF YOU HAVE ANY QUESTIONS, SURE.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: HAVE YOU SEEN THIS REPORT BY HILL WARD HENDERSON THAT WAS PROVIDED TO COUNCIL?

segments.speaker: JAYNE NMADU
segments.text: NO.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: I CAN'T REALLY ASK YOU QUESTIONS. FROM WHAT I'M SEEING, I'LL ASK THE APPLICANT, I GUESS THEIR CHIEF COMPLAINT IS SAYING THAT OUR VARIANCE REVIEW BOARD ERRED ON THE TERMINOLOGY OR THE INTERPRETATION OF REASONABLE CONFIGURATION. BUT SINCE YOU HAVE NOT SEEN WHAT THEY BRIEFED, I'LL HAVE TO WAIT TO COME BACK AND ASK THE QUESTION, BUT YOU CAN COME BACK AFTER THAT SINCE YOU HAVE NOT READ WHAT THEY SUBMITTED.

segments.speaker: JAYNE NMADU
segments.text: I TRIED TO ADDRESS PART OF THE QUESTION OF REASONABLE CONFIGURATION IN SAYING THAT ONE OF THE THINGS OR PART OF WHAT THEY HAD HAD HIGHLIGHTED WAS THE FACT THAT IF THEY WERE TO, YOU KNOW, CHANGE THE LAYOUT OF WHAT THEY ARE PROPOSING, IT WOULD AFFECT THE INTERNAL FLOW OF THE BUILDING OR INTERNAL FLOW OF THE DESIGN. I'M SAYING THAT WHAT THAT PART OF THE CODE SAYS IS, ADVERSELY ALTER. SO IF YOU HAVE TO CHANGE THIS IN A WAY THAT YOU CANNOT FUNCTION WITHIN THE BUILDING OR WITHIN THAT DESIGN, YOU CANNOT GET FROM YOUR LIVING ROOM TO THE BEDROOM OR, YOU KNOW, IT AFFECTS THAT, THEN IT IS ADVERSELY ALTERING THE INTERNAL FLOW OF YOUR STRUCTURE. THAT WE DON'T SEE BY WHAT THE APPLICANT HAS PROVIDED TO US. THE OTHER THING THEY SAID, WE'RE NOT PROPOSING ANY CHANGES TO THE HEIGHT OF THE STRUCTURE OF WHAT THEY ARE PROPOSING. YES, THERE ARE LOW-HANGING BRANCHES, BUT NATURAL RESOURCE EXPERT CAN SPEAK TO THE FACT THAT YOU CAN HAVE TREE PRUNING AND ALL OF THAT THAT WOULD STILL PRESERVE THE TREE AND ALLOW THEM TO BUILD WHAT THEY ARE PROPOSING. BUT THOSE WERE PART OF THE THINGS THEY MENTIONED REGARDING REASONABLE RECONFIGURATION AND STEPHEN CAN ANSWER ANY QUESTIONS REGARDING THE TECHNICAL ASPECTS OF PRUNING THAT TREE OR -- I CAN COME BACK UP TO ADDRESS THAT.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: ALL RIGHT. IF THERE ARE NO OTHER QUESTIONS AT THIS TIME, WE HAVE REBUTTAL FROM THE APPLICANT. FIVE MINUTES FOR REBUTTAL. YES, MA'AM.

segments.speaker: JAIME MAIER
segments.text: I WANT TO ADDRESS A COUPLE OF THINGS FIRST THAT WERE SAID BY STAFF. THE APPLICANT DID NOT SAY THAT -- ACTUALLY, I THINK I'LL HAVE THE APPLICANT HIMSELF SPEAK TO THE ALTERNATIVE CONSTRUCTION. I WANT TO CLARIFY ACTUALLY A FEW THINGS THAT WERE SAID BY EVERYBODY. MOBLEY BUILDS A LOT OF HOMES IN THE AREA. HE FREQUENTLY BUILDS THEM AROUND GRAND TREES. HE DOES THAT. THAT IS A FACT. AND ALSO, HE IS A BUILDER. THERE ARE CONTRACT PURCHASERS THAT COULD BUY THIS HOME. THEY LIVE JUST DOWN THE STREET. THEY WANT A BIGGER HOME. SO FOR WHAT THAT'S WORTH, THERE IS A FACE -- THERE IS A LIVING HUMAN BEING BUYING THIS WHO LIVES ON THE STREET WANTING TO BUY THE HOME FOR WHATEVER THAT'S WORTH. AND I THINK IT IS WORTH SOMETHING. I'M GOING TO HAVE MR. PETERIKA REITERATE HOW THE CODE APPLIES HERE, BUT WHAT STAFF IS SAYING IS, AGAIN, NOT APPLYING THE REASONABLE RECONFIGURATION ANALYSIS WITH CODE. THESE GRAND TREE REMOVAL CASES GET APPROVED A LOT SINCE 2019. YOU DON'T SEE THEM BECAUSE THEY TYPICALLY ARE APPLIED CORRECTLY. THERE'S TYPICALLY NOT AS MUCH PUBLIC COMMENT, FRANKLY, AS THERE WAS AT THIS ONE BECAUSE IT IS EASIER, FRANKLY, TO REMOVE THE GRAND TREES NOW UNDER THIS CODE BECAUSE IT IS OBJECTIVE. THAT WAS THE WHOLE PURPOSE. AND SAYING THE TRZ IS WHAT DICTATES EVERYTHING, NO. THE TRZ IS A WAY TO REMOVE A TREE WITHOUT HAVING TO GET A VARIANCE BASED ON A DEFINED AREA OF THE LOT. AND THEN THE CODE SAYS, IF THE TREE, WHICH THIS TREE IS EIGHT INCHES OUTSIDE OF THAT, IF THE TREE IS OUTSIDE OF THE TREE REMOVAL ZONE, OKAY, THEN YOU HAVE TO GET A VARIANCE. AND HERE ARE THE CRITERIA THAT YOU APPLY TO GET THAT. BASICALLY SAYING, WELL, BECAUSE YOU HAVE TO ASK FOR A VARIANCE IN ORDER TO REMOVE THIS TREE, THAT'S WHY IT SHOULD BE DENIED. WHY WOULD WE HAVE THAT AT ALL? WHY WOULD THAT PROCESS EXIST? THE PROCESS IS THERE IF IT CANNOT BE REMOVED BECAUSE OF THE TRZ. MR. PETERIKA, IF WE CAN CLARIFY, AGAIN, THE REASONABLE RECONFIGURATION ANALYSIS, CAN YOU JUST READ THE CRITERIA OR AT LEAST SUMMARIZE THEM. I KNOW THE CRITERIA ARE IN FRONT OF YOU.

segments.speaker: RICHARD PETERIKA
segments.text: AS APPLICABLE TO THIS LOT AS MANY LOTS, THE REASONABLE RECONFIGURATION CRITERIA ASKS SOMEONE TO TAKE THEIR PROPOSED FOOTPRINT, EITHER, A, SHIFT IT TO THE DESIGN EXCEPTION SETBACK, 10% TO THE FRONT OR THE BACK. THAT WOULD BE TWO FEET. 25% TO THE SIDE, SEVEN FOOT SIDE SETBACKS, MAYBE THERE IS A CORNER SETBACK THAT APPLIES. BARELY A FOOT AND A HALF. THE TREE IS CURRENTLY LOCATED ABOUT SEVEN FEET FROM THE PROPERTY LINE AT THE SIDE SETBACK. IT'S IN DIRECT CONFLICT WITH THE PROPOSED BUILDING WALL. THE TREE TRUNK IS IN CONFLICT WITH THE BUILDING WALL, NOT -- THE BRANCHES MAY ALSO BE IN CONFLICT, BUT IF WE'RE CUTTING OFF AN ARM AT YOUR CHEST, THE ARM IS LESS THE POINT. THERE'S NO CHANGE IN STORIES. I DON'T THINK THAT'S REALLY AN ISSUE, EXCEPT THAT BECAUSE OF THE EXISTING STRUCTURE THAT WAS THERE, THE CANOPY HAD ALREADY BEEN ELEVATED ON THAT SIDE. THAT'S NOT REALLY THE POINT BECAUSE THE TREE IS LOCATED IN THE BUILDING FOOTPRINT. AND THE BUILDING CAN'T BE SHIFTED TO THE DESIGN EXCEPTION. THE HEIGHT OF THE STRUCTURE IS NOT RELEVANT TO THIS PROPOSAL, EXCEPT IF IT WAS REDUCED, IT WOULDN'T ACCOMMODATE THE TREE ANY BETTER. AND THE THIRD PORTION OF THIS SECTION, THE INTERNAL FLOW AND FUNCTION, IS NOT ADVERSELY ALTERED. MS. NMADU IS RIGHT IN STRESSING THAT POINT. IT IS NOT ADVERSELY ALTERED. WE'RE STATING IT IS A 30% REDUCTION IN THE HOUSE THAT WAS PROPOSED. ROOMS AND CIRCULATION ARE LOST BY PRESERVING THE ROOT SYSTEM PROVIDED. AND IN MY OPINION THAT IS AN ADVERSE ALTERATION TO THE INTERNAL FLOW AND FUNCTION OF THE PROPOSAL. THAT'S ALL WE HAVE. THAT'S ALL THE CODE SAYS IN THE SECTION, 27-2,842.5, FOOTNOTE 5, II 123. THAT'S ALL WE CAN WORK FROM. SO I APPRECIATE YOU OFFERING THE OPPORTUNITY TO KIND OF MAKE THAT POINT.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH. COUNCILMAN MIRANDA.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: THANK YOU, MR. CHAIRMAN. MR. PETERIKA, YOU ARE VERY COMPETENT IN YOUR KNOWLEDGE OF TREES AND EVERYTHING ELSE. I'VE TAKEN ALWAYS YOUR TESTIMONY AS THE EVIDENCE OF A PROFESSIONAL, WHICH YOU ARE. REPRESENTED BY ONE OF THE HIGHEST ATTORNEY FIRMS, HILL WARD HENDERSON. I'VE SEEN MANY MOBLEY HOMES BUILT AND THEY ARE VERY, VERY GOOD. HOWEVER, AFTER LISTENING TO ALL THE TESTIMONY AND LISTENING TO EVERYTHING I HEARD, NO ONE HAS MENTIONED THAT IT MIGHT BE A HARDSHIP OF A HOUSE, BUT IT WAS A HARDSHIP BEFORE THE HOUSE WAS DESIGNED. YOU KNOW WHY? BECAUSE IT IS A 50-FOOT LOT. IF IT WAS A 60-FOOT LOT, WE WOULDN'T BE HERE. AM I RIGHT?

segments.speaker: RICHARD PETERIKA
segments.text: YES, THERE WOULD BE SUFFICIENT SPACE ON THE LOT.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: I'M PRETTY LIBERAL ON A LOT OF THINGS, BUT WHEN WE COME UP AND SAY A HARDSHIP, IT IS. WHO CREATED THE HARDSHIP?

segments.speaker: RICHARD PETERIKA
segments.text: I DON'T KNOW IF I'M PREPARED TO ANSWER THAT.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: WHO CREATED THE HARDSHIP? I'M A CRIMINOLOGIST. WHO CREATED THE HARDSHIP?

segments.speaker: RICHARD PETERIKA
segments.text: WAS IT A LOT OF RECORD?

segments.speaker: RICHARD PETERIKA
segments.text: WHEN WAS IT PLATTED? 1908. THE DEVELOPER THEN SET THIS UP.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: THANK YOU, SIR.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH. ALL RIGHT. THAT CONCLUDES REBUTTAL. AT THIS TIME, IS THERE A MOTION, IF THERE ARE NO OTHER QUESTIONS, TO CLOSE THE PUBLIC HEARING.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I'LL MAKE A MOTION TO CLOSE THE PUBLIC HEARING.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: WE HAVE A MOTION BY COUNCIL MEMBER HURTAK. SECONDED BY COUNCIL MEMBER MIRANDA. ALL IN FAVOR? WHAT IS THE PLEASURE OF COUNCIL? WE HAVE THREE POSSIBLE ACTIONS, AFFIRM, REMAND, OR OVERTURN. DOES ANYBODY WISH TO MAKE A MOTION AT THIS TIME?

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: I MOVE TO AFFIRM THE VRB'S DECISION TO DENY THE VARIANCE REQUESTED IN VRB-22-70 FOR THE PROPERTY LOCATED AT 3122 WEST SAN JOSE STREET BECAUSE THE PETITION DOES NOT MEET THE CRITERIA IN SECTION 27-284.2.5 F FOR TO REMOVE THE GRAND TREE.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: WE HAVE A MOTION FROM COUNCIL MEMBER CARLSON. WE HAVE A SECOND FROM COUNCILMAN MIRANDA. ROLL CALL VOTE, PLEASE.

segments.speaker: ORLANDO GUDES
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: LUIS VIERA
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: CHARLIE MIRANDA
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: YES.

segments.speaker: THE CLERK
segments.text: THE MOTION CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY. [ APPLAUSE ] MOTION CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY WITH CITRO BEING ABSENT AT VOTE.

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: THANK YOU VERY MUCH. THAT CONCLUDES OUR AGENDA. MR. SHELBY, SINCE WE START AGAIN AT 5:01 P.M., IS THERE A MOTION TO RECEIVE AND FILE OR JUST WE'RE IN RECESS? I'LL LEAVE NEW BUSINESS UNTIL TONIGHT. CAN I GET A MOTION TO RECEIVE AND FILE?

segments.speaker: GUIDO MANISCALCO
segments.text: MOTION FROM COUNCILMAN GUDES. SECOND FROM COUNCILMAN MIRANDA. ALL IN FAVOR? AYE. ALL RIGHT. WE ARE IN RECESS -- WELL, THE MEETING IS 5:01. IT MAY BE A FEW MINUTES LATER. 5:01. THANK YOU. [ SOUNDING GAVEL ] DISCLAIMER: THIS FILE REPRESENTS AN UNEDITED VERSION OF REALTIME CAPTIONING WHICH SHOULD NEITHER BE RELIED UPON FOR COMPLETE ACCURACY NOR USED AS A VERBATIM TRANSCRIPT.
